guangzhou.name: Guangzhou
guangzhou.tagline: Eat Your Way Through Old Canton
guangzhou.best_time: October–December. Avoid Golden Week (Oct 1–7) and Chinese New Year.

attractions.name: Canton Tower
attractions.tip: Go at sunset — the light show starts after dark. Take the Bubble Tram (highest Ferris wheel in the world) for unique shots. Pair immediately with Pearl River cruise. Book timed entry online.
attractions.elderly_note: Elevator access throughout. No steps required.
attractions.description: China's tallest TV tower at 600 metres, its waist-nipped form inspired by an hourglass — the observation deck gives the best aerial view of the Pearl River Delta's extraordinary urban sprawl. The bubble tram circling the outside at 455 metres offers the most terrifying and exhilarating perspective.

attractions.name: Shamian Island
attractions.tip: 150+ European colonial buildings on a 0.3km² island. The mint-green Starbucks at No.54 Shamian Street is a former British consulate — unique photo spot. Go before 8am for golden light through banyan trees. Combine with Qingping Market nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat, shaded streets. Ideal for elderly.
attractions.description: A perfectly preserved colonial island where French and British consulate buildings from the 1860s stand under enormous banyan trees in complete peace — no cars, no crowds, just old stone buildings with green shutters and locals playing chess in the shade. One of the most atmospheric places in China.

attractions.name: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall
attractions.tip: Guangdong's crown jewel of Lingnan architecture — triple roof sculptures, wood carvings, brick reliefs so intricate poet Guo Moruo wrote: 'Man can replace heaven's work, heaven cannot replicate man's.' 2025 update: AR technology recreates Qing dynasty ceremonies inside. Go morning.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat with some steps. Worth the effort.
attractions.description: The most ornate building in Guangdong — every surface of this 1894 clan temple is covered in extraordinary craftsmanship: ridge sculptures, wood carvings, iron castings, stone engravings and ceramic mosaics depicting 10,000 figures from Cantonese folklore. Nothing else in China looks like this.

attractions.name: Yongqingfang & Enning Road
attractions.tip: The full Yongqingfang Tourism Zone includes Lychee Bay waterway, Cantonese Opera Art Museum, and Bruce Lee's ancestral home. The Guangcai ceramic painting workshop lets you paint porcelain for ¥98/hr. Monthly 2nd Saturday: spontaneous Cantonese Opera flash performance at Jinshen Plaza. Bridge light reflection at night looks like a crescent moon — come back after dark.
attractions.elderly_note: Some cobblestone paths. Rest spots available.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's hippest neighbourhood — a restored Lingnan architecture district where 1920s shophouses now contain craft beer bars, specialty coffee shops and Cantonese fusion restaurants. The contrast of colonial-era architecture with young creative energy is exactly what Guangzhou does better than anywhere.

attractions.name: Dongshankou
attractions.tip: Called Guangzhou's Seoul Seongsu district — trendy Chinese fashion brands with unique aesthetics, indie roastery cafes, streetwear stores. 翠叶道 (Cuiye Road) nearby is a quiet stretch of cafes perfect for a break. Weekday afternoons best.
attractions.elderly_note: Moderate walking on flat streets.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most romantic neighbourhood — Art Deco villas and Republican-era apartment buildings line tree-shaded streets where independent coffee shops and bookstores have moved into ground floors. The combination of 1930s Shanghai-style architecture and Guangdong's tropical vegetation is uniquely beautiful.

attractions.name: Beijing Road Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: 2025 upgrade: The '千年城脉博物馆' on B2 level beneath Guangbai Department Store reveals 11 layers of road surfaces from Qin dynasty to Republic era under glass. Walk in, look down — you're walking on history. Blind box toy shops everywhere. Evening buzz is electric.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Easy access.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's oldest commercial street where modern shopping meets 2,000 years of layered history — glass panels in the pavement reveal Han, Tang, Song, Ming and Qing dynasty street surfaces stacked beneath your feet. The nightly LED screen display above is spectacular.
attractions.photo_attribution: Beijing Road Pedestrian Street Comprehensive Management Office

attractions.name: Baoye Road Night Supper Street
attractions.tip: GZ's late-night supper (夜宵) scene. Grilled seafood, BBQ skewers, claypot congee. Comes alive after 10pm — eat like the locals eat at midnight. Order Chaoshan fish congee at Yong Zuo — thick, savory, absolutely local.
attractions.elderly_note: Noisy and crowded after 10pm. Consider earlier dinner alternatives.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most authentic late-night eating street — dozens of seafood restaurants and Cantonese supper stalls that don't hit their stride until midnight. The house-speciality steamed fish, claypot rice and wonton noodles are extraordinary at 2am when the whole city seems to be eating here.

attractions.name: Baiyun Mountain
attractions.tip: 2025 new: Star observation deck added at Moxing Peak summit — astronomy talks every Friday night. Cable car up, hike down for best experience. Go weekday morning for quiet. White Cloud Mountain: the 'First Mountain in the South.'
attractions.elderly_note: Cable car available. Not suitable for those with mobility issues on hike route.
attractions.description: The 'lungs of Guangzhou' — a forested mountain rising to 382m at the city's northern edge, covered in walking trails, waterfalls and scenic viewpoints. The cable car to the summit at dawn gives extraordinary views of Guangzhou's urban sprawl emerging from morning mist.

attractions.name: Pearl River Night Cruise
attractions.tip: Pro tip: Skip the tourist cruise and take the 水上巴士 (water bus line) for just ¥2 — same river views, completely local experience. Jiangwan Ferry Pier has the best departure time at sunset. Depart from Tianzi Wharf for classic tourist version.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible on cruise boats.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's skyline transforms at night — Canton Tower's LED display changes colours every few seconds, illuminated bridges reflect in the river and the city lights stretch to every horizon. The 1-hour cruise is the best way to see the dramatic scale of China's third city.
attractions.photo_attribution: Pearl River Night Cruise Ticket Office

attractions.name: Yuexiu Park
attractions.tip: Largest urban park in downtown GZ. Five Rams Statue is Guangzhou's city symbol. Zhenhai Tower (600 years old) gives a surprisingly good city overview. Go 6–8am — locals do tai chi, ballroom dancing, singing. The real Guangzhou morning ritual.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully flat park. Perfect for elderly morning walks.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's oldest and largest public park containing the iconic Five Rams Sculpture — the mythological goats who brought rice to the city in ancient times. The Ming dynasty Zhenhai Tower at the park's centre houses the excellent Guangzhou Museum of history.

attractions.name: Museum of the Nanyue King
attractions.tip: Built directly over a 2,200-year-old royal tomb. Walk down into actual underground burial chambers — a jade burial suit with 2,291 pieces sewn in gold wire. One of China's top 100 archaeological discoveries. Nationally ranked first-class museum. Book via WeChat. Closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Ramp access to underground chambers. Suitable with assistance.
attractions.description: An extraordinary discovery beneath a construction site — the 2,000-year-old tomb of the second king of the Nanyue Kingdom, buried with 1,000 jade burial suits, bronze vessels and ivory. The tomb was found intact and the museum built directly over it.

attractions.name: Sacred Heart Cathedral
attractions.tip: One of only 4 all-stone Gothic cathedrals in the world. 2025: Candlelit concerts every Wednesday and Saturday evening at 7pm. New craft market on the east side of the cathedral plaza sells hand-painted glass window fridge magnets — great souvenir.
attractions.elderly_note: Steps at entrance. Interior flat.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's Gothic masterpiece — one of only two all-stone Gothic cathedrals in Asia, built by French missionaries in 1888 with stone shipped from France. The twin 58-metre spires are visible across the Pearl River and the interior stained glass illuminates the nave with colour.

attractions.name: Shangxiajiu Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: China's first commercial pedestrian street. Lined with 骑楼 (arcade-style colonial buildings). Best for food — century-old restaurants Guangzhou Restaurant (1935, '食在广州第一家') and Taotaoju on this strip. Also Lianxiang Lou for lotus paste cakes since 1889.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat street. Covered arcades provide shade.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most atmospheric commercial street — 1.2km of Lingnan shophouse architecture from the Republican era, lined with Cantonese snack shops, herbal tea stalls and century-old businesses. The evening crowds are among the densest and most energetic in China.

attractions.name: Redtory Art & Design Factory
attractions.tip: Former canned food factory turned contemporary art campus. Galleries, studios, events, bookshops, coffee. 2025 address: 128 Yuancun 4th Cross Road. Less touristy than OCT Loft in Shenzhen — this one feels genuinely underground.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some uneven surfaces between buildings.
attractions.description: A Soviet-era tomato paste factory converted into Guangzhou's most creative district — red brick warehouses housing galleries, design studios, wedding photography boutiques and the city's most interesting restaurants. The rust-coloured industrial aesthetic against lush tropical greenery is distinctive.

attractions.name: Huaisheng Mosque (Lighthouse Mosque)
attractions.tip: Built in 627 AD during the Tang Dynasty — China's oldest mosque, older than Islam's arrival in most of China. The 36m minaret once guided ships up the Pearl River as a literal lighthouse. Non-Muslims cannot enter prayer hall but can view courtyard and lighthouse tower. Dress modestly; scarves provided.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat courtyard. Suitable.
attractions.description: One of the oldest mosques in the world — built in 627AD by the Prophet Muhammad's uncle on his mission to China, its lighthouse minaret has guided ships into Guangzhou's port for 1,400 years. The mosque remains an active place of worship for Guangzhou's Muslim community.

attractions.name: Ersha Island & Guangdong Museum of Art
attractions.tip: A quiet island in the Pearl River favored by locals. The 2025 new outdoor camping base lets you do picnics with Cantonese dim sum baskets. Good sunset views of Canton Tower from the western shore. Guangdong Museum of Art is free and genuinely excellent.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat island. Easy cycling available.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most elegant island — a residential enclave of European-style villas and tree-lined boulevards home to the Guangdong Museum of Art, one of China's finest art institutions. The combination of green lawns, contemporary art and river views makes this the most peaceful spot in the city.

attractions.name: Hope & Sesame Speakeasy Bar
attractions.tip: Asia's 50 Best Bars winner since 2019. Hidden entrance inside a traditional Cantonese sundry shop — you have to find the secret door. Jazz every night. Open 6pm–2am daily. Book early on weekends — fills up completely. One of Guangzhou's most unique experiences.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable — bar environment, late hours.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most acclaimed cocktail bar — a Prohibition-era speakeasy concept hidden behind a vintage telephone booth entrance, serving extraordinary cocktails that use traditional Chinese ingredients. Consistently ranked among Asia's top 50 bars.

attractions.name: Chimelong Tourist Resort
attractions.tip: Multiple parks: Safari World (world's largest, home to panda triplets 萌萌帅帅酷酷), International Circus (world's largest performance stage), Water Park (opens April 26 2025), Happy World. Kids and families: full day minimum. Featured in '爸爸去哪儿' TV show — enormous brand recognition in China.
attractions.elderly_note: Tram transport within resort. Suitable for elderly with mobility.
attractions.description: China's most visited theme park complex — Chimelong Safari Park houses the world's only panda triplets and Chimelong Paradise has consistently ranked as Asia's top theme park. The nighttime water show at Chimelong Water Park is the most spectacular in Asia.

attractions.name: Liwan Lake Park
attractions.tip: One kilometre from Yongqingfang — quieter and more local. Former site of a Qing dynasty private garden. Morning: elderly locals singing, playing instruments, practicing calligraphy by the lake. Very peaceful contrast to the busy tourist areas nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat park. Perfect for elderly.
attractions.description: A classical Lingnan garden centred on a lake — the willow trees, lotus ponds and traditional pavilions create a peaceful counterpoint to the surrounding urban density. The park's teahouse serves Cantonese morning tea to local families every day from 7am.

attractions.name: Guangzhou Opera House
attractions.tip: Designed by Zaha Hadid — exterior looks like a pebble. Free to photograph outside. Best viewed from across Huacheng Square at night when lit. Pair with Canton Tower visit — both are in Zhujiang New Town. 2025: outdoor camping base opened at Ersha Island nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Exterior only. Flat surroundings.
attractions.description: Zaha Hadid's most celebrated building in China — twin 'pebbles' of glass and granite that seem to have been deposited by a flood on the Pearl River bank. The interior is equally extraordinary: a 1,800-seat concert hall of such acoustic perfection that performers request it by name.

attractions.name: Guangxiao Temple
attractions.tip: One of Guangdong's oldest Buddhist temples — where the 6th patriarch of Zen Buddhism, Huineng, trained in 7th century. A pilgrimage site for Zen Buddhists worldwide. Much less touristy than Chen Clan but equally significant. Vegetarian restaurant inside: ¥10 meal admission if you eat there.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat temple grounds.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's oldest temple — founded in the 4th century on land where the great Buddhist monk Huineng once lived, the patriarch of Chan (Zen) Buddhism. The ancient iron pagodas in the courtyard are the oldest cast-iron structures in China.

attractions.name: Thirteen Hongs Museum
attractions.tip: The story of how Guangzhou was the ONLY port China opened to the West for 80+ years — and how the Thirteen Hongs merchant consortium became some of the world's wealthiest people. Surprisingly engaging for history lovers. Excellent English signage.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat museum. Suitable.
attractions.description: The story of Canton's extraordinary role as China's only legal trading port for 150 years — the museum recreates the Thirteen Factories district where British, American, Dutch and French merchants traded silk, tea and porcelain with the world. An essential chapter of global commercial history.
attractions.photo_query: thirteen hongs museum guangzhou trade history

attractions.name: Huacheng Square (Flower City Square)
attractions.tip: Guangzhou's modern heart. '珠江之眼' (Eye of the Pearl River) observation deck lights up every night at 7:30pm. Fountain shows, art installations, families flying kites. Pairs well with Canton Tower and Opera House — all within walking distance.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat plaza. Ideal for elderly.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's centrepiece of modern civic design — a vast public plaza surrounded by the city's most dramatic contemporary architecture, including Zaha Hadid's Opera House, the Guangdong Museum and the Canton Tower visible on the horizon. Best visited at night when the surrounding buildings illuminate.

attractions.name: Sanjie Temple Area & Xiguan Old Houses
attractions.tip: West of Yongqingfang: the old Xiguan neighborhoods have traditional West Guangzhou mansion architecture (西关大屋) — screen doors with 满洲窗 (Manchurian stained glass), grand courtyards. Walk between Changshou West Road and Enning Road for the best examples. Almost no tourists.
attractions.elderly_note: Some narrow alleys. Mostly flat.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Guangzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Xigu Cannon Fort Anti-British Relic Site

attractions.name: Panxi Restaurant Garden Teahouse
attractions.tip: Founded in 1947 — one of Guangzhou's four famous garden teahouses. Lingnan garden architecture with pavilions, koi ponds, moon gates. Order traditional dim sum in a setting that feels like the 1960s Guangzhou high life. Weekend mornings fill fast — book ahead.
attractions.elderly_note: Garden paths with some steps. Mostly accessible.
attractions.description: Guangzhou's most beautiful restaurant — a 1950s Lingnan garden with lotus ponds, rockery and covered walkways housing the city's most celebrated dim sum. Weekend morning dim sum here is the most authentic yum cha experience in China, with pushcart service starting at 7:30am.
attractions.photo_query: panxi teahouse guangzhou garden dim sum

attractions.name: Canton Tower Wharf
attractions.tip: Evening cruises departing between 7-8pm offer the best views when Canton Tower's LED light show is most spectacular. Buy tickets from official booths at the wharf to avoid markup from street vendors.
attractions.description: This bustling embarkation point along the Pearl River sits directly beneath the iconic Canton Tower, offering prime riverside access. Board a river cruise here to witness Guangzhou's illuminated skyline from the water while enjoying optimal tower views.

attractions.name: Pearl River Night Cruise Ticket Office
attractions.tip: Book tickets directly at the office 30-60 minutes before sunset departures for better availability and rates compared to online resellers. Weekend and holiday cruises fill up quickly.
attractions.description: The ticket office serves as the gateway to Guangzhou's most enchanting evening experience—a cruise along the illuminated Pearl River. Glide past glowing bridges and the city's modern skyline reflected in dark waters, capturing the essence of contemporary Guangzhou after sunset.

attractions.name: Party Pier Plaza
attractions.tip: Visit during weekends around 7-8pm when street musicians and performers gather near the waterfront. The plaza provides better Pearl River cruise boarding access than the crowded tourist terminals nearby.
attractions.description: This waterfront plaza along the Pearl River transforms into a lively gathering spot as evening falls, with locals gathering for riverside strolls and street performances. The open plaza offers unobstructed river views and connects seamlessly to the Pearl River cruise terminals.

attractions.name: Chimelong Paradise
attractions.tip: Arrive at opening time and head straight to the Ten Inversion Coaster and Dive Coaster first—queues triple by midday. Download the Chimelong app beforehand to check real-time wait times and show schedules.
attractions.description: This massive amusement park anchors southern China's thrill-ride scene with world-class roller coasters, including the record-breaking Ten Inversion Coaster. Expect international-standard attractions spanning gentle family rides to extreme white-knuckle experiences across themed zones.

attractions.name: Yuntai Garden
attractions.tip: Enter through the west gate early morning to avoid crowds and catch soft light on the fountains. The upper terraces offer the best panoramic views for photos without other visitors in frame.
attractions.description: Terraced flower gardens cascade down Baiyun Mountain's foothills, connected by winding paths and elegant tiered fountains. Visit during spring or autumn to witness rotating seasonal flower displays that transform the entire hillside into a sea of color.

attractions.name: Moxing Summit
attractions.tip: Start your hike before 6am to catch the sunrise and avoid crowds. Bring a light jacket even in summer as temperatures drop noticeably at the peak.
attractions.description: The highest peak in Guangzhou's Baiyun Mountain range towers at 382 meters, offering panoramic views of the sprawling city below. Reach the summit for breathtaking sunrise vistas and refreshing mountain air above the urban bustle.

attractions.name: Baiyun Mountain Cableway
attractions.tip: Buy tickets for the cableway at the base station early morning on weekends to avoid hour-long queues. Sit on the right side going up for the best city views across the Pearl River Delta.
attractions.description: This scenic cable car glides smoothly up Baiyun Mountain, offering panoramic views of Guangzhou's sprawling cityscape framed by lush peaks. The effortless ride transforms a steep hike into a relaxing ascent, perfect for reaching the summit viewpoints without breaking a sweat.

attractions.name: Chimelong Safari Park
attractions.chinese: 广州长隆野生动物世界
attractions.tip: Arrive right at opening to catch feeding times for koalas and pandas when animals are most active. Drive-through safari tickets sell out fast on weekends, so book at least three days ahead online.
attractions.description: One of China's largest safari parks sprawls across 133 hectares in Panyu District, where visitors drive through open habitats housing over 500 species including rare white tigers and giant pandas. The self-drive safari experience and close-up animal encounters make this a world-class wildlife destination.

attractions.name: Chimelong Water Park
attractions.tip: Arrive right at opening to claim prime cabana spots near the wave pool, then hit the major slides before lunch when crowds peak. Lockers and tube rentals are near each zone to avoid backtracking.
attractions.description: One of Asia's largest water parks spreads across 450,000 square meters with towering slides, wave pools, and lazy rivers set against tropical landscaping. The park's record-breaking attractions and nightly entertainment shows make it a full-day aquatic adventure.

attractions.name: Chimelong International Circus Ticket Office
attractions.chinese: 长隆国际大马戏售票处
attractions.tip: Book tickets online at least a day in advance for better seat selection and discounts up to 20%. Avoid visiting the ticket office during 6-7pm when tour groups arrive, causing long queues.
attractions.description: The gateway to one of Asia's most spectacular circus performances, featuring acrobats, aerialists, and animal acts in a state-of-the-art theater. This is where you secure tickets to Chimelong's flagship evening entertainment extravaganza that rivals Las Vegas productions.

food.name: Dim Sum (Early Morning Tea)
food.where: Dim Dou Dak 点都德 (all-day including dinner, multiple locations); Taotaoju 陶陶居 (Shangxiajiu, est. 1880); Guangzhou Restaurant 广州酒家 (est. 1935, '食在广州第一家')
food.tip: Order: hongchangfen (脆皮肠粉 — rice noodle roll with crispy pastry inside shrimp), har gow (虾饺), cheong fun (猪肠粉), egg tarts. At Taotaoju: get the liu sha bao (流沙包, salted egg custard bun) — they invented it here. Check Dianping for ¥80/¥100 vouchers.

food.name: Crab Roe Noodles
food.where: Xie San Bao 蟹叁宝 — multiple locations
food.tip: Blew up on 小红书 and Lemon8 SG. Rich orange crab roe sauce coating fresh noodles. Distinctly seasonal flavor. Arrive 15 mins before opening to beat queues.

food.name: Char Siu BBQ Pork & Roast Meats
food.where: Any 烧腊 shop — look for hanging roast meat in the window. No specific chain — local shops are always better.
food.tip: The caramelised char siu glaze should have slightly charred edges — that's quality. Order with white rice (白饭). Get the combination platter: char siu + roast pork (烧肉) + roast duck (烧鸭). Under ¥40 for a full meal.

food.name: Claypot Rice
food.where: Wan Xing Claypot 万兴煲仔饭 — opens 5:30pm only. One chef, 15 stoves, queues before opening.
food.tip: Order frog or Chinese sausage & lap cheong version. The crispy rice crust (锅巴) at the bottom is the prized part — scrape and eat it. This is why locals queue.

food.name: Double Boiled Milk Pudding
food.where: Guangzhou Dessert institution on Baohua Road (宝华路); Shawan Sweet Shop 沙湾甜品食馆 on Xihua Road
food.tip: Made with buffalo milk — non-negotiable GZ order. Also try: 姜撞奶 (ginger milk pudding — poured hot milk into ginger juice to set), 凤凰炒奶 (pan-fried milk — unique texture), 芝麻糊 (hand-ground sesame paste — get the one with the 40-year old stone mill).

food.name: Chen Tianji Fish Skin & Pig Intestine Rice Noodle
food.where: 陈添记 — Baohua Road No.59, Liwan District (宝华路59号侧十五甫三巷). Est. 1937. Only sells 3 items.
food.tip: Only sells three things: chilled dace fish skin (爽鱼皮) with sesame & peanuts, pig intestine rice noodle (猪肠粉), and boat congee (艇仔粥). The fish skin is their signature — cold, bouncy, dressed in soy sauce and sesame. A local institution for 87 years.

food.name: Wonton Noodles
food.where: Baohua Noodle Shop 宝华面店 on Baohua Road; Hanming Noodles nearby
food.tip: GZ wonton noodles are different from HK — thinner 竹升面 (bamboo-pressed) noodles, smaller wontons. Baohua's broth uses roast pork bones, dried flounder, dried scallops, and fresh shrimp — the traditional 上汤 method. Can't get this anywhere else.

food.name: Boat Congee
food.where: Xihua Road food stalls; Pantang area near Liwan Lake; Panxi Restaurant
food.tip: Named after Pearl River boat dwellers who sold congee from sampans. Silky GZ-style congee base with fish slices, squid, peanuts, char siu, fried dough. Very different from congee elsewhere. Perfect breakfast dish.

food.name: Jasmine Milk Tea with Cheese Cream
food.where: Chali Tea 茶里 — Guangzhou-origin brand. Multiple locations.
food.tip: Blew up on 小红书 and Lemon8 among SEA travellers. Cheese cream layer on jasmine tea. Also try 阿嬷手作 (Grandma Handmade) for their rice wine fermented milk tea — completely unique to the PRD region.

food.name: Roasted Goose
food.where: Bingsheng Pinwei 炳胜品味 (Tianhe area); or any established 烧腊 restaurant displaying 烧鹅
food.tip: Guangzhou roast goose is the original — crispier skin than Peking duck, deeper flavour. Order half goose for 2–3 people. Dip in the plum sauce. This is what Cantonese food writers rank as the dish that best represents the region.

food.name: Zhu Ji Jiu (Pig's Trotter in Ginger & Vinegar)
food.where: Xihua Road stalls; Liwan district sweet shops
food.tip: A Cantonese postpartum tradition — sweet black vinegar braised pork trotter with ginger and boiled eggs. Sounds unusual but tastes incredible. Rich, tangy, warming. Served year-round in Guangzhou. Order with a bowl of congee.

food.name: Jidi Congee (Offal Congee)
food.where: Wuzhanji 伍湛记 — Longdong East Road & Longdong Middle Road, Liwan
food.tip: Pork balls, pork liver, pork intestine in silky congee — the 'three grades' of imperial exam (三元及第). Wuzhanji has been making this since the 1950s. Won China's National Famous Snack award in 1997. Find the shops by the queue.

food.name: White Cut Chicken
food.where: Any Cantonese restaurant — or Bingsheng Pinwei for premium version
food.tip: GZ's most famous dish to outsiders — gently poached free-range chicken, just-cooked through, served cold with ginger scallion oil. The secret is the chicken quality and the dipping sauce. Locals say: if the restaurant's white cut chicken is good, everything is good.

food.name: Taro Milk Tea (大芋头专线)
food.where: Trending at various milk tea shops in Tianhe and Dongshankou areas
food.tip: Chunky taro paste milk tea with real taro pieces — not the purple powder version. GZ's taro milk tea scene is much more artisanal than Singapore's. Look for handwritten menus and long local queues.

food.name: Coconut Ice Cream
food.where: Shunji Ice Room 顺记冰室 — Baohua Road No.85. Est. 1920s, one of Guangzhou's last four original ice parlors.
food.tip: Hand-made coconut ice cream using the original 1920s recipe. Very fresh, not over-sweet. The mango gelato is also excellent. A childhood memory for all Guangzhou locals over 40.

food.name: % Arabica Guangzhou
food.must_order: Iced latte, pour-over single origin
food.tip: The Japanese minimalist coffee brand beloved across Asia. The Guangzhou location has a striking all-white interior that photographs beautifully. Always a queue on weekends. The coffee quality is excellent — not just for photos.

food.name: Lianxiang Lou (莲香楼) Dim Sum
food.must_order: Char siu bao (BBQ pork bun), egg tart, turnip cake
food.tip: One of Guangzhou's oldest and most celebrated dim sum restaurants — over 100 years old. The breakfast dim sum (6–11am) is the quintessential old Canton experience. Noisy, steamy, joyful chaos. Push carts come to your table. Arrive before 8am or expect a long wait.

food.name: Guangzhou Wonton Noodle Shops
food.near: Any traditional street near Beijing Road
food.must_order: Wonton noodle soup — shrimp and pork wontons in clear broth
food.tip: Guangzhou's definitive breakfast dish — silky noodles in a clear pork bone and dried flounder broth with plump wontons. Look for old-school shops with red-and-white tile, plastic stools, and elderly proprietors. ¥12–20 per bowl. Ask locals where they go — the best ones have no English menu.

food.name: Bingsheng Pinwei (炳胜品味)
food.must_order: Creative Cantonese dim sum, signature steamed dishes
food.tip: The most creative Cantonese cuisine in Guangzhou — not traditional, but pushing boundaries. The dim sum here is extraordinary — unusual ingredients, beautiful presentation. Popular with business diners and food-forward visitors. Book ahead for weekend lunch.

food.name: Canton Tower Observation Café
food.must_order: Coffee with Pearl River panorama
food.tip: Multiple cafes at the base of Canton Tower with river views. At night the tower changes colors against the city skyline. The riverside promenade has outdoor seating. For the ultimate Canton Tower photo, shoot from the Pearl River cruise at sunset — the tower and city skyline reflected in the water.

food.name: Chimelong Food Street
food.must_order: Any Cantonese theme park food
food.tip: Multiple dining options inside Chimelong Resort complex — the food quality is better than average theme park food. There are also many restaurants outside the main gate in Panyu where locals eat — 30% cheaper than park prices. Ask your hotel to recommend the best nearby.

food.name: Song Sichuan Cuisine
food.tip: Arrive early for lunch to avoid peak crowds. Order the mapo tofu and water-boiled fish for authentic Sichuan flavors. Request spice level adjustments if needed.

food.name: LOCKCHUCKCOFFEE
food.tip: A hidden gem along the Pearl River with a perfect 5-star rating. Ideal for a quiet coffee break away from touristy spots—arrive early for the best riverside atmosphere.

food.name: Apf. Kafe
food.tip: A hidden gem near Pearl River with specialty coffee. Visit during golden hour for riverside views. Try their signature hand-drip coffee for the full experience.

food.name: Starbucks Coffee
food.tip: Visit during off-peak hours (2-4pm) to enjoy Pearl River views without crowds. Try the China-exclusive teas like Dragon Well Latte for a local twist.

food.name: Kafelaku Coffee
food.tip: Popular riverside spot near Pearl River. Visit during golden hour for scenic waterfront views while enjoying your coffee. Outdoor seating fills up quickly on weekends.

food.name: Yuyuan Coffee
food.tip: Visit during golden hour for stunning Pearl River views from the window seats. Try their signature latte with local Guangdong honey for a unique twist.

food.name: Taotaoju
food.tip: Arrive before 10am for traditional Cantonese dim sum at its best. Must-try: shrimp dumplings and char siu buns. Expect queues on weekends.

food.name: Arabica Cafe
food.tip: Order their signature latte with the iconic Arabica logo latte art. Arrive mid-morning on weekdays to avoid crowds and secure a window seat with natural lighting.

food.name: Yutang Chunnuan Restaurant
food.tip: Popular choice for families visiting Chimelong. Book ahead during weekends and holidays as it gets busy with theme park visitors seeking convenient dining options.

food.name: Qiaomei Shijia
food.tip: Popular with Chimelong visitors for quick meals before or after the park. Arrive early during peak seasons to avoid crowds from nearby tourist attractions.

food.name: Nido Coffee
food.tip: A hidden gem near Chimelong. Ask for their specialty pour-over selections. Quieter during weekday mornings, perfect for a pre-park caffeine boost.

practical.payment: Set up Alipay International before landing. Link Visa/Mastercard via 'International Version.' WeChat Pay also works. Keep ¥200 cash for small stalls and markets.
practical.transport: Metro is clean, cheap, fully bilingual. Download Gaode Maps (高德地图) — far better than Google Maps for China. APM line (driverless monorail) is unique — sit in front or rear car for best views.
practical.connectivity: Get Airalo eSIM before flying or buy mainland SIM at airport. VPN essential — install before arriving. Google, Instagram, WhatsApp all blocked without VPN.
practical.language: Less English than Shanghai. Screenshot restaurant names in Chinese before going. Use Alipay's translation lens in restaurants.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping (大众点评) for restaurant vouchers — always cheaper than walk-in. Klook for Canton Tower tickets. Meituan for food delivery if staying multiple nights.
practical.book_ahead: Canton Tower timed entry (buy online). Popular dim sum on weekends — arrive before 10am or wait 45 mins. Panxi Restaurant — reservations recommended.
practical.best_time: October–December. Spring (March–April) also good. Summer is extremely hot and humid. Avoid Golden Week holidays.
practical.transport_tip: Three-day metro pass (¥50) covers 90% of attractions. Water bus ticket (¥2) for Pearl River views.

guangzhou.recommended_base_reason: Yuexiu district is central with metro access to all key sights

kids_attractions.name: Chimelong Safari Park
kids_attractions.tip: Asia's largest safari park — the crown jewel of the Chimelong resort complex. The safari tram through open lion, tiger and bear enclosures is extraordinary. The giant panda habitat has the most pandas outside Chengdu. The nightly 'World Circus' acrobatic show (included) is one of the best in China. Book via Klook for package deals combining multiple Chimelong parks.

kids_attractions.name: Chimelong Paradise (Theme Park)
kids_attractions.tip: Right next to Safari Park — the full thrill-ride theme park with 10 world-record roller coasters. The 'World Record Breaker' section alone has 4 record-holding rides. Better for families with older kids (8+) or teenagers. Combine with Safari Park using a 2-day combo ticket (great value).

kids_attractions.name: Guangzhou Children's Park
kids_attractions.tip: Guangzhou's best free outdoor playground — massive outdoor climbing structures, water play area, sandpit and toddler zones. Very popular with local families on weekends. Open 7am–9pm. Best for under-8s. The adjacent Parent-Child Activity Centre has indoor play for rainy days.

hotels.name: Four Seasons Hotel Guangzhou
hotels.tip: Floors 74–98 of the IFC tower — unmatched panoramic views over Pearl River and Canton Tower. Forbes Five-Star rated consistently. The Cantonese restaurant 'Jiang by Chef Fei' is one of Guangzhou's best. 5-min walk to river cruise pier and metro.

hotels.name: Curio Collection by Hilton, Yuexiu Hotel
hotels.chinese: 广州越秀希尔顿格芮精选酒店
hotels.area: Xiaobei Road, Yuexiu — next to Yuexiu Park
hotels.tip: The highest-rated hotel in Yuexiu district — adjacent to Yuexiu Park, 1km from Sun Yat-sen Memorial Hall. Rooms feature Cantonese cultural design with Dyson hairdryers, 55" TVs and washing machines. Kids play area on 7th floor. Exceptional service consistently praised in reviews.

hotels.name: Sofitel Guangzhou Sunrich
hotels.chinese: 广州圣丰索菲特大酒店
hotels.tip: Best value 5-star in Guangzhou — French elegance at significantly lower prices than Four Seasons or Ritz-Carlton. TripAdvisor 4.8/5 from 7,000+ reviews. Good pool, excellent breakfast. Convenient Tianhe location near metro. Praised by foreign travellers for English-speaking staff.

hotels.name: Mercure Guangzhou Beijing Road
hotels.tip: Best mid-range location for tourists — steps from Beijing Road pedestrian street, 3-min walk to metro. Friendly English-speaking reception (reviewers specifically praise the staff). Clean, modern rooms. Helpful with local dining and transport tips. Walk to Chen Clan Academy, Dafo Temple, Pearl River.

hotels.name: Guangzhou Hotel (越秀)
hotels.tip: Best budget option in the old city — metro station literally at hotel entrance (Haizhu Square, Lines 2 & 6), Pearl River views from upper floors. Recently renovated rooms. Direct bus to Hong Kong/Macau from front door. Reviewers consistently praise location as unbeatable for the price. Walk to Beijing Road in 5 minutes.

hotels.name: Rosewood Guangzhou
hotels.tip: In Guangzhou's tallest building (530m) — sky lobby and rooms from the 93rd floor upward. Forbes Five-Star 2025. Extraordinary views over Pearl River delta. The most exclusive address in Guangzhou.

hotels.name: Langham Place Guangzhou
hotels.tip: Directly connected to Canton Fair complex. Ming Court Cantonese restaurant is excellent. Riverside garden unique in Guangzhou. Langham reliability — consistent service. Walk to Pearl River night cruise.

hotels.name: Dorsett Chengdu — use Dorsett Guangzhou
hotels.tip: Solid mid-range right in the tourist heart of Guangzhou. Clean, spacious rooms. Families praised for breakfast variety and helpful staff. Walking distance to Chen Clan Academy.

hotels.name: Ibis Guangzhou Tianhe
hotels.tip: Best budget option near Tianhe CBD — Accor Ibis reliability at budget prices. Clean rooms, good metro access. Walk to Canton Tower area. Great for travellers who just need a clean, reliable base.

hotels.name: Chimelong Hotel (Panyu)
hotels.tip: THE hotel for families visiting Chimelong — themed animal rooms, early park entry, shuttle buses included. Kids go wild in the animal-themed corridors. Worth paying for if Chimelong Safari and Paradise are on your itinerary. Book animal theme rooms months ahead.

shenzhen.name: Shenzhen
shenzhen.tagline: China's Future City With a Local Soul

attractions.name: Ping An Finance Centre Observation Deck
attractions.chinese: 平安国际金融中心·观光层
attractions.tip: World's 4th tallest building at 599m. 'Free Sky' observation deck at 116th floor. On a clear day you can see Hong Kong's New Territories. Nicknamed '春笋' (bamboo shoot) by locals. Go evening for the best city lights. Book online — walk-up queue is brutal.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable for elderly.
attractions.description: The world's fourth tallest building at 599 metres — the observation deck on floor 116 gives the most dramatic bird's-eye view of any Chinese city, with Shenzhen's extraordinary skyline spreading to every horizon and Hong Kong's mountains visible on clear days.
attractions.photo_query: ping an finance centre shenzhen sky

attractions.name: OCT Loft Creative Culture Park
attractions.tip: Former factory district turned creative enclave. The abandoned aircraft inside (刷爆小红书的废弃飞机) went viral on 小红书 — it's a real decommissioned aircraft on-site used as an art installation. Galleries, cool indie cafes, street art, vintage markets. Called 'a hipster's paradise.'
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some cobblestone between buildings.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Dafen Oil Painting Village
attractions.tip: Village that once produced 60% of the world's oil paintings. Artists now making original work alongside copies. Get a custom oil portrait for ¥200–500. The alleyways now have trendy cafes and galleries alongside the studios. Very photogenic.
attractions.elderly_note: Some uneven alley surfaces. Otherwise accessible.
attractions.description: The world's most extraordinary art production village — 8,000 artists produce 60% of the world's decorative oil paintings in a single Shenzhen neighbourhood, from mass-produced Van Gogh copies to bespoke commissions. Fascinating and slightly surreal, it raises genuine questions about authorship and art.
attractions.photo_query: dafen oil painting village shenzhen artists

attractions.name: Lianhuashan Park
attractions.tip: Bronze Deng Xiaoping statue at the top is a pilgrimage for mainland Chinese visitors. The panoramic Shenzhen skyline from the hill is perfectly aligned with the Civic Center skyscrapers — iconic shot. Described as 'a hidden gem — hard to believe it's in the middle of the city.' Spotlessly maintained.
attractions.elderly_note: Gentle incline to top. Benches throughout.
attractions.description: The hill at the heart of Shenzhen bearing the most important statue in the city — Deng Xiaoping's bronze figure looks southward toward Hong Kong, the direction China turned when it opened to the world. The view from the summit over the entire Futian CBD is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Nantou Ancient Town
attractions.tip: 1,700-year-old walled city revitalized since 2019. Where the Treaty of Nanjing was signed inside — both Shenzhen and Hong Kong trace their administrative roots here. Now: red brick lanes, craft coffee shops, indie boutiques, art exhibitions, weekend pop-up markets. Best described: 'ancient China meets Brooklyn.' Open daily 10am–8pm, closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat cobblestone lanes.
attractions.description: The oldest settlement in Shenzhen — a 1,700-year-old walled town that somehow survived the city's explosive growth, now being carefully restored alongside trendy cafes and studios. The juxtaposition of ancient China inside a modern megacity is unlike anything else.

attractions.name: Dameisha Beach
attractions.tip: 1.8km of golden sand backed by green hills — free entry, genuinely beautiful. Best beach in mainland China near a major city. Parasol and jet ski rentals available. Go weekday morning — weekend crowds are enormous. Metro Line 8 to Dameisha Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat beach. Suitable with care on sand.
attractions.description: Shenzhen's most popular free beach — a 2km crescent of white sand backed by green hills on the eastern coast of the city. The water quality is significantly better than Sanya's mass-market resorts and the surrounding Dapeng National Geopark provides extraordinary coastal scenery.

attractions.name: Yantian Coastal Boardwalk
attractions.tip: 19.5km coastal greenway — Shenzhen's most beautiful coastal walk. Connects Shatoujiao, Yantian Harbour, Dameisha to Xiaomeisha. Rocky sea views, lighthouse at Beizai Corner, 'Fairy Tale Town' photo spot. Sunrise is spectacular. Cycle or walk.
attractions.elderly_note: Long route — do only sections. Metro access at multiple points.
attractions.description: A 7km elevated boardwalk along the most unspoiled coastline in the Pearl River Delta — the green hills of the Wutong Mountain National Forest Park on one side, the emerald water of Dapeng Bay on the other. Few people outside Shenzhen know this exists.

attractions.name: Dongmen Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: Shenzhen's oldest shopping street — bulk fashion, electronics, street food. The fresh-pounded lemon tea (手打柠檬茶) stalls here are a Shenzhen institution. White Rabbit sweet variations in multiple flavors at corner stalls. BBQ skewers everywhere. Evening atmosphere is chaotic and fun.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Window of the World
attractions.tip: 130 miniature world landmarks. Sunday night at 9pm: 1,500-drone light show above the Arch of Triumph — cyberpunk patterns. Robot interactions near major landmarks weekends 2–4pm. Best for families and kids. Evening has more atmosphere than daytime.
attractions.elderly_note: Tram transport within park. Suitable.
attractions.description: 75 famous world landmarks scaled down and crammed into one Shenzhen theme park — including a 1/3 scale Eiffel Tower that you can climb. More interesting as a uniquely Chinese phenomenon than as a tourist attraction, it reveals an entire country's relationship with the world it encountered late.
attractions.photo_attribution: Window of the World Ticket Office

attractions.name: Dapeng Peninsula & Ancient City
attractions.tip: Eastern peninsula: speed boats, clifftop hikes, fresh seafood fishing villages. Dapeng Ancient City (大鹏所城) is a 600-year-old Ming dynasty fortress — well-preserved, less touristy. Combine with Judiaosha Beach (角场尾) and Yangmeikeng (杨梅坑 — the bluest water in Shenzhen) for a full day.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable due to distance and terrain.
attractions.description: A Ming dynasty coastal fortress 1.5 hours from Shenzhen city — remarkably intact, with original battlements, moats and ancestral halls still inhabited by descendants of the soldiers who built it. The surrounding Dapeng Peninsula has some of the best beaches on China's east coast.

attractions.name: Sea World (Shekou)
attractions.tip: Expat enclave with Western-style bars and restaurants built around a grounded ship (Minghu). The adjacent Sea World Culture and Arts Center is an excellent contemporary art museum — free on some days. Easy access to Hong Kong ferry from here.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat area. Suitable.
attractions.description: Shenzhen's most international neighbourhood — a permanently docked 1970s cruise ship converted into a concert venue, surrounded by bars and restaurants that attract the city's large expat community. The waterfront plaza hosts concerts and events most evenings.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Bay Park
attractions.tip: 6.6km seafront greenway along Shenzhen Bay — bike, walk, or rollerskate. Sunset views of the bay and Shenzhen Bay Bridge are excellent. Migratory birds (Oct–March) including rare black-faced spoonbills. Best evening city walk in Shenzhen. Metro Line 9 to Shenzhen Bay Park Station Exit D2.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat paved path. Ideal for elderly.
attractions.description: A 15km coastal park stretching along Shenzhen Bay with unobstructed views across the water to Hong Kong's New Territories mountains. At sunset the mountains turn purple and the bay turns gold simultaneously — one of China's best urban running and cycling routes.
attractions.photo_query: shenzhen bay park sunset hong kong

attractions.name: Huaqiangbei Electronics Market
attractions.tip: China's electronics wholesale capital — phone parts, gadgets, robotics, surveillance systems, drones. SEG E-Market and Huaqiangbei Road have floor after floor of component stalls. If you like tech, this is mesmerizing. Don't buy without knowing prices first. Metro: Huaqiang North Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Busy and crowded. Not recommended for elderly.
attractions.description: The world's most important electronics market — 100,000 stalls across multiple interconnected buildings selling every electronic component and gadget imaginable, many of which won't appear anywhere else for six months. The energy of human ingenuity at industrial scale is genuinely thrilling.

attractions.name: Gangtang Hakka Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Called 'Shenzhen's 梦华录' (Dream of Splendor). Hakka culture village with 350-year history — ancient watchtowers, traditional houses, manga museum, ceramics workshop, and Hakka food. Rent hanfu (汉服) from ¥40 for photos. Weekday recommended. Metro Line 10 to Gangtang Station, then community bus.
attractions.elderly_note: Some uneven paths. Mostly accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zhong-Ying Street (Chung Ying Street)
attractions.tip: Historically unique: one side is Shenzhen, the other is Hong Kong (Sha Tau Kok). 4-meter-wide street, two systems. Old shopfront signs in both Simplified and Traditional Chinese. Requires ID registration to enter. Historic for 'one country two systems' photography. Not busy — almost no tourists know it.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat street. Easy walking.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: OCT Harbour & Bay Glory Ferris Wheel
attractions.chinese: 欢乐港湾·湾区之光摩天轮
attractions.tip: Called '湾区之光' (Light of the Bay Area) — one of China's largest sea-view Ferris wheels with transparent gondolas. 360-degree view: Shenzhen Bay, Qianhai, Hong Kong on clear days. Photo spot at the LOVE light sign at base. Best 30 minutes after sunset when city lights come on. Book online.
attractions.elderly_note: Gondola entry requires stepping. Otherwise suitable.
attractions.description: The most scenic ferris wheel in China — 128 metres tall and situated directly on the Shenzhen Bay waterfront, each capsule enclosed in glass for 360-degree views of the bay, Hong Kong and the city skyline. The reflection on the bay at night is extraordinary.
attractions.photo_query: oct harbour ferris wheel shenzhen bay night

attractions.name: Talangshan Country Park (Sunset Hike)
attractions.tip: 1-hour hike to summit — panoramic Shenzhen city and bay views. Perfect for sunset: start at 6pm, reach top at 7pm as city lights emerge. Metro Line 5 to Tanglang Station. Bring hiking shoes and water. Less crowded than other Shenzhen hills.
attractions.elderly_note: Steep hill. Not suitable for elderly.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Splendid China Folk Village
attractions.tip: Miniature reproductions of 82 China landmarks (Great Wall, Forbidden City, Potala Palace, Terracotta Warriors) at 1:15 scale plus 27 ethnic minority villages. Evening light shows. Good for one comprehensive China culture overview before visiting real sites.
attractions.elderly_note: Large park — tram available. Good for elderly.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Museum
attractions.tip: Tells Shenzhen's incredible story from a 1970s fishing village to China's Silicon Valley in 40 years. Free entry — book via WeChat in advance. The history exhibition is genuinely fascinating: includes exhibits on the first people to 'jump into the sea' (下海) to start businesses after Deng Xiaoping's reform.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible museum.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Happy Valley Theme Park
attractions.tip: 86-acre theme park with 100+ rides. China's first suspended roller coaster (Xueshan Feilong). Playa Maya Water Park inside, open May–early October. Night shows on weekends. Best for families with teens.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable for most elderly — rides are intense.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shenzhen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Central Park
attractions.tip: Enter from the north gate near Chegongmiao Station for the best lotus pond views, especially beautiful from June to August. Rent a bicycle at the east entrance to cover more ground efficiently.
attractions.description: This expansive urban oasis stretches through Futian's heart with tree-lined paths, serene ponds, and bamboo groves offering rare tranquility amid the skyscraper jungle. Come early morning to witness locals practicing tai chi and dancing alongside the misty waterways.

attractions.name: World Sculpture Park
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon for softer lighting that's perfect for photos. The replicas look most impressive from angles that hide the surrounding modern buildings in your frame.
attractions.description: A landscaped garden within Window of the World displaying miniature replicas of iconic global monuments from the Eiffel Tower to the Taj Mahal. Experience a whirlwind world tour in one afternoon without leaving Shenzhen.

attractions.name: Lizhi Park
attractions.tip: Visit in late May to early June during litchi harvest season when the trees are laden with fruit and locals gather to taste fresh litchis. The northwest corner near the children's playground has the oldest trees.
attractions.description: This 29-hectare urban oasis in Futian's heart shelters hundreds of ancient litchi trees around a serene lake crossed by traditional bridges. Locals gather here at dawn for tai chi and evening strolls, offering an authentic glimpse into Shenzhen's slower rhythms amid the city buzz.

attractions.name: OCT (Overseas Chinese Town)
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid crowds at the miniature Great Wall and Forbidden City replicas. The traditional dance shows at 2pm and 7pm are included in admission and worth planning around.
attractions.description: This sprawling cultural complex showcases miniature replicas of China's most iconic landmarks alongside traditional dance performances and contemporary art galleries. It's where Southeast Asian visitors can experience centuries of Chinese heritage compressed into a single walkable park.

attractions.name: East OCT (Overseas Chinese Town)
attractions.tip: Buy the combined ticket for both Tea Stream Valley and Grand Canyon parks if visiting on weekends to avoid separate queuing. Weekdays see significantly fewer crowds, especially at the water park and cable car stations.
attractions.description: This sprawling eco-tourism resort in Yantian district combines theme parks, a water park, scenic valley trails, and replica European villages across lush mountain terrain. It's a one-stop destination where families can experience thrilling rides, nature walks, and cultural attractions without leaving the complex.

attractions.name: He Xiangning Art Museum
attractions.tip: Visit on weekdays when the galleries are nearly empty for a meditative experience. The museum cafe on the ground floor serves excellent Yunnan coffee and overlooks a peaceful sculpture garden perfect for post-visit reflection.
attractions.description: This contemporary art museum spans three elegant floors showcasing modern Chinese paintings and rotating exhibitions honoring revolutionary artist He Xiangning. The thoughtfully curated galleries offer a rare window into 20th-century Chinese art movements rarely seen outside major cultural capitals.

attractions.name: COCO Park
attractions.tip: Visit the basement level supermarket Ole for imported groceries and ready-made meals. The north plaza hosts weekend markets and performances—check the mall's WeChat account for schedules.
attractions.description: This open-air lifestyle complex blends international fashion brands with tree-lined plazas where locals gather from dawn to dusk. The ground floor dining corridor delivers everything from Sichuan hotpot to specialty coffee, making it Shenzhen's urban living room.

attractions.name: Window of the World Ticket Office
attractions.tip: Buy tickets online through the official WeChat mini-program at least a day ahead for 10-20% discount compared to walk-up prices, and skip the queues during weekends and holidays.
attractions.description: This is the main ticketing center for Window of the World, Shenzhen's famous theme park featuring miniature replicas of global landmarks from the Eiffel Tower to the Pyramids. Purchase your entry here to explore over 130 scaled reproductions of world monuments and cultural performances in one sprawling park.

food.name: Dim Sum at Fanlou
food.where: Fanlou 蘩楼 — Futian District, multiple locations. Famous spot always with queues.
food.tip: Arrive before 10am to get a number. XLB (小笼包), siu mai (烧卖), and shrimp dumplings (虾饺) are standouts. Check Dianping for voucher deals — usually 80元 for 100元 worth. The cha siu bao (叉烧包) here is exceptional.

food.name: Taier Sauerkraut Fish
food.where: Taier 太二 — multiple Shenzhen locations (CoCo Park, Coastal City, etc.)
food.tip: Famous Shenzhen-origin chain known for historic 4-hour queues. Use WeChat/app remote queue while exploring nearby. One fish, one pot, one sauce. The sauerkraut broth is genuinely unlike fish hotpot anywhere else. The restaurant's cheeky rule: 'No modifications to the menu.'

food.name: BBQ Skewers at CoCo Park
food.where: 西塔老太太泥炉烤肉 at CoCo Park, Futian — popular Friday night local hangout
food.tip: The grilled eel (烤鳗鱼) here is extraordinary — smoky sweetness unlike anything in Singapore. Lemon8 SG travellers repeatedly call it a 'taste you can't find back home.' Also order: enoki mushrooms with chili oil (金针菇), lamb skewers (羊肉串).

food.name: Fermented Rice Wine Milk Tea
food.where: 阿嬷手作 (Grandma Handmade) — Futian area multiple locations
food.tip: Exclusive to Shenzhen/GZ PRD region. Fermented rice wine base with taro balls and milk tea cream — complex, slightly tart, unlike anything in Singapore. Multiple Lemon8 reviewers call it their single best drink discovery in China.

food.name: Spicy Sour Glass Noodles
food.where: 连城新天地 (Liancheng Xintianye) mall food court
food.tip: Thick potato starch noodles in a spicy-sour broth — 中辣 (medium) unless you're confident. At ¥9.90 a bowl, best value eat in Shenzhen. Completely different texture from Singapore's instant version.

food.name: Crab Roe Pasta (Trending Fusion)
food.where: Gentle Pasta 健意 — PA Mall, Futian
food.tip: Italian pasta with Chinese crab roe sauce — trending hard on 小红书 and Lemon8. Weekend wait is about 30 mins. The signature crab roe pasta is non-negotiable order. Unusual fusion that works remarkably well.

food.name: Egg Tarts (HK Style)
food.where: HK-style cha chaan teng cafes throughout Shenzhen; Sudaxing bakery
food.tip: Shenzhen's proximity to HK means exceptional Hong Kong-style egg tarts everywhere. Multi-layered flaky pastry, creamy custard. Eat fresh out of the oven. Significantly better and cheaper than Singapore versions.

food.name: Claypot Rice (Random Local Shop)
food.where: Any local 煲仔饭 shop in Futian or Luohu neighborhoods — look for the clay pots on open stoves
food.tip: In Shenzhen, unlike GZ, good claypot rice isn't at famous shops — it's the random neighborhood place. Multiple Lemon8 reviews: 'went into a random place in Futian, staff so friendly, food so good and cheap.' Look for smoke coming from the pots.

food.name: Potchuang Hakka Food at Gangtang
food.where: Restaurants inside Gangtang Hakka Town 甘坑客家小镇
food.tip: Hakka cuisine: 酿豆腐 (stuffed tofu with pork — Hakka's most famous dish), 猪肚鸡 (pork stomach and chicken soup with white pepper), 客家黄酒 (fermented rice wine). Earthy, home-style, deeply traditional. Very different from Cantonese.

food.name: Late Night Congee Street (Sandpot Congee)
food.where: 卖鱼佬砂锅粥 (Luohu) — Dianping 2024 Must-Eat Award winner. 牛巷番薯粥 — Dianping Top 1 small eats.
food.tip: Shenzhen's late-night supper culture is centered around slow-cooked sandpot congee. Unlike Guangzhou's boat congee, this version simmers longer and is served in hot clay pots. Seafood, pork, fish — order the freshest item of the day. Best after 9pm.

food.name: Hand-Pounded Lemon Tea
food.where: Stalls throughout Dongmen and Futian areas — look for live pounding action
food.tip: The lemon tea here is fresh-pounded with actual whole lemons bashed against the cup to release oils from the skin. Completely different from Singapore's bottled lemon tea. Always order 微糖 (low sugar). A Shenzhen institution.

food.name: Braised Duck & Chicken Feet
food.where: Dongmen area stalls; Shuiwei Night Market, Futian
food.tip: Shenzhen's brine-braised meats are exceptional — braised duck with aromatic spice broth, chicken feet in mala (spicy numbing) or lemon flavor. The Shuiwei Night Market in Futian is the best night food destination —琳琅满目的小吃 (endless variety of local snacks).

food.name: Chagee (Tea) — Original
food.where: Multiple locations throughout Shenzhen
food.tip: Chagee originated in Yunnan and exploded across China. In Shenzhen it's ¥18–25 vs Singapore's ¥7–10 premium. The TGTC (Tie Guan Yin milk tea with cheese cream) here uses actual tieguanyin — different product to what Singapore gets. Worth the comparison.

food.name: Omakase at Sushi Jyukan
food.where: Sushi Jyukan — Futian District (search on Gaode Maps)
food.tip: Premium omakase with foie gras and sea urchin for ~SGD$56 — viral on Lemon8 SG as the best value omakase outside Japan. The quality-to-price ratio shocked multiple Singapore reviewers. Booking essential — WeChat only.

food.name: OCT-LOFT Cafes & Restaurants
food.must_order: Single origin coffee, creative lunch sets
food.tip: Shenzhen's coolest creative district — converted factory buildings filled with galleries, indie cafes and design studios. The cafes here are all independently run with distinct concepts. Browse the whole park before settling on one. Best on weekday afternoons.

food.name: Sea World Shekou Food Strip
food.must_order: International food variety — Thai, Japanese, Western, local
food.tip: Shenzhen's most international food strip — dozens of restaurants surrounding the permanently docked cruise ship 'The World'. Young, vibrant atmosphere. Best at night when the Sea World plaza fills up. Good options for SEA travellers who want familiar flavours with a Shenzhen twist.

food.name: Shenzhen Bay Park Cafe
food.must_order: Cold brew to go, walk along bay
food.tip: Multiple cafe kiosks along the Shenzhen Bay waterfront with views to Hong Kong across the bay. Best at sunset when the light turns golden and Hong Kong's New Territories mountains are silhouetted. Cyclists and joggers fill the path — great energy.

food.name: Futian CBD Rooftop Bars
food.near: Futian CBD, near Ping An Finance Centre
food.must_order: Cocktail at sunset with city skyline view
food.tip: Shenzhen's most dramatic city views — the Ping An Finance Centre (599m, world's 4th tallest) dominates the skyline. COCO Park area has several rooftop bars with excellent views. Go at dusk to see the city transition from day to night. A spectacular contrast to Hong Kong's harbour.

food.name: MixC Shenzhen Bay Food Hall
food.must_order: Browse — best regional Chinese food stalls in one place
food.tip: Shenzhen's locals eat here — a massive food hall in the MixC mall with every regional Chinese cuisine. Peking duck, Sichuan hot pot, Cantonese roast meats, Yunnan rice noodles all under one roof. Much better value than tourist restaurants. Kids love the variety.

food.name: Huayueting
food.tip: Specializes in refined Cantonese cuisine. Try their dim sum during lunch service for the freshest selection. Arrive before noon on weekends to avoid peak crowds.

food.name: Azzurro Italy Private Kitchens
food.tip: Reserve ahead for the private dining rooms. Ask for the daily fresh pasta specials and seasonal truffle dishes when available. Quieter during weekday lunches.

food.name: Huachu
food.tip: Popular spot in Futian's business district, ideal for lunch meetings. Arrive before noon to avoid peak crowds. Request a table near the window for better ambiance.

food.name: BLEND Cafe
food.tip: Try their hand-drip coffee selection for a refined experience. Afternoon visits offer a quieter atmosphere perfect for working or reading with quality specialty brews.

food.name: 92 Coffee Club
food.tip: Popular spot in Futian's business district. Visit during weekday afternoons to avoid the morning rush and enjoy a quieter atmosphere for remote work or meetings.

food.name: TT Cafe
food.tip: Popular spot in Futian's business district. Arrive before 10am on weekdays to avoid the office rush and secure a window seat with skyline views.

food.name: Starbucks
food.tip: Visit during weekday mornings to avoid crowds. Try the China-exclusive hazelnut latte or seasonal regional drinks not available elsewhere. Mobile order ahead to skip lines.

food.name: Something For
food.tip: Popular spot in Futian's business district. Arrive before 11am on weekends to snag a window seat, as the cafe fills up quickly with brunch crowds.

food.name: Morse Coffee
food.tip: Popular neighborhood spot near OCT attractions. Arrive before 10am on weekends to snag a window seat and avoid the brunch crowd from nearby creative parks.

food.name: Coffee Is Very Good
food.tip: Visit during weekday afternoons to avoid crowds. Their signature hand-drip coffee is highly recommended by regulars. Seating is limited, so consider takeaway during peak hours.

food.name: GEE Coffee Roasters
food.tip: Visit in the afternoon to catch the roasting process in action. Their single-origin pour-overs showcase freshly roasted beans—ask the barista for tasting notes and brewing recommendations.

food.name: Fanlou
food.tip: Traditional Cantonese restaurant serving classic dim sum and stir-fries. Visit during lunch hours for the freshest steamed dishes. Request a table near the window for better ambiance.

food.name: Home Cafe
food.tip: A cozy neighborhood spot perfect for remote work. Arrive early on weekends to snag a window seat with natural light and power outlets.

food.name: Luckin Coffee
food.tip: Download the app for significant discounts and promotions. The raw coconut latte is a local favorite. Avoid peak morning hours (8-9am) when office workers queue heavily.

food.name: Xiti Island Coffee
food.tip: A neighborhood café in Yantian's eastern district. Good for a quiet coffee break away from the city center. Try their specialty coffee drinks.

food.name: Black White Cafe Bar
food.tip: Popular neighborhood spot in Yantian East with a dual identity. Visit during daytime for coffee, or return in the evening when it transforms into a casual bar.

food.name: Jade Garden
food.tip: Order their signature Cantonese dim sum during lunch service when items are freshest. The steamed scallop dumplings and char siu bao are standout choices worth arriving early for.

food.name: NCafe
food.tip: Popular among tech workers from nearby Nanshan offices. Arrive before 10am on weekdays for quieter seating and fresher pastries before the lunch rush.

food.name: Back Coffee Shop
food.tip: Popular spot near theme parks for quality espresso drinks. Arrive before 10am on weekends to avoid crowds from nearby attractions. Their flat white is consistently excellent.

practical.payment: Alipay and WeChat Pay. Link YouTrip or Trust Bank card for fee-free CNY payments. ¥200 cash for markets.
practical.transport: 17-line metro — very efficient and bilingual. High-speed rail to Guangzhou takes 30–40 mins from Shenzhen North (深圳北). Easy day trip between cities.
practical.from_hk: High-speed rail from West Kowloon to Futian: 13 minutes. Lo Wu/Lok Ma Chau border crossing also easy. Ferry from Shekou to Hong Kong Pier: 30 mins.
practical.connectivity: VPN essential before arrival. Airalo eSIM recommended. Download DiDi for taxis.
practical.best_time: October–December. Avoid summer (extremely hot and humid). November–December best weather.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping for restaurant discovery and vouchers. Klook for Window of the World, Happy Valley, theme parks. WeChat for most local bookings.
practical.book_ahead: Ping An observation deck online. Window of the World drone show — Sunday 9pm. Gangtang recommended weekdays.

shenzhen.recommended_base_reason: Nanshan/Futian area has best metro access to OCT parks

kids_attractions.name: Shenzhen Happy Valley
kids_attractions.tip: The original Happy Valley — the template for all others across China. Best for families with kids 7+. The wooden roller coaster Gold Rushing is iconic. Thrilling for teens, manageable for brave 8-year-olds. Combine with nearby Window of the World on the same day using combo ticket.

kids_attractions.name: Window of the World
kids_attractions.tip: Scale replicas of 130 world wonders — Eiffel Tower, Taj Mahal, Egyptian Pyramids, Grand Canyon. Kids can 'travel the world' in one afternoon. Less about thrill rides, more about exploration and photography. The evening show 'World Stage' has acrobatics and cultural performances. Great for younger children who can't do big rides.

kids_attractions.name: Shenzhen Museum of Natural History
kids_attractions.tip: Opened 2024 — one of China's most impressive new natural history museums. Outstanding dinosaur exhibition with interactive holographic displays. The biodiversity section has living insects, reptiles and amphibians. Free but book in advance. Award-winning architecture by Lvl. Particularly good for ages 5–12.

kids_attractions.name: OCT-Loft & Shenzhen Children's Museum
kids_attractions.tip: The Shenzhen Children's Museum of Art within OCT-Loft is specifically designed for children's creativity — interactive art installations, painting workshops and sensory play spaces. The surrounding creative district has excellent cafes for parents. Free on weekdays.

hotels.name: The Langham, Shenzhen
hotels.tip: Consistently Shenzhen's highest-rated 5-star — Top 1% of hotels worldwide (TripAdvisor 2025). Legendary service, immaculate rooms. 8-min walk to metro. Often praised as better value than staying in Hong Kong. T'ang Court Cantonese restaurant is exceptional. Outdoor and indoor pools.

hotels.name: Futian Shangri-La Shenzhen
hotels.chinese: 深圳福田香格里拉大酒店
hotels.tip: Iconic Futian landmark directly connected to Convention Centre — excellent if attending any Shenzhen events. Rooftop pool with city views. The breakfast buffet is one of Shenzhen's best. 5 min walk to Futian Railway Station for Hong Kong (14 mins). Family-friendly with children's pool.

hotels.name: Westin Shenzhen Nanshan
hotels.area: Nanshan District, near Window of the World
hotels.tip: Best choice for families visiting Nanshan theme parks — Window of the World, Happy Valley and OCT all within 10 mins. Shenzhen Bay Park nearby for morning runs. Good pool and family rooms. More relaxed atmosphere than Futian CBD hotels. Shekou Sea World dining area 15 mins.

hotels.name: Hyatt Place Shenzhen Dongmen
hotels.chinese: 深圳东门凯悦嘉轩酒店
hotels.tip: Excellent mid-range in a vibrant, local neighbourhood. 9.0/10 Trip.com with consistent praise for spacious rooms and varied breakfast spread. Above a metro station and shopping mall. Good English-speaking staff. Close to Dongmen pedestrian street for local shopping and street food. Best mid-range for authentic Shenzhen experience.

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Shenzhen Futian Huaqiangbei
hotels.chinese: 深圳华强北希尔顿欢朋酒店
hotels.tip: Best-rated budget option in Futian — Hampton reliability at affordable prices. Right above metro station (Lines 1, 2, 3 all accessible). Free hot breakfast included. Staff praised for helpfulness with English. Walk to Huaqiangbei electronics market. Easy access to all Futian sights. A great choice for price-conscious travellers who don't want to sacrifice location.

hotels.name: Four Seasons Hotel Shenzhen
hotels.tip: Forbes Five-Star in Futian's financial heart. Sky lobby with panoramic city views. Directly accessible from Futian metro station. Ping An Finance Centre is 10 min drive. Michelin restaurant on-site.

hotels.name: Andaz Shenzhen Bay
hotels.tip: Design-led Hyatt luxury near Shenzhen Bay Park. Modern, artsy aesthetic with stunning sea views. Close to Nanshan theme parks and Sea World Shekou dining area. Popular with younger luxury travellers.

hotels.name: Residence G Shenzhen
hotels.tip: Hip design hotel in Shekou Sea World area — surrounded by international dining, bars and Shenzhen Bay waterfront. Praised for stylish rooms and young, helpful staff. Good mid-range for Nanshan sightseeing.

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Shenzhen Futian
hotels.chinese: 深圳福田智选假日酒店
hotels.tip: IHG reliability at budget prices in Futian. Free hot breakfast included. Good metro access. Clean, modern rooms. English-speaking staff. Walk to COCO Park mall. The right choice for budget travellers who want Futian's central location.

hotels.name: Hotel Indigo Shenzhen OCT
hotels.chinese: 深圳华侨城英迪格酒店
hotels.tip: Boutique IHG luxury hotel inside OCT-Loft creative district. Outstanding design — every floor has a different artistic concept. Walk to OCT galleries, cafes and natural history museum. Popular with design-conscious travellers and families visiting Nanshan attractions.

shanghai.name: Shanghai
shanghai.best_time: March–May (spring) and September–November (autumn). November is peak season from SG — golden foliage.

attractions.name: The Bund
attractions.tip: 52 historic buildings in Gothic, Baroque, Art Deco styles — the 'Museum of 10,000 Nations.' Best photo position: 九江路 (Jiujiang Road) for the full sweep. Evening light show from May–October. Don't miss at 23:00 — lights turn off sharp. Walk to Waibaidu Bridge (外白渡桥) for classic Old Shanghai shots.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside promenade. Excellent for elderly.
attractions.description: A kilometre of 52 colonial-era buildings facing the Huangpu River — the architectural record of Shanghai's extraordinary history as the world's most cosmopolitan city. The view from the Bund looking across to Pudong's futuristic skyline captures old and new Shanghai in a single frame that stops everyone.

attractions.name: Tianzifang
attractions.tip: 石库门 Shikumen stone-gate architecture converted to creative lanes. Get there by 10am before tour groups. The real value is the side alleys — Tianzifang alleys get the tourist traffic, but duck into the smaller 弄堂 (longtang) side streets for the authentic lanes. Metro Line 9: Dapuqiao Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Some narrow alleys. Mostly flat.
attractions.description: A 1930s factory complex converted into a labyrinth of indie boutiques, cafes and art studios inside preserved shikumen lane houses. More authentic atmosphere than Xintiandi, with better prices and fewer tour groups — the rooftop terrace cafes overlooking the maze of alleyways below are extraordinary.

attractions.name: Yu Garden (Yuyuan)
attractions.tip: 450-year-old Ming dynasty garden. The 玉玲珑 (Yu Linglong) rock alongside Su Zhou's Guan Yun Peak and Hangzhou's Zhou Yun Peak are the 'Three Great Taihu Rocks of the South.' The surrounding commercial bazaar: eat at Nanxiang Steamed Bun Restaurant for XLB at the 九曲桥 (Nine-Bend Bridge). Beautiful lit up at night.
attractions.elderly_note: Some steps within garden. Overall accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Wukang Road & French Concession
attractions.tip: 14 heritage buildings along Wukang Road including Wukang Building (武康大楼), Ba Jin's former residence, Song Qingling's former home. The full walk: Wukang Road → Anfu Road → Fumin Road → Changshu Road. The %Arabica coffee opposite Wukang Building's curved face is the best photo spot. Go before noon — afternoon gets very crowded.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat tree-lined streets. Ideal for leisurely walks.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xintiandi
attractions.tip: Shikumen architecture preserved as upscale restaurant and retail district. Navigate to 'Xintiandi North' (新天地北里) for the best section. Also contains Site of First National Congress of the Communist Party — historic and free to visit. Walk to adjacent Sinan Mansions (思南公馆) for gorgeous garden villas.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat heritage streets. Good for elderly.
attractions.description: 1920s shikumen lane houses converted into Shanghai's most upscale dining and nightlife precinct — the restored grey brick and dark wood architecture is genuine, even if the prices are not. The square at its heart is the ideal place to watch Shanghai dress up on a Friday evening.

attractions.name: Shanghai Tower Sky Observatory
attractions.chinese: 上海中心大厦·上海之巅
attractions.tip: World's fastest elevator. 119th floor — 2nd highest observation deck globally. FREE HACK: Go to B1 level entrance of Shanghai Tower — there's a free panoramic viewing area inside the lobby that most tourists don't know about. Actual paid deck is ¥180 but worth it for the best views. See all 3 Lujiazui towers from above.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable for elderly.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Nanjing Road Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: Look for the HKRI Taikoo Hui mall on Nanjing Road — inside is the TikTok/小红书-famous curved dragon escalator (龙形自动扶梯). The mall's B1 food hall has excellent local eats. At night the whole street glows with neon — classic Shanghai energy.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Very accessible.
attractions.description: The world's busiest shopping street — 1.8 kilometres of department stores, street food vendors and neon signs stretching from the Bund to People's Square. Garish, crowded and absolutely essential for understanding the commercial energy that defines modern Shanghai.

attractions.name: Zhujiajiao Water Town
attractions.tip: Ancient water town 1 hr from city — gondola rides (¥80/boat), 36 stone bridges, riverside Ming-Qing architecture. 'Nine turns and eighteen bends' waterway. Go weekday — weekends are packed. Take Metro Line 17 to Zhujiajiao Station. Combine with Qingpu for a full half-day trip.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat canal paths. Gondola is accessible with assistance.
attractions.description: A must-visit experience in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Shanghai Zhujiajiao Ancient Town Tourist Zone

attractions.name: 1933 Old Millfun
attractions.tip: Former 1933 slaughterhouse — Art Deco meets Bauhaus, outer square inner circle, concrete spiral ramps wide enough for cattle, interlocking sky bridges. Now galleries, cafes, restaurants, art events. Called 'a light and shadow maze of industrial heritage.' Few tourists know it. Art photography lovers will obsess over this building.
attractions.elderly_note: Some ramps and stairs. Partially accessible.
