attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: M50 Art District
attractions.tip: 120+ contemporary art galleries in a converted Suzhou Creek textile mill. Shanghai's real underground art scene. Free entry to most spaces. Affordable original art prints available. Combine with a walk along Suzhou Creek riverside for a quiet, unhurried Shanghai afternoon.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat factory compound. Accessible.
attractions.description: Fifty contemporary art galleries in a preserved 1930s cotton mill along Suzhou Creek — the most important contemporary art hub in China after 798 in Beijing. The raw industrial architecture provides perfect neutral space for art ranging from established Chinese masters to emerging experimental artists.

attractions.name: Shanghai Museum (People's Square)
attractions.chinese: 上海博物馆·人民广场馆
attractions.tip: One of China's four great museums — nearly 1 million artefacts across 21 categories: bronze, ceramics, calligraphy, jade, painting, coin. Free but requires WeChat advance booking (People's Square branch). East branch (浦东馆) doesn't require advance booking. Book Tuesday–Sunday 9am–5pm. Closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible. Elevator throughout.
attractions.description: One of the world's great Asian art museums — 120,000 artefacts spanning 5,000 years of Chinese civilisation in a purpose-built space designed to look like a bronze ding vessel. The jade collection and the ancient bronze collection are among the finest on earth, and it's completely free.

attractions.name: Jing'an Temple & District
attractions.tip: Active Buddhist temple sandwiched between luxury malls — gold roof, incense, monks amidst skyscrapers. The juxtaposition is uniquely Shanghai. The surrounding Jing'an district is Shanghai's most livable neighborhood — good cafes, Jade Buddha Temple nearby (free), international vibe. Metro Line 2/7: Jing'an Temple Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Some steps at temple entrance. Surrounding streets flat.
attractions.description: A 1,700-year-old Buddhist temple incongruously surrounded by luxury skyscrapers in the heart of Shanghai's wealthiest district — golden statues, incense smoke and monks in grey robes against a backdrop of Cartier and Gucci. The contrast is perfectly Shanghai.

attractions.name: Sinan Mansions (Sinan Road)
attractions.tip: Former French Concession garden villas housing high-end restaurants and boutiques. Plane trees lining the streets create a natural canopy. A quieter, more refined alternative to Xintiandi. The old Song Qingling residence and Zhou Enlai's former home are nearby. Best on a sunny afternoon.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat streets. Ideal for gentle walks.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Suzhou Creek Walking Route
attractions.tip: Locals call this Shanghai's best-kept secret walking route — far less crowded than Bund or Wukang Road. The riverbanks from Changhua Road to Suzhou Creek are lined with restored warehouse galleries and independent coffee shops. Morning mist on the creek is atmospheric. Better Citywalk than Wukang Road for avoiding crowds.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside path. Excellent for elderly.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shanghai Disney Resort
attractions.tip: TRON Lightcycle Run is the main unique ride (not at other Disneys). Buy tickets with Premier Access for the big rides — worth the premium on weekends. New spring festival light shows in 2025 — fireworks at 20:15 and 21:30. Book minimum 1 week ahead. Metro Line 11 direct from city.
attractions.elderly_note: Large park — wheelchair rental available. Suitable with mobility aids.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Longhua Temple
attractions.tip: Shanghai's oldest and largest Buddhist temple, founded 242 AD. Adjacent Longhua Martyrs' Cemetery is a peaceful memorial park. Much less visited than Jing'an — the authentic temple experience without the crowds. March–April: peach blossom season turns the temple grounds spectacular.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some steps. Suitable.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tiantian Love Road (Sweet Love Road)
attractions.tip: Called 'Shanghai's most romantic road.' The love letter postbox at the entrance stamps every letter sent with a special 'love' postmark. Couples queue to write notes and mail them. Wall of love poetry murals. Close to Lu Xun Museum and 1933 Old Millfun — good half-day area.
attractions.elderly_note: Short flat road. Accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: West Bund Art Zone
attractions.tip: Shanghai's answer to London's South Bank — contemporary art museums (Long Museum, TANK Shanghai) in converted oil tanks along Huangpu River. Free to walk the riverbank. TANK Shanghai's entrance fee is worth it for temporary exhibitions. Beautiful at dusk with sunset over the river. Metro Line 11: Yunzhong Road Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside paths. Accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: People's Square & Historical Museum
attractions.chinese: 人民广场·上海历史博物馆
attractions.tip: The Historical Museum (上海历史博物馆) inside the old Hong Kong Shanghai Racing Club building is free and excellent. Tells Shanghai's complete story from fishing village to global megacity. People's Square itself has: Lego flagship store, M&M flagship, Madame Tussauds, Shanghai Museum — whole complex is a full day.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat plaza. Excellent for elderly.
attractions.description: The civic heart of Shanghai — a grand public square above the world's busiest metro interchange, flanked by the Shanghai Museum, the Grand Theatre and the Urban Planning Exhibition Hall. The scale model of Shanghai in the Planning Hall showing the entire city in extraordinary detail is unmissable.

attractions.name: Qibao Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Less touristy than Zhujiajiao — smaller but more authentic. Ancient street food along the canal: rice cakes, glutinous rice, smelly tofu, sugar-cane juice. Good weekday morning outing. Metro Line 9 to Qibao Station (20 mins from city center). Better food experience than Zhujiajiao at lower cost.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit experience in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shanghai Greenhouse Garden
attractions.tip: Described as 'more Studio Ghibli than city centre.' Botanical glasshouse where art, tropical plants, and architecture converge. Winding paths, sun-dappled corners, excellent photo spots. Very popular on 小红书 for couple photography. Book ahead — weekends sell out.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat paths within greenhouse. Suitable.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Duolun Road Cultural Street
attractions.tip: 550-meter pedestrian street dedicated to early 20th century Chinese literary figures. Lu Xun's former home and museum nearby. Bronze statues of writers, antique shops, old teahouses. China's literary history made physical. Combine with 1933 Old Millfun and Sweet Love Road for a full Hongkou cultural day.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Suitable.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Oriental Pearl Tower & Riverfront
attractions.tip: 468m tall, iconic pink globe towers. The glass floor viewing platform is the must-do at the 263m Sightseeing Sphere. The Jin Mao Tower 88th floor 'Walk in the Clouds' skywalk is scarier and more unique. The Shanghai History Museum inside Pearl Tower is included in ticket price — actually excellent.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable.
attractions.description: Shanghai's most iconic structure — the pink spheres of the TV tower that defined the Pudong skyline before the supertalls arrived. The glass floor observation deck and the city history museum inside the lower sphere are both worth the visit beyond the famous exterior.

attractions.name: Xinchang Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Called Shanghai's best-kept water town secret — even locals don't all know it. Film location for Ang Lee's 色戒 (Lust, Caution). Traditional teahouses, Old Eight Styles local dishes (老八样), almost no foreign tourists. Metro Line 16 to Xinchang Station. Different feel from Zhujiajiao — quieter, rawer.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat canal streets. Accessible.
attractions.description: A must-visit experience in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Jiahe Food Market
attractions.tip: A 小红书-discovered local wet market that became a pilgrimage for food lovers. 20–30 mins from city center. Specializes in fresh vegetables and unusual ingredients. The market has multiple small eateries attached — eat where you literally shop. Less crowded, completely authentic.
attractions.elderly_note: Busy indoor market. Some uneven surfaces.
attractions.description: A must-visit experience in Shanghai that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yuyuan Old Street
attractions.tip: Visit after 4pm when tour groups thin out, and head to the upper floors of shops for better prices and fewer crowds. The Nanxiang Steamed Bun Restaurant's second floor has shorter queues than ground level.
attractions.description: A labyrinth of Ming and Qing dynasty-style buildings filled with traditional teahouses, snack vendors, and craft shops surrounding the historic Yuyuan Garden. The perfect spot to sample Shanghai street food like soup dumplings while browsing for silk scarves and calligraphy supplies.

attractions.name: Bund Sightseeing Tunnel
attractions.tip: Skip this pricey gimmick and take the pedestrian tunnel or ferry instead for a fraction of the cost—the views are better and you'll save money for actual attractions.
attractions.description: An automated light show tunnel beneath the Huangpu River connects the Bund to Pudong with psychedelic projections and music. It's a quick, quirky way to cross the river that feels like a 1990s sci-fi theme park ride.

attractions.name: Shanghai Tower
attractions.tip: Visit at sunset around 5-6pm to see the city transition from day to night. Book tickets online in advance to skip the ground floor queues, and go on a clear day after rain for the best visibility.
attractions.description: Soar to the 118th floor of China's tallest building, where glass elevators rocket you 632 meters into the clouds in under a minute. The observation deck reveals a breathtaking 360-degree panorama of Shanghai's glittering skyline, the Huangpu River snaking below, and the historic Bund in miniature.

attractions.name: Shanghai Old Street
attractions.tip: Visit the small folk museum midway down the street for free historical context, and haggle firmly at antique shops as prices start inflated for tourists.
attractions.description: This reconstructed Ming and Qing-style street near Yuyuan Garden recreates old Shanghai with traditional pagoda-roofed buildings lining a pedestrian thoroughfare. Browse antique shops, teahouses, and snack vendors selling local treats while soaking in nostalgic architecture.

attractions.name: Oriental Pearl TV Tower
attractions.tip: Skip the main observation decks and head straight to the higher Sky Walk at 259 meters with glass floors for fewer crowds and better photos, especially at sunset when both daylight and evening cityscapes merge.
attractions.description: Shanghai's iconic 468-meter spaceship-like tower dominates the Pudong skyline with its distinctive pink spheres and futuristic silhouette. Ascend through glass elevators to observation decks offering sweeping views across the Huangpu River to the Bund's colonial architecture.

attractions.name: Century Park
attractions.tip: Enter through Gate 7 on the southwest side to rent bicycles cheaply and explore the entire park efficiently. Visit on weekday mornings to avoid weekend crowds and enjoy the tai chi practitioners by the lake.
attractions.description: Shanghai's largest green space sprawls across 140 hectares in the heart of Pudong, with expansive lawns, ornamental gardens, and a massive lake framed by the district's futuristic skyline. The perfect urban escape for picnics, paddle boating, or simply sprawling on the grass away from the city's relentless pace.

attractions.name: Pirates of the Caribbean: Battle for the Sunken Treasure
attractions.chinese: 加勒比海盗-沉落宝藏之战
attractions.tip: Ride during parade or fireworks times when crowds migrate elsewhere. Queue moves fastest mid-afternoon. The boat seats are rows of six, so solo travelers can often skip ahead to fill gaps.
attractions.description: Shanghai Disneyland's groundbreaking boat ride plunges you into Captain Jack Sparrow's world with stunning animatronics, projection mapping, and pulse-pounding naval battles. This technologically advanced attraction sets a new global standard for dark rides with its immersive storytelling and jaw-dropping special effects.

attractions.name: Disneytown
attractions.tip: Head here after 6pm when the park empties out—restaurants have shorter waits and the evening light show over Wishing Star Lake creates a magical backdrop without the daytime crowds.
attractions.description: This pedestrian entertainment district sits just outside Shanghai Disneyland's gates, offering lakeside dining, international brand shopping, and nightly performances at the central plaza. Visit for world-class shows at the Walt Disney Grand Theatre and vibrant street entertainment without needing a theme park ticket.

food.name: Xiao Long Bao (Soup Dumplings)
food.where: Jia Jia Tang Bao 佳家汤包 — Huanghe Road No.90 (local institution, always queues); Nanxiang Mantou Dian 南翔馒头店 in Yu Garden (est. 1900, birthplace of XLB); Lai Lai XLB 莱莱小笼 (viral on 小红书, Michelin-noted)
food.tip: Technique: bite small hole at side → sip soup → eat. The non-oily broth at Jia Jia Tang Bao is the gold standard. Lai Lai XLB's crab roe version (蟹黄小笼包) is the premium option. Nanxiang is the original but more tourist-heavy. 佳家 wins on purity of flavour.

food.name: Sheng Jian Bao (Pan-Fried Pork Buns)
food.where: Yang's Fried Dumplings 小杨生煎 (multiple locations, eat standing up fresh from pan); Da Hu Chun 大壶春 (charcoal pan method — smoky and more traditional)
food.tip: Shanghai's most iconic street breakfast. Crispy golden bottom, juicy pork and soup inside, soft steamed top. Eat immediately — crust softens fast. Locals eat standing outside the shop. The charcoal-pan version at Da Hu Chun has smokier flavor. ¥14.5 for 4 pieces at Da Hu Chun.

food.name: Scallion Oil Noodles
food.where: Any local noodle shop — look for 'congyu ban mian' on menu. Tianzifang area stalls.
food.tip: Brown noodles tossed in rendered scallion oil with soy sauce — looks plain, tastes transformative. The caramelized scallion oil is the key. Pure Shanghai home cooking. One of those dishes that proves perfect simplicity.

food.name: Hong Shao Rou (Red Braised Pork Belly)
food.where: Old Jesse 老吉士 (No.41 Tianping Road, Xuhui — local favourite since forever); Xing'an Restaurant (Xin'an Road, only 8 tables — lines up early)
food.tip: Shanghai's comfort food soul — thick pork belly slow-braised in soy sauce, sugar, Shaoxing wine until melt-in-mouth and glazed dark. Shanghai version is distinctly sweeter than other regions. Eat with plain white rice — the sauce absorbed into rice is the point. Old Jesse does the definitive version.

food.name: Crab Roe Dishes (Seasonal)
food.where: Restaurants throughout Huaihai Road; Xie Huang Yu 蟹黄鱼 (Taicang Road No.200) for yellow croaker noodle soup with crab roe
food.tip: October–December: hairy crab (大闸蟹) season. Everything gets crab roe: XLB, noodles, tofu, pasta, rice. The full steamed hairy crab at Wang Bao He (王宝和) restaurant near People's Square is the traditional experience — order the set to include crab paste dumplings. Non-negotiable if visiting in season.

food.name: You Tiao & Soy Milk Breakfast
food.where: Morning street stalls throughout residential areas near your hotel
food.tip: The most authentic Shanghai morning: fried dough stick (油条) with warm soy milk (豆浆). Locals order salty soy milk (咸豆浆 — with vinegar and spring onion that curdles the soy milk into silky pudding). Find a neighborhood stall, not a franchise. ¥8 for a complete breakfast.

food.name: Charcoal Hotpot (Hidden Gem)
food.where: 1100 Dingxi Road, Changning District — old-school charcoal hotpot. Open Mon–Fri 11am–2pm, 4:30pm–2am; Sat–Sun 11am–2am
food.tip: Soup simmered over real charcoal fire — broth from ¥28. Old Shanghai charm with unbeatable prices. The broths start thin and deepen over the meal. YouTrip SG calls it 'a warm hug but a fraction of Singapore prices.' Very local crowd.

food.name: Jianbing (Savory Breakfast Crepe)
food.where: Morning street stalls, available until 11am only. Near residential areas and near Jia Jia Tang Bao on Huanghe Road.
food.tip: Paper-thin crepe spread on a hot griddle, egg cracked and spread, then filled with crispy crackers, scallions, pickled vegetables, hoisin sauce. The Shandong-style jianbing in Shanghai is different from Beijing's — crisper, lighter. Order with extra 脆饼 (crispy cracker) inside.

food.name: Soufflé Pancake
food.where: Trending cafes along Anfu Road and Wukang Road area
food.tip: Japanese-style jiggly soufflé pancakes are a 小红书 staple in Shanghai — multiple flavors (matcha, original, seasonal). Very photogenic — made for the gram. Best on weekday mornings to avoid 30-min waits. The matcha version at quality cafes is consistently excellent.

food.name: Hogwarts Bakery (Apoli Itabakery)
food.where: Apoli Itabakery — Changning District. Search Gaode for exact location.
food.tip: Arched windows, warm amber lighting, stained glass — wizard-school aesthetic. The croissants and pastries are genuinely excellent (not just Instagram bait). Blew up on 小红书. Located between Tianzifang and French Concession area — perfect afternoon stop.

food.name: Shanghai Drunken Chicken
food.where: Jesse Restaurant 老吉士 (Tianping Road); Fu 1039 (Yuyuan Road, Changning) — upscale version
food.tip: Cold chicken marinated in Shaoxing wine, goji berries, ginger. Served in jelly-set wine broth. Distinctly Shanghai: herbal, alcoholic, refreshing. Perfect summer dish. The wine-infused quality should be powerful — if it tastes mild, it's been cut. Order as starter before heavier dishes.

food.name: Matcha Gelato at Kujuye
food.where: 九十葉 — Anfu Road / French Concession area
food.tip: 5 grades of matcha with gelato and drink options — the difference between the grades is genuinely perceptible. The premium matcha (ceremonial grade) gelato is extraordinary. Went viral on 小红书 and repeatedly cited by Lemon8 SG reviewers. Small queue but worth it.

food.name: Pain Chaud Bakery
food.where: 百丘 Pain Chaud — multiple locations, Paris Baguette-style local franchise
food.tip: Shanghai's beloved local bakery chain — coffee, ice cream, breads, pastries, quiche. The caramel croissant is the signature. Multiple reviewers cite this as better than franchise competitors at lower price. 2025: expanding rapidly, now over 40 locations.

food.name: Songshu Guiyu (Squirrel Fish)
food.where: Fu 1088 (Zhenning Road) — upscale Shanghainese; Zhangshengji (LuJiaZui West Road) for premium version
food.tip: Whole mandarin fish deboned, scored, deep-fried until porcupine-shaped, topped with bright red sweet-and-sour sauce. Visually stunning — the fish fans out like a squirrel tail. Festive dish traditionally for special occasions. The crispy-to-tender texture contrast is exceptional. Order as main sharing dish.

food.name: Pork Ribs & Rice Cake
food.where: Xian De Lai 鲜得来 (Yunnan South Road) — traditional Shanghai specialty restaurant
food.tip: Crispy fried pork ribs served with soft, chewy rice cakes in special sauce — a pairing unique to Shanghai. Xian De Lai has been making this since the 1940s. The rice cake (年糕) absorbs the sauce perfectly. Very cheap, very local, very satisfying.

food.name: Seesaw Coffee (Bund area)
food.must_order: Cold brew, oat milk latte with Bund view
food.tip: Multiple excellent specialty coffee options near the Bund. Seesaw and M on the Bund area cafes have river views. The key is finding a window or terrace seat overlooking the Huangpu River — go early morning before the Bund crowds arrive.

food.name: Jia Jia Tang Bao
food.near: Near People's Square, 10 min from Bund
food.must_order: Xiaolongbao (pork soup dumplings), crab xiaolongbao in season
food.tip: Shanghai's most famous xiaolongbao — always a queue but moves fast. The crab roe version (蟹黄汤包) in autumn is extraordinary. Price is a fraction of Din Tai Fung for the same (or better) quality. Open from 6:30am — perfect pre-Bund breakfast.

food.name: Bar Rouge Rooftop
food.must_order: Classic cocktail with Pudong skyline view
food.tip: The most celebrated rooftop bar on the Bund — 7th floor terrace looking directly at the Pudong skyline. Arrive at sunset (6–7pm) to see the towers light up as day turns to night. Book a terrace spot ahead — it fills fast on weekends.

food.name: 13DE MARZO Bear Cafe (Anfu Road)
food.must_order: Any drink — the bear charm is the point
food.tip: The most viral cafe in Shanghai 2024–2025 — every drink comes with a collectible removable bear charm. Chinese fashion brand turned cafe. Always a queue but moves relatively fast. The tree-lined Anfu Road backdrop makes every photo worth it. Wukang Road is 5 min walk.

food.name: Luneurs Bakery
food.near: Anfu Road / Changshu Road area, French Concession
food.must_order: Croissant, kouign-amann, seasonal tart
food.tip: The best bakery in Shanghai — a genuine rarity. French-trained bakers making exceptional bread and pastries. The croissants are perfectly laminated. Always sells out by 11am on weekends — arrive early. Small queue but worth it. No seating — take your pastry to Fuxing Park 5 min away.

food.name: Tianzifang Alley Food & Cafes
food.must_order: Coffee at any indie cafe, Shanghai scallion pancake from street stalls
food.tip: A maze of narrow alleyways converted from 1930s factory buildings — dozens of indie cafes, restaurants and art studios. More authentic atmosphere than Xintiandi. Look for cafes with rooftop or second-floor terrace seating. Best visited in the morning before crowds.

food.name: Fu 1088
food.near: Yuyuan Road, Jing'an / French Concession border
food.must_order: Red-braised pork (Hong Shao Rou), steamed fish, Lion's Head meatball
food.tip: The best traditional Shanghainese cuisine in the city — in a restored 1930s villa with rooms named after different eras of Shanghai history. The setting is extraordinary. Book ahead. The red-braised pork belly is legendary — rich, gelatinous, perfectly sweet.

food.name: Cloud 9 Bar (Grand Hyatt)
food.near: Grand Hyatt, Jin Mao Tower, Pudong
food.must_order: Signature cocktail with Shanghai Tower view
food.tip: 87th floor bar inside Jin Mao Tower — the atrium view looking straight down through 30 floors of hotel is mind-bending, and the city views are extraordinary. Best at night. Minimum spend applies. Worth every yuan for the view.

food.name: Xiao Long Bao Din Tai Fung
food.near: Multiple locations, closest to Pudong at Super Brand Mall
food.must_order: Pork xiaolongbao, shrimp dumpling, taro dessert
food.tip: The most reliable xiaolongbao experience for first-timers — Michelin one-star consistently, English menu, see-through kitchen. More expensive than local alternatives but the quality is impeccable. The pork and crab roe dumplings in autumn are outstanding.

food.name: M on the Bund
food.tip: Reserve a window table for sunset views over the Huangpu River. The pavlova is exceptional. Dress smart-casual; the seventh-floor terrace bar is perfect for pre-dinner cocktails.

food.name: Lost Heaven Bar
food.tip: Request a table on the second floor for better views of the Bund. Try their signature Yunnan-style cocktails and the mint-infused mojito with local herbs.

food.name: The House of Roosevelt
food.tip: Request a table on the terrace for stunning Bund views. The signature cocktails are excellent. Reservations essential for dinner, especially weekends.

food.name: Manner Coffee
food.tip: Skip the queue by ordering via their WeChat mini-program. Try their signature flat white with oat milk. Morning rush peaks around 8-9am near Bund office buildings.

food.name: RAC Coffee & Bar
food.tip: Visit during sunset hours for stunning Bund views with your coffee. Their signature espresso drinks pair well with light pastries. Quieter on weekday mornings.

food.name: Arabica Shanghai Roastery
food.tip: Visit early morning for the freshest roasted beans and to avoid crowds. Try their signature Spanish latte with latte art. The roastery equipment makes for great photos.

food.name: Baker&Spice Cafe
food.tip: Arrive early on weekends for the freshest pastries, especially the almond croissants. The outdoor terrace offers great Bund-area people-watching during milder months.

food.name: Shanghai French Concession Needle General Yard Former Site
food.chinese: 上海法租界针织总厂旧址
food.tip: Visit during late afternoon to explore the creative boutiques and studios before settling into one of the courtyard restaurants for dinner as the lights come on.

food.name: Le Comptoir De Pierre Gagnaire
food.tip: Book ahead for the chef's tasting menu featuring innovative French techniques with Asian influences. Lunch sets offer better value than dinner service.

food.name: La Creperie
food.tip: Order the savory galettes made with buckwheat flour for an authentic Breton experience. Weekday lunches are quieter than weekends in this cozy French Concession spot.

food.name: French Concession
food.tip: Visit during weekday mornings to avoid crowds and secure a window seat. Their signature flat white and homemade pastries are highly recommended by regulars.

food.name: Baker & Spice
food.tip: Arrive early on weekends for the best selection of sourdough loaves and pastries. The almond croissants and quiches sell out quickly by mid-morning.

food.name: Ops Cafe
food.tip: Popular French Concession neighborhood spot ideal for afternoon coffee breaks. Arrives early on weekends to snag a window seat for prime people-watching along the tree-lined streets.

food.name: Laomai Coffee
food.tip: Visit during weekday mornings to avoid the brunch crowd. Their hand-drip coffee and homemade pastries are local favorites. Cozy space fills up quickly on weekends.

food.name: Café del Volcán
food.tip: Prime spot for Lujiazui skyline views. Visit during golden hour for stunning photos of the Oriental Pearl Tower. Their specialty coffee blends are worth trying.

food.name: Mietal Hands Shanghai Branch
food.tip: Visit during off-peak afternoon hours for a quieter atmosphere with views of the Pudong skyline. Their specialty coffee and handcrafted desserts are customer favorites.

food.name: Paradise Garden Restaurant
food.tip: Request a table near the garden area for a more pleasant dining atmosphere. Popular with business lunches, so arrive early during weekday noon hours to avoid waits.

food.name: Resume Coffee
food.tip: Popular with Pudong finance professionals during morning hours. Try their signature hand-drip coffee and arrive before 10am on weekdays to snag a quiet corner seat.

food.name: Tavola
food.tip: Book ahead for weekend dinners as this Italian spot fills quickly. Their handmade pasta dishes are the standout—try the truffle tagliatelle if available.

food.name: Fat Cow
food.tip: Prime steaks and quality beef cuts are the specialty here. Book ahead on weekends as it's popular with Disney visitors staying nearby.

food.name: Peet's Coffee
food.tip: Try their signature dark roast blends for a bolder alternative to typical Shanghai cafes. Weekday mornings are quieter. Their iced drinks can be overly sweet, so request less syrup.

practical.payment: Alipay and WeChat Pay everywhere. Link YouTrip, Wise, or Trust Bank card for best exchange rates. ¥200 cash for taxi tips and small stalls.
practical.transport: Metro is the best way to get around — comprehensive, bilingual, efficient. DiDi for taxis. AMap (高德地图) for navigation.
practical.connectivity: Airalo eSIM before flying. VPN essential — install before leaving Singapore. China Unicom or China Mobile SIM available at Pudong Airport.
practical.language: Most tourist areas have English signage. More English-friendly than Guangzhou and Shenzhen.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping for restaurant vouchers and discovery. Klook for Disney, Pearl Tower. WeChat mini-programmes for museum bookings. Meituan for food delivery.
practical.book_ahead: Shanghai Disney — 1 week ahead minimum, especially weekends. Shanghai Museum (People's Square branch) — WeChat advance booking. Shanghai Greenhouse Garden — book online. Zhujiajiao — weekday strongly recommended.
practical.best_time: March–May (spring) and September–November (autumn). November is peak from SG — beautiful plane tree foliage on Wukang Road and French Concession. December–February: cold but cheaper and less crowded.
practical.day_trips: Suzhou (苏州, 25 min by high-speed rail) — classical gardens, silk market. Hangzhou (杭州, 45 min) — West Lake. Both are easy day trips from Shanghai.
practical.transport_tip: 24-hour public transport card available at Metro customer service — convenient for multi-day stays.

shanghai.recommended_base_reason: French Concession is central with great food and nightlife

kids_attractions.name: Shanghai Disneyland
kids_attractions.tip: The best Disney park in Asia — 14.7M visitors in 2024, a record. The world's largest Disney castle. Book 'Lightning Lane' (fast pass) immediately on entering. Zootopia Land (opened 2023) is the hottest new attraction — queue for 'Zootopia: Hot Pursuit' first thing. Toy Story Hotel on-site is excellent value. Arrive at park opening (9am) and go straight to the most popular ride. Avoid all Chinese public holidays.

kids_attractions.name: LEGOLAND Shanghai Resort
kids_attractions.tip: World's largest LEGOLAND — opened July 2025. Perfect for ages 2–12. Eight themed lands: Harry Potter rival is the Monkie Kid zone (Sun Wukong Chinese mythology). The LEGO water-town boat tour through miniature Chinese cities is unique. Far less crowded than Disney — shorter queues. 1hr from central Shanghai by train from Shanghai South Station to Jinshan North Station. LEGOLAND Hotel has themed rooms (Castle, NINJAGO, FRIENDS).

kids_attractions.name: Shanghai Ocean Aquarium
kids_attractions.tip: Asia's largest aquarium — 155m underwater tunnel, 350+ species. Shark feeding at 11am and 2pm is the highlight. The Antarctic penguin habitat is excellent. Located next to the Oriental Pearl Tower — combine both in one morning. Kids under 1.2m enter free.

kids_attractions.name: Shanghai Science and Technology Museum
kids_attractions.tip: One of China's best interactive science museums — robots, spacecraft, virtual reality and a working earthquake simulator. The IMAX dome shows 40-minute nature documentaries (¥30 extra). The Children's Science Land section (ages 3–10) is outstanding — hands-on experiments with bubbles, light and machines. Very popular on weekends.

kids_attractions.name: Happy Valley Shanghai
kids_attractions.tip: For older kids (8+) who want roller coasters and thrill rides. Different experience from Disneyland/Legoland — more Chinese-style excitement. The Screaming Eagle wooden coaster is legendary. Far cheaper than Disney. On weekdays outside holidays you can ride everything twice in one day.

hotels.name: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund
hotels.chinese: 上海外滩华尔道夫酒店
hotels.tip: The most iconic address in Shanghai — 1920s Art Deco building on the Bund with Huangpu River views. The Long Bar (Shanghai's oldest bar) and Bund-facing rooms are extraordinary. Walking distance to Yu Garden and Nanjing Road. Premier choice for first-time visitors to Shanghai. Book river-view rooms.

hotels.name: Pudong Shangri-La Shanghai
hotels.chinese: 上海浦东香格里拉大酒店
hotels.tip: Best Pudong option — riverside location with sweeping views of the Bund from guest rooms, the opposite perspective from Waldorf Astoria. 3-min walk to Oriental Pearl Tower and Shanghai Tower. The Rose Veranda for afternoon tea is excellent. Great for families in Pudong.

hotels.name: The PuLi Hotel and Spa
hotels.tip: Boutique urban resort in Jing'an — extraordinary spa, beautiful pool and intimate atmosphere. Condé Nast Gold List consistently. Quieter than Bund hotels but excellent metro access (Lines 2 & 7). Right next to Jing'an Park. The place locals recommend to visiting friends for a special stay. Often better value than larger luxury hotels.

hotels.name: Okura Garden Hotel Shanghai
hotels.tip: A hidden gem — Japanese-managed hotel with legendary service consistency, set in a beautifully maintained French Concession garden. Much quieter than CBD hotels. The garden and pool area are a genuine oasis in the city. Walk to Xintiandi, Tianzifang and the best French Concession restaurants. Excellent value for location quality.

hotels.name: Jinjiang Inn Shanghai Nanjing Road
hotels.chinese: 锦江之星上海南京路店
hotels.tip: Best budget option in central Shanghai — Nanjing Road location means everything is walkable: Bund (10 min), People's Square (5 min), Yu Garden (20 min). Rooms are small but clean and well-maintained. Reliable Chinese chain with English-friendly check-in. Book well ahead as it fills fast at this price point in this location.

hotels.name: Capella Shanghai Jian Ye Li
hotels.chinese: 上海建业里嘉佩乐酒店
hotels.tip: Ultra-boutique in restored 1930s shikumen townhouses — private courtyards, garden paths, complete silence in the city. Condé Nast Top Hotel consistently. Only 55 rooms. The most unique address in Shanghai for those who want exclusivity over skyline views.

hotels.name: The Middle House Shanghai
hotels.tip: Acclaimed design-led luxury in Jing'an — beautiful pool, excellent restaurant, artistic interiors inspired by Chinese ink painting. Walk to West Nanjing Road, Shanghai Exhibition Centre, French Concession. Very popular with design-conscious travellers.

hotels.name: HUALUXE Shanghai Twelve at Hengshan
hotels.chinese: 上海衡山十二于上酒店
hotels.tip: IHG's Chinese luxury brand right on leafy Hengshan Road — the best mid-range in the French Concession. Private balconies in many rooms. Walk to Xintiandi, Tianzifang and the best restaurant streets. More affordable than nearby 5-stars with similar location quality.

hotels.name: Ibis Shanghai Nanjing Road
hotels.chinese: 上海南京东路宜必思酒店
hotels.tip: Cheapest reliably good budget option in central Shanghai. Accor brand standards — always clean, decent staff. The Nanjing Road/People's Square location is exceptional for the price. Walk to Bund, Yu Garden, Shanghai Museum. Book far ahead as it fills quickly.

hotels.name: Kerry Hotel Pudong Shanghai
hotels.tip: Best mid-range 5-star for families in Pudong — enormous breakfast spread, excellent pool, outstanding kids' facilities. Connected to Kerry Parkside mall. Close to Expo Centre and Century Park. Slightly removed from Old Town but good value for the quality.

chongqing.name: Chongqing

attractions.name: Hongyadong
attractions.tip: The real-life Spirited Away. Go after 7pm when the lights come on. Best photos from Qiansi Gate Bridge (千厮门大桥) across the river — exit Line 6 at Dajuyuan, walk to Bao Ding Gate stairs down. Bridge lights: 19:00–21:30.
attractions.description: A cluster of 11-storey stilted buildings clinging to the Jialing River cliff — the night view from the opposite bank with all its lights reflected in the river below is one of China's most photographed images. Traditional Tujia architecture translated into a dramatic riverside spectacle.

attractions.name: Jiefangbei CBD
attractions.tip: Chongqing's Times Square. The liberation monument dates to 1945. Skip the tourist-trap Haochi Street nearby — locals eat at Guanyin Bridge (观音桥好吃街) or Huayuan Night Market instead.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Ciqikou Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Nearly 1,000 years of history. Go before 9am to beat tour groups. Buy the Chen twisted doughnuts (陈麻花) fresh from the fryer. The riverside end has the best views — follow the alley all the way down.
attractions.description: A remarkably preserved Ming dynasty river port town — stone cobbled streets lined with teahouses, opera houses and vendors selling Chongqing's most famous snacks. Arrive before 9am to see old Chongqing before the crowds arrive.

attractions.name: Liziba Monorail Station
attractions.tip: The monorail that passes THROUGH a residential building on floors 6–8. Best viewed from the observation platform below. Trains pass every 2–3 minutes. Take Line 2 to Liziba station. Completely free — just stand and watch.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Eling Erchang (Old Factory Park)
attractions.tip: Former 1940s printing factory turned film set. Filming location for 'From Your Window'. The red umbrella mural wall is the most photographed spot. Combined with nearby Liziba — walk downhill from here to the monorail station.
attractions.description: A must-visit arts-district in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yangtze River Cable Car
attractions.tip: Cross the Yangtze in a cable car with panoramic mountain city views. Buy return tickets (单程+回程). Evening rides are most cinematic. Long queues on weekends — aim for weekday afternoons.
attractions.description: One of the last remaining urban river cable cars in China, offering a spectacular 5-minute suspended journey above one of the world's mightiest rivers with Chongqing's mountain skyline on both sides. Most memorable for the sheer unexpected drama of it.

attractions.name: Nanshan One Tree Viewpoint
attractions.tip: Best panoramic night view of the whole city — 360° lights. Take a taxi (¥30 from city centre) as buses are infrequent. Arrive 30 mins before sunset for golden hour, stay for the 8pm light show. The newer 壹华里 park below is free and less crowded.
attractions.description: A must-visit viewpoint in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Baixiang Residence
attractions.tip: Layered residential building from the film 'Better Days' (少年的你) — also the best spot to photograph the cable car. Go to building No.3 platform for cable car shots. Residents still live here, be respectful and quiet.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Chongqing Rongchang District Changyuan St Dao White Elephant Community Juwu Supervision Committee

attractions.name: Daijiaxiang Alley
attractions.tip: 600m hillside alley with cascading city views. Best in the evening when lights come on. The stairs at the bottom lead to the river — wonderful for golden hour photos. Very local atmosphere, far fewer tourists than Hongyadong.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Three Gorges Museum
attractions.tip: World-class free museum covering Chongqing and Three Gorges history. The flooded city diorama showing submerged towns is hauntingly beautiful. Collect all 60+ stamps in a notebook — a beloved local tradition. Book via WeChat. Closed Mondays.
attractions.description: The finest museum covering the history of the Yangtze River civilisation — displaying 170,000 artefacts recovered from the Three Gorges before the dam flooded the valleys. Free entry.

attractions.name: Huguang Guild Hall
attractions.tip: China's largest surviving Ming-Qing guild hall complex — built by wealthy Hubei merchants in the 1700s. The yellow walls photograph beautifully from every angle. 300+ intricate wood carvings inside. Daily afternoon Sichuan opera performance included.
attractions.description: The most perfectly preserved Ming dynasty guild hall in China — a complex of theatres, shrines and courtyards built by migrants from Hunan and Hubei in the 18th century, clinging to the cliff above the Yangtze. The Sichuan opera performances held in the original theatre space are extraordinary.
attractions.photo_query: huguang guild hall chongqing historic theatre

attractions.name: Shancheng Stepway (Shan Cheng Bu Dao)
attractions.tip: Chongqing's most iconic stepped walkway — string of historical sites, suspended walkways with river views, indie cafes and art studios. Walk the Shanyi section (山城巷): Kangjiandang Theatre, ancient city wall, Buddha Tower. Best late afternoon.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Wulong Tiankeng (Natural Bridges)
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage Site — world's largest natural stone bridge group. Filming location for Zhang Yimou's 'Curse of the Golden Flower'. 2hrs from city by train. Book the earliest time slot. Take the electric car inside (¥15) to save legs for the walk.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: 18 Steps (Shiba Ti)
attractions.tip: Ancient stepped lanes connecting upper and lower Chongqing — renovated in 2021. The city terrace at the top gives a great panoramic view. Starbucks inside a historic gatehouse at the top. Good photo opportunity from the 较场口 metro exit.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Jingangbei Ancient Village
attractions.tip: A genuinely hidden ancient village beneath Jinyun Mountain. Preserved Ming/Qing buildings along a stream. Locals say this is how Chongqing looked 100 years ago. Weekdays are very quiet. About 40km from city centre — worth the taxi ride out.
attractions.description: A must-visit hidden-gem in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Luozhonglizhuzao Qianxi Gate Bridge Viewpoint
attractions.tip: THE spot to photograph Hongyadong from below — glittering reflections in the Jialing River. Take Line 6 to Dajuyuan station, navigate to Bao Ding Gate (保定门) stairs down to river level. Bridge lights on 19:00–21:30 (weekends to 22:30). Free.
attractions.description: A must-visit viewpoint in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hua Yuan Night Market
attractions.tip: Where locals actually eat — not the tourist Haochi Street. Open from 5pm. Skewers, grilled fish, cold noodles, cold skin noodles (凉皮), stinky tofu. Very affordable (¥20–40pp). The energy here at 9pm on a Friday is pure Chongqing.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: S. Gard. Shiji Spec. Night Market

attractions.name: Huangque Pinggu Old Street
attractions.tip: The Chongqing Academy of Fine Arts (川美) neighbourhood — entire street buildings covered in murals painted by students and artists. The Traffic Teahouse (交通茶馆) is one of the last original 1970s-style teahouses in China — ¥10 bottomless tea.
attractions.description: A must-visit arts-district in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: People's Liberation Monument + Underground Mall
attractions.tip: Largest underground shopping mall in Asia beneath Jiefangbei. 10km of passages, 1,000+ shops. Air-conditioned escape in summer heat. Locals shop here; far cheaper than above-ground stores. The direct underground connection to Raffles City is remarkable.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Chongqing People's Hall
attractions.tip: Modelled on the Temple of Heaven in Beijing, this 1954 civic hall is magnificent. Free to enter the grounds. The view from the steps looking down to the Three Gorges Museum creates a perfect symmetrical photo. Combine with the museum next door.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Two Rivers Ferry (Liang Jiang Xiao Du)
attractions.tip: The hidden-gem experience in Chongqing — a ¥10 local ferry from Chaotianmen to Danzi Shi. On the water, surrounded by the dramatic city skyline. Locals commute on this. Operating hours 6am–10pm. The 10-minute ride gives the best city views.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Chongqing Liangjiang Sightseeing Boat Ticket Office

attractions.name: Sanxia Guangchang (Times Square View)
attractions.tip: The sky bridge connecting the twin Raffles City towers at 250m — best city view from a building. The glass floor section is terrifying and wonderful. Book online. The free lobby view below the bridge is pretty good too if you skip the ticket.
attractions.description: A must-visit viewpoint in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zhazi Cave (Red Rock Site)
attractions.tip: Former KMT prison from the 1940s Civil War — historically significant, sobering experience. Based on the novel 'Red Rock'. Free entry. Combined with White Mansion (白公馆) nearby. Take T198 bus from Ciqikou. Closed Mondays.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Wansheng Black Valley
attractions.tip: Spectacular karst canyon 2hrs from city — glass walkways, waterfalls, ancient forests. The 'swinging glass bridge' is terrifying in the best way. Avoid summer and Golden Week holidays (200k visitors/day). Book early time slot.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Chongqing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Dazu Rock Carvings
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage Site — 75,000 Buddhist, Taoist and Confucian sculptures carved into cliffs from the 9th–13th century. Baoding Shan section is the main highlight. 1.5hrs from city by high-speed train. An often-overlooked world treasure.
attractions.description: UNESCO-listed Buddhist, Taoist and Confucian carvings carved into cliffsides over 1,000 years — 50,000 stone sculptures in 75 sites around Dazu County. The Baodingshan group is the finest outdoor sculpture complex in China, depicting scenes from Chinese cosmology with extraordinary delicacy.

attractions.name: Hongyadong (Northwest Gate)
attractions.tip: Visit after dark for the spectacular illuminated views, but arrive before 7pm to avoid peak crowds. Enter from the top level and work your way down to fully appreciate the unique stilted structure.
attractions.description: Eleven stories of traditional stilted wooden buildings cascade down the cliffside, glowing with golden lanterns at night like a scene from an animated fantasy. This architectural wonder blends Bayu heritage architecture with modern restaurants, teahouses, and craft shops overlooking the Jialing River.

attractions.name: Hongyadong Folk Custom Scene Area
attractions.tip: View from across the river at Qiansi Gate Bridge for the best photos between 6-10pm when illuminated. Avoid weekends when crowds make navigating the narrow alleys difficult.
attractions.description: This stilt-house complex cascades down a cliff face along the Jialing River, glowing with golden lights that inspired the bathhouse in Spirited Away. Visit after dark when the 11-story traditional architecture transforms into Chongqing's most photographed spectacle.

attractions.name: Eling Park
attractions.tip: Visit at sunset when the city lights begin twinkling, then head to the traditional teahouse near the entrance where locals play mahjong—a perfect slice of authentic Chongqing life.
attractions.description: Perched on Chongqing's highest hill, this elegant park offers sweeping panoramas of where the Yangtze and Jialing rivers converge. Come for the iconic Liangjiang Pavilion viewpoint and tranquil teahouse tucked among bamboo groves.

attractions.name: Huangge Ancient Road
attractions.tip: Start from the Nanshan side early morning to walk downhill and avoid the steep climb, stopping at the small noodle shops where locals eat breakfast for authentic Chongqing flavors at half the tourist area prices.
attractions.description: This centuries-old stone path winds through bamboo groves and weathered steps connecting old Chongqing neighborhoods along the mountainside. The atmospheric trail offers a glimpse into traditional life with locals still using it daily, flanked by teahouses and crumbling stone architecture.

attractions.name: Chongqing Zoo
attractions.tip: Visit the panda pavilion right when gates open at 8:30am before tour groups arrive—pandas are most active during morning feeding time and you'll have better photo opportunities without crowds.
attractions.description: This sprawling zoo in the heart of Chongqing has been home to giant pandas and rare wildlife for over six decades. Come for the chance to see playful pandas up close alongside Yangtze alligators, South China tigers, and hundreds of other species in naturalistic enclosures.

attractions.name: Three Natural Bridges
attractions.tip: Start your visit early morning to avoid crowds and catch the ethereal mist rising through the gorge. Wear comfortable shoes with good grip as the descent involves steep stone staircases with over 200 steps down into the valley.
attractions.description: Three colossal natural limestone bridges tower over the Yangshui River gorge, creating a dramatic landscape where mist-shrouded karst formations rise hundreds of meters high. This UNESCO World Heritage site offers a rare chance to walk beneath ancient stone arches carved by nature over millions of years.

attractions.name: Longshuixia Difeng
attractions.tip: Wear non-slip shoes as the walkways get wet from waterfall spray. Visit after rainfall when waterfalls are most impressive, but avoid going during heavy rain when the gorge may close for safety.
attractions.description: A dramatic natural gorge carved deep into limestone cliffs, where vertical rock walls tower above a rushing stream. Walk along suspended platforms and bridges through this geological wonder, experiencing the raw power of water erosion over millennia.

attractions.name: Wulong Karst Tourist Area
attractions.tip: Visit Three Natural Bridges early morning on weekdays to avoid tour groups, and wear non-slip shoes as paths near waterfalls get slippery. The Furong Cave section requires separate tickets, so plan accordingly.
attractions.description: This UNESCO World Heritage site showcases dramatic limestone formations including soaring natural rock bridges, hidden caves, and thundering waterfalls carved over millions of years. The otherworldly landscape offers riverside trails through sheer gorges that reveal nature's spectacular architectural artistry.

attractions.name: Chongqing Chaotianmen Square
attractions.tip: Visit the viewing platform on the upper deck around 6-7pm for the best vantage point of the two rivers converging, especially after rain when the color contrast is most dramatic.
attractions.description: This dramatic riverside plaza sits at the confluence where the Yangtze and Jialing rivers merge, their contrasting brown and green waters meeting in a natural spectacle. Come at dusk to watch the river cruise ships depart under the illuminated modern architecture that frames this historic gateway to old Chongqing.

food.name: Chongqing Hotpot (Old Style)
food.where: Zhuyi Road (诸一路) area or Nanbin Road riverside
food.tip: Order the 九宫格 (9-grid) pot. Must-try: 毛肚 (tripe), 鸭肠 (duck intestines), 黄喉 (pork throat). Real Chongqing hotpot uses beef tallow (牛油) base — heavier and more flavourful. Dip in sesame oil + garlic. One of the essential China food experiences.

food.name: Chongqing Xiaomian (Spicy Noodles)
food.where: Any small local shop — avoid places near tourist spots
food.tip: The ultimate Chongqing breakfast. Bowl of spicy noodles with 7+ condiments for under ¥15. Order 干溜 (no soup) for the richest version. The city's unofficial 'Michelin Guide' is the annual Top 50 Xiaomian list — any shop on that list is exceptional.

food.name: Suan La Fen (Sour Spicy Noodles)
food.where: Jiefangbei Haochi Street or Guanyin Bridge area
food.tip: Sweet potato glass noodles in sour-spicy broth, topped with peanuts, yellow beans and coriander. One of the most satisfying cheap eats in China. Look for shops with a line — the handmade noodle version is superior to the reconstituted type.

food.name: Mao Xue Wang
food.where: Jianghu Cai restaurants in Shapingba or Jiangbei
food.tip: Spicy broth loaded with duck blood (not pork), tripe, bean sprouts and glass noodles — a Chongqing jianghu classic. Perfect shared dish. Pair with steamed rice to balance the heat. Not for the timid but deeply satisfying.

food.name: Chen Mahu a (Ciqikou Twisted Doughnuts)
food.where: Ciqikou Ancient Town main street
food.tip: Crispy sesame-coated twisted doughnuts — signature snack of Ciqikou since the Qing dynasty. Three flavours: original, spicy, sweet. Buy freshly made — they go stale within hours. Great edible souvenir.

food.name: Kao Naohua (Grilled Brain)
food.where: Night market stalls near Hongyadong or Jiefangbei
food.tip: Pig brain grilled in tin foil with chilli, garlic and spring onion. Silky, creamy texture like firm tofu. A true Chongqing specialty you won't find elsewhere. The adventurous eater's badge of honour in this city.

food.name: Wanzhou Grilled Fish
food.where: Wanzhou Grilled Fish restaurants citywide
food.tip: Whole fish grilled then sizzled on a hotplate with toppings. Order the spicy bean curd (麻辣豆腐) version. Keep the heat on low — sauce gets better as it reduces. Very shareable, one fish serves 2–3 people.

food.name: Douhua Rice
food.where: Small local restaurants across the city
food.tip: Freshly-made soft tofu served with a dipping sauce of chilli oil, sesame paste and soy, plus steamed rice. Under ¥20/person. A simple but deeply satisfying Chongqing meal. The best douhua is jiggly and silky — never watery.

food.name: Jiefangbei Rooftop Cafes
food.must_order: Coffee with Yangtze River view
food.tip: Several rooftop cafes near Jiefangbei offer views of the Yangtze and Jialing Rivers merging. At night the entire peninsula lights up spectacularly — Chongqing has China's best city-at-night photography. The confluence of two rivers from the rooftop is a signature Chongqing shot.

food.name: Hongyadong Hot Pot
food.must_order: Chongqing numbing-spicy hotpot — pork slices, tripe, brain, mochi
food.tip: Chongqing is the hotpot capital of China — more intense, more numbing (麻), more spicy than anywhere else. The area around Hongyadong has dozens of good hotpot restaurants. Tell the server 'zhong la' (medium spicy) unless you have a high spice tolerance. Order brain if adventurous — it's a Chongqing delicacy.

food.name: Ciqikou Ancient Town Tea Houses
food.must_order: Gaiwan tea, dan dan noodles, mochi
food.tip: Traditional teahouses in the ancient Ming dynasty town — sit on wooden chairs in a riverside pavilion drinking gaiwan tea while watching the river. Much more authentic than tourist spots. The small streets behind the main tourist drag have the best local food. Try the handmade mochi and dan dan noodles.

food.name: Yangtze River Night View Restaurants
food.must_order: Sichuan cuisine with river view, night photography perch
food.tip: Nanbin Road is the best place to photograph Chongqing's famous night skyline. Dozens of restaurants line the south bank of the Yangtze with unobstructed views of the illuminated Yuzhong Peninsula. Arrive at sunset (6:30pm). The light show begins around 8pm. Bring a tripod or lean on railings for long-exposure shots.

food.name: Small Noodle (Xiaomian) Street
food.must_order: Chongqing xiao mian — ¥8–12 per bowl
food.tip: Chongqing's signature street food — thick wheat noodles in a complex spicy sauce with peanuts, sesame paste, Sichuan pepper and preserved vegetables. ¥8–12 per bowl from any local shop with plastic stools. The definitive cheap local breakfast. Every neighbourhood has its best xiaomian shop — ask hotel staff which one is theirs.

food.name: Chunyanglao Wine House
food.tip: Traditional Chongqing wine house specializing in local baijiu and classic Sichuan drinking snacks. Visit in the evening for authentic atmosphere and try their house-infused spirits with spiced peanuts.

food.name: Kanshang Restaurant
food.tip: Request a window table for stunning Yangtze River views. Try their signature Chongqing hot pot or spicy grilled fish. Visit during sunset for the best atmosphere.

food.name: Ruoshui Coffee
food.tip: Perfect hideaway in bustling Yuzhong Peninsula. Ask for their pour-over coffee—baristas take time to explain bean origins. Visit during weekday afternoons for a quieter atmosphere.

food.name: Luckin Coffee
food.tip: Download the Luckin app for significant discounts and promotions. The raw coconut latte is a customer favorite. Peak hours are 8-10am, so order ahead to skip queues.

food.name: Diwang Shangdao Cafe
food.tip: Try their Hainanese-style coffee and traditional pineapple buns. Located in the bustling Yuzhong Peninsula area, perfect for a break between sightseeing stops.

food.name: Xiewu Coffee
food.tip: A hidden specialty coffee spot in Yuzhong Peninsula. Ask the barista for pour-over recommendations—they source quality beans and know their craft well.

food.name: Secret Garden
food.tip: Visit during late afternoon for the best natural lighting. Ask for their signature house blend coffee and try pairing it with their homemade desserts.

food.name: Jinyaoxuan
food.tip: Arrive early during peak hours as this highly-rated local spot fills quickly. Ask staff for their daily specials showcasing Chongqing's signature flavors.

food.name: Luhe Junfeng Restaurant
food.tip: A local favorite in Jiulongpo South area. Call ahead to confirm operating hours and specialties, as this neighborhood spot may have limited English service.

food.name: Lulu Maojiang Coffee Shop
food.tip: Try their signature hand-dripped coffee for the best experience. The cozy atmosphere makes it perfect for afternoon visits when it's less crowded.

food.name: The Corner Cafe & Bistro
food.tip: Perfect spot for a quiet coffee break away from Chongqing's bustling streets. Try their signature lattes and pastries during off-peak afternoon hours.

food.name: Baihuo Cafe
food.tip: A hidden gem in Jiulongpo's southern district. Perfect for a quiet afternoon coffee break away from the tourist crowds. Try their specialty hand-drip coffee.

food.name: Lingling Restaurant
food.tip: As a highly-rated local spot in Chongqing, arrive early for lunch to avoid peak crowds. Ask staff for their spiciness recommendations if you're not accustomed to authentic Chongqing heat.

food.name: The Moment
food.tip: Visit during late afternoon for quieter ambiance and better seating options. Try their signature latte paired with local Chongqing desserts for a unique fusion experience.

food.name: Hongya Cave Dawan Tea
food.tip: Visit during sunset for stunning Jialing River views while sipping traditional bowl tea. Try their signature da wan cha (big bowl tea) served the old Chongqing way.

food.name: Ugly Monkey Cafe
food.tip: Popular with local creatives in Jiangbei's quieter north area. Try their specialty coffee drinks and enjoy the laid-back atmosphere away from tourist crowds.

hotels.name: Chongqing W Hotel
hotels.tip: Marriott's W brand in Chongqing — dramatic design, rooftop bar with Yangtze River and skyline views. Young and energetic vibe, great for couples and design lovers. Walk to Hongyadong and Jiefangbei. Popular for special occasions.

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Chongqing
hotels.tip: Reliable Hyatt luxury in Yuzhong — 5-min walk to Jiefangbei and Hongyadong. Very good Cantonese and Sichuan restaurants on-site. Pool and spa. Consistently praised for attentive service and English-friendly staff.

hotels.name: Marriott Executive Apartments Chongqing
hotels.tip: Spacious apartment-style rooms with kitchenettes — ideal for families or longer stays. Marriott reliability. Walk to all Yuzhong sights. Good value for the apartment facilities compared to standard hotel rooms at similar price.

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Chongqing Yangtze
hotels.tip: IHG mid-range with some Yangtze River views — good breakfast, reliable service. Walk to Hongyadong and Liberation Monument. Solid choice for travellers who want IHG reliability without luxury prices. Family rooms available.

hotels.name: Ibis Chongqing Jiefangbei
hotels.chinese: 重庆解放碑宜必思酒店
hotels.tip: Right at the Liberation Monument — the most central budget hotel in Chongqing. Walk to Hongyadong, Ciqikou and all Yuzhong sights. Accor Ibis clean and reliable. Small rooms but staff generally helpful. Book ahead as it fills fast.

chongqing.recommended_base_reason: Yuzhong Peninsula is the dense old city core — Hongya Cave and hilltop views walkable

kids_attractions.name: Chongqing Zoo
kids_attractions.tip: One of the best zoos in inland China — giant pandas, Yangtze alligators, South China tigers and golden monkeys. The panda section is excellent. Much cheaper and less crowded than Chengdu's base. Kids under 1.2m enter free. Best visited in the morning before the heat. The monorail running through the zoo area is a Chongqing quirk.

kids_attractions.name: Chongqing Indoor Playgrounds (Jurassic Kingdom)
kids_attractions.tip: Chongqing is famous among expat families for having the best indoor playgrounds in China. Jurassic Kingdom in the Jiangbei mall area is the most popular — life-size dinosaur models, indoor climbing, ball pits and interactive games. Perfect for under-10s on rainy days. Multiple indoor play venues in the same mall area.

chengdu.name: Chengdu
chengdu.tagline: Slow Down. Eat More. See Pandas.

attractions.name: Giant Panda Research Base
attractions.tip: Go at 8am opening — pandas most active before 10am. Baby pandas are in the nursery (月亮产房). Book via WeChat app or official website — walk-ins turned away on weekends and holidays. The red panda section is often overlooked but equally adorable.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding

attractions.name: Wuhou Shrine + Jinli Street
attractions.tip: 1,800 years of Three Kingdoms history. Go early — the red wall bamboo corridor at 7am is extraordinary. Exit into Jinli Street for street food but skip the main drag and head to the back lanes (锦里后街) for three-cannon sticky rice (三大炮) and Zhang Fei beef.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-site in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Kuanzhai Alley (Wide & Narrow Alleys)
attractions.tip: Three parallel Qing dynasty alleyways. Go before 9am for peaceful atmosphere. Ignore the main street tourist shops — duck into Well Lane (井巷子) for local snacks and the preserved old city wall section. The side gate off Kuan alley has the best old building views.
attractions.description: Three parallel Qing dynasty alleyways where traditional Sichuan courtyard architecture houses the best concentration of specialty coffee and innovative cuisine in Chengdu. The % Arabica branch here — a former siheyuan courtyard converted by a Japanese design studio — is the most photographed cafe interior in the city.

attractions.name: People's Park & Heming Teahouse
attractions.chinese: 人民公园 & 鹤鸣茶社
attractions.tip: The soul of Chengdu. Sit in a bamboo chair at Heming Teahouse (¥16 for bottomless gaiwan tea), watch elderly locals play cards and fan dance. Get an ear cleaning (采耳, ¥30) — a Chengdu-only experience. The matchmaking corner (相亲角) nearby is fascinating to observe.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Taikoo Li + IFS Climbing Panda
attractions.tip: The giant panda climbing IFS mall is Chengdu's most viral landmark. Best photo angle: stand on street level looking up. Taikoo Li opposite is the most beautiful mall in China — traditional Sichuan architecture wrapping luxury brands. Best visited after 7pm when lit up.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding

attractions.name: Dufu Thatched Cottage
attractions.tip: Former home of Tang dynasty poet Du Fu. The bamboo groves and pavilions feel genuinely ancient. The 'Poet's Plum Blossom Trail' outside the paid zone is free and lovely. Visit in rain for the most poetic atmosphere — exactly the setting Du Fu described.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: East District Memory (Dong Jiao Ji Yi)
attractions.tip: Former Soviet-era electronics factory turned music and art district. The 'Chengdu' wall mural is the most viral photo spot. Live concerts most weekends. The Time Post Box (时光邮局) lets you mail a letter to yourself in 5 years — beautiful concept.
attractions.description: A must-visit arts-district in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Jinsha Site Museum
attractions.tip: 3,000-year-old Shu Kingdom site with extraordinary gold and jade artefacts — including the Sun and Immortal Birds gold foil, now China's cultural heritage symbol. Far less crowded than Wuhou Shrine. Book the joint ticket with Sanxingdui for a discount.
attractions.description: A 3,000-year-old Bronze Age site discovered in 2001 — the Jinsha culture produced extraordinary gold, bronze and jade artefacts including the iconic Sun and Immortal Birds gold foil, now China's cultural heritage emblem. The museum is built directly over the active excavation site.
attractions.photo_query: jinsha museum chengdu bronze age gold

attractions.name: Sanxingdui Museum
attractions.tip: The most mysterious archaeological site in China — 3,000-year-old bronze masks with huge protruding eyes found in 1929, origin still unknown. New museum opened 2023. 1.5hrs from Chengdu. The bronze 'tree of life' is truly unlike anything else. Book weeks ahead in high season.
attractions.description: The most mysterious archaeological site in China — a Bronze Age civilisation 3,000 years old with no connection to any known Chinese culture, producing gold sceptres, giant masks with protruding eyes and a 4-metre bronze tree. The 2023 new museum houses artefacts still being excavated.

attractions.name: Qingcheng Mountain (Front Range)
attractions.tip: One of Taoism's four sacred mountains, 1hr from Chengdu. Dense cloud forest, Taoist temples and a cable car. The 'sutra copying for free accommodation' activity (抄经换宿) at the Taoist academy is limited to 10 spots daily — book via their WeChat. The back mountain (后山, ¥20) has better scenery and fewer crowds.
attractions.description: One of the four sacred Taoist mountains of China — ancient temple complexes connected by stone steps through dense bamboo forests and pine groves on a 1,260-metre peak. Far less crowded than Emei and more genuinely atmospheric as a place of Taoist retreat.

attractions.name: Dujiangyan Irrigation System
attractions.tip: 2,300-year-old water diversion system still in daily use — a UNESCO World Heritage engineering marvel. Walk across the ancient rope bridge (安澜索桥). Combine with Qingcheng Mountain on the same day (15 min drive between them).
attractions.description: The world's oldest functioning irrigation system — built in 256BC by the engineer Li Bing to control the Min River and irrigate the Chengdu Plain, it has operated continuously for 2,276 years without a dam. A UNESCO World Heritage marvel of hydraulic engineering.

attractions.name: Sichuan Opera (Face Changing Show)
attractions.tip: Performers change elaborate painted masks in under a second — genuinely impossible to track how. The Shufeng Yayun teahouse show includes tea service and snacks. Book front-row seats. The fire-spitting act that opens the show is spectacular.
attractions.description: China's most theatrical performance — actors change between elaborately painted masks in fractions of a second using techniques so secret they are passed down only within families. Combined with fire-breathing and acrobatics, this is the most uniquely Sichuan cultural experience.

attractions.name: Yulin Road & Local Nightlife
attractions.tip: Zhao Lei's famous song 'Chengdu' is about this street. An authentic local neighbourhood with indie cafes and bars that hasn't been touristified. Start on Yulin North Road (玉林北路), wander into the side lanes. The small bar Jiu Guanjia is a local institution since 1996.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Leshan Giant Buddha (Day Trip)
attractions.tip: World's largest stone Buddha at 71m — carved into a cliff face over 90 years (713–803 AD). Book the ferry boat view (¥70) for the full frontal perspective you've seen in photos. The cliff-face stairs descend right past the Buddha's feet. Get there before 9am.
attractions.description: The world's largest stone Buddha — carved into a cliff face over 90 years in the Tang dynasty, Leshan's seated Maitreya is 71 metres tall and his toenails are each larger than an adult human. The boat tour from the river gives the only view that captures the full scale.

attractions.name: Chengdu Research Base Night Tour
attractions.tip: New night tour at the panda base — see red pandas and nocturnal animals under lantern light. Very limited tickets, book weeks ahead via official app. Completely different experience from the daytime visit. The full moon in September is a special event.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding

attractions.name: Huang Long Xi Ancient Town
attractions.tip: 1,700-year-old water town 40km from Chengdu — much less visited than Wuzhen. Ancient streets, fire dragon lantern festival (正月) and a famous fresh fish market. Worth it for the peaceful riverside teahouses alone. Free, unlike most ancient towns.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Huanglongxi Ancient Town Tourism Area （Northeast Gate）

attractions.name: Tianfu Art Park
attractions.tip: Brand new 2023 world-class arts complex — Chengdu's answer to M+ in Hong Kong. Three museums, massive public sculpture garden and lake. The architecture alone is worth the trip. Free outdoor areas are spectacular. Museum tickets ¥20–80 depending on exhibition.
attractions.description: A must-visit arts-district in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tomb of Emperor Wang Jian (Yongling)
attractions.tip: The only surviving above-ground royal tomb in China from the Five Dynasties period (907–925 AD) — an actual emperor's burial chamber you walk into. Low-key and rarely crowded. The stone musicians carved on the tomb are extraordinary works of art.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Chengdu that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding
attractions.chinese: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地
attractions.tip: Arrive right at opening time (7:30am) when pandas are most energetic during breakfast. Head straight to the nursery area first, then work backwards as crowds arrive later and pandas become sleepy.
attractions.description: Set within bamboo-filled grounds that mimic natural habitat, this conservation center lets you observe giant pandas lounging, playing, and munching bamboo at close range. The morning feeding sessions reveal these gentle giants at their most active and adorable.

attractions.name: Huanglongxi Ancient Town (Northeast Gate)
attractions.tip: Visit early morning before 9am to avoid crowds and catch locals practicing tai chi by the riverside. Skip the main street restaurants and head to side alleys for cheaper, more authentic Sichuan snacks.
attractions.description: Step onto cobblestone lanes flanked by Ming and Qing dynasty stilted buildings, ancient temples, and a working water mill. This riverside town offers an authentic glimpse into old Sichuan life with teahouses, traditional snacks, and peaceful canal views.

attractions.name: Happy Valley Chengdu
attractions.tip: Visit on weekdays to avoid massive weekend crowds, and download the park's app beforehand to check real-time wait times for popular rides like the wooden coaster and save hours of queuing.
attractions.description: This sprawling theme park in northern Chengdu delivers thrilling roller coasters, themed zones from Caribbean to ancient Shu kingdoms, and evening performances. It's Southwest China's answer to major international parks, combining adrenaline rides with cultural entertainment under one admission.

attractions.name: Chengdu Museum
attractions.tip: Visit the fifth floor first for the shadow puppet theater performances (usually 2pm and 3pm on weekends), then work your way down chronologically through the permanent exhibitions to avoid crowds and understand the historical flow better.
attractions.description: This striking contemporary museum towers over Tianfu Square, housing five floors of artifacts that trace Chengdu's 4,500-year journey from ancient Shu Kingdom to modern metropolis. The shadow puppet collection and Tang Dynasty treasures offer rare glimpses into Sichuan's distinct cultural identity.

attractions.name: People's Park
attractions.tip: Visit Heming Teahouse on weekends to see the traditional ear-cleaning service in action—skilled practitioners with long metal tools work on relaxed tea drinkers, a unique Chengdu custom worth photographing.
attractions.description: This beloved downtown green space pulses with authentic Chengdu life, from elderly tai chi practitioners at dawn to matchmaking corner displays and teahouse mahjong games. Come to sip tea by the lake, watch locals dance, or simply absorb the unhurried rhythm of everyday Chengdu culture.

attractions.name: Chengdu Museum New Hall
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid crowds, and don't miss the fourth floor's shadow puppet theater performances held twice daily. Free admission requires ID or passport at the entrance.
attractions.description: This contemporary museum on Tianfu Square showcases Chengdu's 3,000-year history through innovative exhibitions spanning ancient Shu civilization to modern times. The shadow puppet collection and interactive displays of regional folk culture make Sichuan's heritage accessible and engaging.

attractions.name: Dujiangyan Scenic Area (Southeast Gate 2)
