attractions.tip: Feed reindeer by hand, ride horse-drawn sleds through snow forests, take Christmas-themed photos. Kids absolutely love this. The reindeer are very tame. Opens from mid-December once snow accumulates. Combine with Mójie rafting on the same day — both in the Erdaobai River area.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: 雪乡 梦幻家园 snow town

attractions.name: Yanbian Korean Culture (Yanji City)
attractions.chinese: 延边朝鲜族自治州 — 延吉市
attractions.tip: Yanji is the capital of China's Korean autonomous prefecture — feel like you've stepped into Korea without a visa. Korean script on all signs, authentic Korean BBQ and cold noodles, North Korean restaurants. The Tumen River border checkpoint lets you see North Korea from 50m away. Great day in Yanji en route to or from Changbaishan. Fly into Yanbian Airport (YNJ) as an alternative to Changbaishan Airport (NBS).
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Changbaishan Scenic Area (North Slope)
attractions.tip: Visit between 6-9am for clearest views of Heaven Lake as afternoon clouds often obscure the crater. Bring a warm jacket even in summer—temperatures drop significantly at the summit.
attractions.description: The North Slope offers the most accessible route to Changbaishan's stunning Heaven Lake crater, a sapphire jewel cradled in volcanic peaks at 2,189 meters. Marvel at roaring waterfalls, primeval forests, and hot springs that showcase the raw power of this sacred mountain.

attractions.name: Changbai Mountain
attractions.tip: Visit the North Slope entrance early morning before tour groups arrive, and bring multiple layers—temperatures at the summit can drop dramatically even in summer, with sudden weather changes common.
attractions.description: An active volcanic massif straddling the China-North Korea border, crowned by the ethereal Heaven Lake crater at 2,189 meters. Come for the pristine alpine landscapes, cascading waterfalls, hot springs, and rare glimpses of volcanic peaks emerging through mist.

attractions.name: Changbai Korean Folk Village
attractions.tip: Visit during lunch hours to join a family-style Korean meal in a traditional home—locals often prepare homemade kimchi and rice wine that aren't available in restaurants.
attractions.description: This living cultural village showcases the traditions of Korea's ethnic minority in Northeast China through traditional architecture and daily customs. Experience authentic Korean-Chinese culture with folk performances, traditional ondol heated homes, and local handicraft demonstrations.

attractions.name: Changbai Mountain West Slope Scenic Area
attractions.tip: Start your climb early morning to avoid afternoon fog that often obscures the lake view. Wear sturdy hiking shoes as the wooden stairway can be slippery after rain.
attractions.description: The western approach to Changbai Mountain rewards adventurous visitors with dramatic canyon vistas and fewer crowds than the popular north slope. Climb 1,442 steps to reach the crater rim for sweeping views of Heaven Lake from a different perspective.

attractions.name: Changbaishan International Holiday Resort (East Gate)
attractions.chinese: 长白山国际度假区（东门）
attractions.tip: Buy your Changbaishan scenic area tickets at the resort's service center the night before to skip morning queues at the mountain entrance and catch the best weather windows for crater views.
attractions.description: A sprawling alpine resort village at the foot of Changbaishan Mountain, featuring ski slopes, hot springs, and European-style architecture. The resort serves as the premier base for exploring the volcanic peaks and pristine forests of the Changbai Mountain range.

attractions.name: Changbaishan Lu'Neng Ski Resort
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid crowds and get freshly groomed slopes. Rent equipment from the resort's shop early as popular sizes run out quickly during peak season.
attractions.description: One of Northeast China's premier ski destinations sprawls across pristine mountain slopes near the Wanda Resort complex. World-class facilities and powder snow conditions attract skiers of all levels seeking adventure in Changbaishan's winter wonderland.

attractions.name: Changbai Mountain Natural Ski Park
attractions.tip: Arrive at lift opening to enjoy freshly groomed runs before crowds. Rent equipment from Wanda Resort properties rather than on-mountain shops for better selection and competitive rates.
attractions.description: Northeast China's premier ski destination sprawls across pristine powder slopes beneath volcanic peaks at Changbai Mountain. State-of-the-art facilities and consistent snow quality from November to April make this a winter sports paradise.

attractions.name: Changbaishan International Holiday Resort
attractions.tip: Visit the resort's free observation deck at sunset for stunning views of Changbaishan's peaks without entering the main scenic area. The resort shuttle buses run every 30 minutes to the mountain entrance.
attractions.description: Northeast China's premier four-season mountain resort sits at the foot of volcanic Changbaishan, offering world-class skiing in winter and alpine adventures year-round. The sprawling complex blends luxury accommodations with direct access to pristine forests and the legendary Tianchi crater lake nearby.

attractions.name: Laolike Lake
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when mist often hovers over the water surface, creating ethereal photography conditions. The wooden boardwalk loop takes about 40 minutes at a leisurely pace.
attractions.description: This serene alpine lake sits cradled between forested slopes in the Erdaobai River valley, its mirror-like surface reflecting the changing colors of Changbai's wilderness. The tranquil setting and relatively uncrowded trails make it a peaceful escape from the main scenic areas.

attractions.name: Maoer Mountain National Forest Park
attractions.chinese: 吉林帽儿山国家森林公园
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning during weekdays to avoid crowds and catch the sunrise from the summit. The trail can be slippery after rain, so wear proper hiking shoes with good grip.
attractions.description: This forested mountain park near Yanji offers hiking trails through dense pine forests with panoramic views of the surrounding valleys. The distinctive hat-shaped peak provides a rewarding climb and serves as a natural escape from the city.

attractions.name: Cap Child Hill Botanic Garden
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when mist often rolls through the garden, creating ethereal photo opportunities among the mountain plants. The upper trails are less crowded and offer the best panoramic viewpoints.
attractions.description: This hillside botanical garden near Yanji showcases alpine flora native to the Changbai Mountain region, with winding paths through carefully cultivated plant zones. The elevated position offers sweeping views across the valley, making it ideal for nature lovers seeking a peaceful escape.

attractions.name: Tumen Port
attractions.tip: Visit in the morning when visibility is clearest and border activity is most active. Bring binoculars for better views of the North Korean side, and your passport as ID checks are common near the border zone.
attractions.description: Standing at the frontier between China and North Korea, this border crossing offers a rare glimpse into one of the world's most isolated nations just across the Tumen River. The viewing platform provides fascinating perspectives of North Korean villages and daily life unfolding on the opposite bank.

food.name: Hot Spring Eggs
food.tip: Eggs cooked directly in the volcanic hot spring pools on the north slope — ¥10 for 2. Unique experience. Taste is like a perfectly soft-boiled egg with a faint mineral tang. Every visitor does this.

food.name: Korean Cold Noodles (Yanji)
food.tip: Authentic Korean-style cold noodles in Yanji — completely different from Chinese Korean noodles. Thin wheat noodles in cold beef broth with pickled vegetables and hard-boiled egg. Best in winter when you need something refreshing after skiing.

food.name: Northeast Iron Pot Stew (Tiě Guō Dùn)
food.tip: The quintessential Northeast China dish — massive cast iron pot of braised pork, mushrooms, glass noodles and tofu. The Wanda resort area has several good restaurants. Hearty and warming after a day on the slopes.

food.name: Korean BBQ (Yanji Style)
food.tip: Genuine Korean-style BBQ in Yanji — marinated beef and pork grilled at the table, served with banchan side dishes. Much more authentic than the Korean BBQ in Chinese cities. Water market area (水上市场) is the best food strip.

food.name: Wanda Resort Hot Spring Restaurant
food.must_order: Northeast iron pot stew (铁锅炖), wild mushroom hot pot
food.tip: The resort hotels all have restaurants but the quality varies. The best is the iron pot stew (铁锅炖) concept — pork belly, mushrooms, glass noodles and tofu braised in a cast iron pot over an open flame. Incredibly warming after skiing. Wild mushrooms from Changbaishan forests are extraordinary — order any mushroom dish.

food.name: Erdaobai River Local Restaurants
food.near: Erdaobai River town, north slope base
food.must_order: Yanbian Korean cold noodles, Korean BBQ, Northeast stew
food.tip: The town at the north slope base has much cheaper restaurants than the Wanda resort — 60–70% less for the same food quality. Korean influence is strong (Yanbian Korean Autonomous Prefecture is nearby) — try the cold noodles and the Korean-style BBQ. The local mushroom markets sell Changbaishan specialties at source prices.

food.name: Yanji Korean Quarter Restaurants
food.near: Yanji City, Yanbian Korean Autonomous Prefecture
food.must_order: Authentic Korean cold noodles (냉면), Korean BBQ, kimchi jjigae
food.tip: Yanji feels like visiting Korea without a visa — Korean script everywhere, genuine Korean food at Chinese prices. The cold noodles (冷面) here are made by ethnic Koreans to the original recipe — completely different from Chinese imitations. The Korean BBQ restaurants near the Water Market (水上市场) are excellent. ¥40–60 per person for a full meal.

food.name: Mr. Cheng's Restaurant
food.tip: Popular with skiers visiting Changbaishan's North Slope. Best for hearty Northeast Chinese dishes after a day on the mountain. Arrive early during peak winter season.

food.name: Knight Coffee City Coffee Station
food.tip: Perfect warming stop before or after exploring Changbaishan's North Slope. Arrive early morning for fresh brews before heading to the scenic area.

food.name: Feike Coffee
food.tip: Perfect warm-up stop after visiting Changbai Mountain's north slope. Try their hot lattes to combat the mountain chill. Gets busy mid-morning with tour groups.

food.name: Kanon Coffee
food.tip: Warm up with a hot latte after morning activities on the snowy slopes. Arrive early during peak season as seating fills up quickly with skiers and hikers.

food.name: Fangbaili Cafe
food.tip: Warm up with a hot coffee after exploring Changbaishan's north slope. Arrive early during peak season as seating is limited near the scenic area.

food.name: Haidilao Hot Pot
food.tip: Order the hand-pulled noodle performance at your table. Arrive before 6pm to avoid long waits, even in this smaller mountain town location.

food.name: Fushanfang Restaurant
food.tip: Popular spot for warming Northeast Chinese cuisine after mountain activities. Try the stewed dishes and wild mushrooms. Arrive early during peak tourist season as it fills up quickly.

food.name: Cafe Rottibun
food.tip: Perfect warming stop after skiing or hiking in Changbaishan. Try their signature rotti buns with hot drinks to refuel during cold mountain weather.

food.name: Coffee Bene
food.tip: Warm up with hot coffee after skiing or hiking. Popular Korean-style franchise offering reliable espresso drinks and desserts. Gets crowded during peak tourist season, visit mid-morning for quieter ambiance.

food.name: Qingzhu Stone Pot Rice (Longjing Main Branch)
food.tip: Order the signature stone pot rice with Korean-style toppings. Arrive early during lunch hours as the crispy rice crust takes time to prepare properly.

food.name: Changlai Restaurant
food.tip: Local spot serving northeastern Chinese cuisine. Try the hearty stews and wild mushroom dishes typical of the Changbai Mountain region. Arrive early during peak tourist season.

food.name: Hands Coffee
food.tip: A highly-rated specialty coffee spot in Yanji. Perfect for warming up after exploring the mountains. Try their hand-drip coffee for the full artisanal experience.

food.name: Free Space Coffee Bar
food.tip: Perfect warming stop after mountain excursions. Try their hot Americano paired with local pastries. Quiet atmosphere ideal for planning your next day's Changbaishan itinerary.

food.name: C.Straits Coffee House
food.tip: Perfect warm-up spot after winter mountain activities. Try their hot coffee specialties paired with local pastries. Visit during off-peak afternoon hours for a quieter atmosphere.

food.name: Constellation Coffee
food.tip: Perfect warming stop before or after mountain trips. Ask for their signature hot lattes to combat the region's cold climate. Cozy atmosphere ideal for trip planning sessions.

food.name: Yijia Coffee
food.tip: A cozy neighborhood spot perfect for warming up after exploring Changbaishan. Try their Americano paired with local pastries. Quietest in late afternoons.

hotels.name: Wanda Sheraton Changbaishan Resort
hotels.chinese: 万达长白山喜来登度假酒店
hotels.tip: The flagship ski-in/ski-out property. Many room rates include hot spring access and ski lift tickets. Mountain view rooms are stunning. Book well in advance for winter weekends.

hotels.name: Wanda Westin Changbaishan Resort
hotels.chinese: 万达长白山威斯汀度假酒店
hotels.tip: Directly adjacent to Sheraton in the Wanda complex. More intimate, slightly smaller. Both properties share access to Hanashan Hot Spring.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Resort Changbaishan
hotels.tip: Swiss-branded resort with excellent panoramic views of Changbaishan peaks. Popular with families. Indoor pool, spa, kids' area.

hotels.name: Erdaobai River Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses (民宿) are 60–70% cheaper than Wanda resorts. Good base for north slope day trips. Average ¥250/night for double room. Communal atmosphere — meet local guides and other travellers.

hotels.name: The View Changbai Mountain MGallery
hotels.tip: MGallery boutique within the Wanda complex. Smaller and more design-focused than the big 5-stars. Often better value. Luneng ski resort access (slightly smaller than Wanda's slopes but less crowded).

hotels.name: Park Hyatt Changbaishan
hotels.tip: The most acclaimed luxury property in the Wanda resort complex — Park Hyatt elegance with mountain views, world-class spa and ski-in/ski-out access. Praised for immaculate service and beautiful design. Book package rates including hot spring and ski lift access for best value.

hotels.name: Wanda Vista Changbaishan
hotels.tip: Wanda's premium hotel brand within the resort — slightly smaller and more intimate than Sheraton and Westin. Very good value for the quality. Pool with mountain views, excellent ski equipment rental service. Praised for smooth check-in and friendly staff.

hotels.name: Jinyue Hot Spring Hotel
hotels.tip: Mid-range hotel with genuine volcanic hot spring access included — the best value hot spring experience near Changbaishan. Simple rooms but the hot spring pools (indoor and outdoor) are the attraction. Good base for north slope Heavenly Lake day trip.

hotels.name: Erdaobai River Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses at 60-70% less than Wanda resort prices. Communal atmosphere — share tips with guides and other travellers. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ Trip.com ratings. Average ¥250/night. Walk to local restaurants and shuttle buses to north slope.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Resort Changbaishan
hotels.tip: Swiss hotel management quality at mid-range prices — more intimate than the big 5-stars, panoramic Changbaishan mountain views, excellent buffet breakfast. Good value in the resort for the quality of service received. Indoor pool and good ski storage facilities.

sanya.name: Sanya
sanya.recommended_base_reason: Yalong Bay has the best beaches, clearest water and highest concentration of 5-star resorts
sanya.seasonal_note: Nov–Apr is peak season — dry, sunny, 25–30°C. Jul–Sep has typhoon risk and summer crowds. May–Jun and Oct are shoulder season — good value.

clusters.haitang-bay: Atlantis, Wuzhizhou Island ferry, duty-free mall, 68 Global Food Street
clusters.yalong-bay: Best public beach, 5-star hotel strip (Ritz, Hilton, St Regis), forest park, rose valley
clusters.dadonghai: Budget beach, water sports, backpacker vibe, convenient for city centre
clusters.sanya-bay: Coconut promenade, sunset viewing, local restaurants, more affordable
clusters.tianya-haijiao: Iconic coastal cultural landmark, scenic drive west of city

attractions.name: Wuzhizhou Island
attractions.tip: Known as 'China's Maldives' — 5A national scenic area. Crystal clear water with 10–30m visibility, pristine coral reefs, 4 dive sites. Take the 15-minute ferry from Haitang Bay pier (early — 8am ferry). Activities: PADI diving (¥680+), snorkelling, parasailing, banana boat, helicopter ride. Electric cart tour covers the island in 1hr. Famous 'Lover's Bridge' and 'Sunrise Rock' photo spots. Kids under 1.2m free. Note: 70+ adults require liability waiver to board ferry.
attractions.description: China's clearest coastal water — 10-30 metre visibility, pristine coral reefs and four world-class PADI dive sites surrounding a small island accessible by 15-minute ferry. The 'Lover's Bridge' connecting two rock formations at the island's tip has been a marriage proposal destination since the island opened.
attractions.photo_query: wuzhizhou island sanya diving coral reef

attractions.name: Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark
attractions.tip: Massive waterpark attached to Atlantis Resort — slides, wave pools, lazy river, dedicated toddler splash zone. The 'Aquaventure' main slide complex includes the 'Leap of Faith' (near-vertical drop). Indoor sections work year-round. The resort's 'Lost Chambers Aquarium' is separately ticketed (¥120) and excellent for younger kids. Book combo ticket (waterpark + aquarium) for best value. Non-Atlantis hotel guests can purchase day access.
attractions.description: A must-visit theme-park in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yalong Bay Beach
attractions.chinese: 亚龙湾国家旅游度假区
attractions.tip: Sanya's finest beach — 7.5km of white coral sand, water visibility 9m+, lifeguards on duty. The public beach section is free. Flanked by 5-star hotels (Ritz-Carlton, Hilton, Sheraton). Water sports at designated spots: jetski ¥200/15min, parasailing ¥300. Best morning beach in Sanya — arrive before 9am for empty sand. Afternoon gets crowded. Combine with Tropical Forest Park (¥158) for a full day.
attractions.description: Sanya's finest beach — 7.5km of white coral sand with water visibility of 9 metres, flanked by the best concentration of five-star resort hotels in China. The combination of tropical water quality and reliable sunshine from November to April makes this China's undisputed beach capital.
attractions.photo_query: yalong bay beach sanya tropical resort

attractions.name: Nanshan Cultural Tourism Zone
attractions.tip: Home to the 108m ocean Guanyin statue — one of the tallest statues in the world, standing on a platform in the sea. Stunning at sunrise or sunset. Temple complex includes Nanshan Temple (authentic, not touristy), Longevity Culture Garden and vegetarian restaurant (surprisingly excellent). 30 min west of city centre. Kids fascinated by the massive statue scale. Combine with Tianya Haijiao on the same western day trip.
attractions.description: The most dramatic religious statue in China — a 108-metre white jade Guanyin stands on a platform in the sea at the edge of China, visible from 10km away. The surrounding Nanshan Temple complex dating from the Han dynasty is one of China's most sacred Buddhist sites.

attractions.name: Tianya Haijiao (End of the Earth)
attractions.tip: Iconic coastal landmark where two massive inscribed boulders read 'Tianya' and 'Haijiao' (Edge of the Sky, Corner of the Sea). Romanticised in Chinese poetry and culture for centuries — couples see it as a declaration of eternal love. The crooked coconut tree nearby is now more famous than the rocks (always has a queue for photos). 40-min walk along scenic coast. Combine with Nanshan on same day.
attractions.description: The legendary 'End of the World' — two enormous granite boulders inscribed with characters meaning 'Edge of the Sky' and 'Rim of the Sea' at Sanya's southwestern tip. A classical metaphor for the most remote place imaginable has been physically real here for 1,300 years.
attractions.photo_query: tianya haijiao sanya granite rock sea

attractions.name: Yalong Bay Tropical Forest Park
attractions.chinese: 亚龙湾热带天堂森林公园
attractions.tip: Filming location for several famous Chinese films. China's only all-sea-view glass walkway. Zip line through tropical forest. Cable car rides. The glass walkway section is genuinely scary and spectacular. Best morning activity in Yalong Bay. Combine with beach afternoon. Kids love the zip line (minimum height applies).
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: Yalong Bay Tropic Paradise Forest Park

attractions.name: Haitang Bay Duty Free Mall
attractions.chinese: 海棠湾三亚国际免税城
attractions.tip: World's largest duty-free mall — massive discounts on cosmetics, perfume, spirits, watches, handbags. No customs forms needed as a Chinese domestic visitor — just show your ID. SEA visitors on a Chinese tourist visa can also shop. Evenings are best — cooler and beautifully lit. The 68 Global Food Street next door has excellent international dining options.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.
attractions.photo_attribution: China Duty Free Sanya Duty Free Shop

attractions.name: Hou Hai (Back Sea) Surf Village
attractions.tip: China's #1 surf destination — consistent waves, professional instructors, relaxed village vibe. Called 'China's Hawaii'. Beginner lessons ¥200 (equipment + 2hr lesson). More bohemian atmosphere than resort beaches — colourful cafes, board rental shops, local fishermen. Best waves Oct–Mar. In summer, wakesurf (indoor cable system) is popular. A complete contrast to the resort strip.
attractions.description: A must-visit adventure in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Sanya Qiangujing Cultural Park
attractions.tip: Large-scale outdoor cultural performance — 'Sanya Through the Ages' show blends Li minority culture, maritime Silk Road history and ancient Hainan traditions. Massive stadium, spectacular light shows, acrobatics and traditional dance. Evening show starts at 7:30pm. Kids love the spectacle. The park around the theatre also has a water park and children's playground (daytime). One of Sanya's best evening activities.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Luhuitou Park
attractions.tip: Skip the daytime heat and arrive an hour before sunset to catch golden hour photos at the deer statue, then stay for the city lights turning on below.
attractions.description: This hillside park crowns a peninsula with sweeping panoramas of Sanya Bay, the city skyline, and the South China Sea. The iconic deer sculpture celebrating a romantic local legend makes it a beloved spot for sunset viewing and cultural photography.

attractions.name: Tianya Haijiao (Edge of the Sky, Corner of the Sea)
attractions.tip: Visit at sunrise around 6:30 AM to avoid tour groups and capture the best photos of the illuminated rock inscriptions with softer lighting that brings out the texture.
attractions.description: Legendary seaside park where massive ancient boulders carved with poetry rise from white sand beaches along turquoise waters. These iconic rocks symbolize eternal love and the ends of the earth, drawing visitors seeking both dramatic coastal scenery and cultural significance.

attractions.name: Yalongwan Yuntian Tropical Park
attractions.tip: Visit in the late afternoon around 4-5pm when the light is softer for photos and temperatures drop. The uphill walking trails can be steep, so wear comfortable shoes with good grip.
attractions.description: This hillside tropical park rises above Yalong Bay, offering elevated viewpoints draped in rainforest greenery and colorful flowering plants. Come for sweeping panoramic views of the crescent bay and its turquoise waters stretching to the horizon.

attractions.name: Yanoda Rainforest
attractions.tip: Arrive right at opening to avoid tour groups and experience the rainforest at its most tranquil. Bring a lightweight rain jacket as afternoon showers are common in the valley, creating magical misty conditions.
attractions.description: A lush tropical rainforest park in Sanya's hinterland where ancient trees, rushing waterfalls, and mist-shrouded valleys create a jungle wonderland. Come for canopy walkways through pristine rainforest and thrilling zip-line adventures high above the emerald valley floor.

attractions.name: Sanya Bay
attractions.tip: Visit the western end near Coconut Dream Corridor around 5-6pm for the best sunset shots with fewer tourists. Local seafood restaurants along Yuya Road offer better prices than beachfront hotels.
attractions.description: A sweeping 22-kilometer crescent bay flanked by dramatic peninsulas, where coconut-lined promenades meet powder-soft sand. Come for spectacular sunset views over the South China Sea and leisurely beachside strolls away from the resort crowds.

attractions.name: Yalong Bay International Rose Valley
attractions.tip: Visit in the early morning between 8-10am when roses are freshest and temperatures are cooler for walking the hillside paths. The upper terraces offer the best panoramic valley views with fewer crowds.
attractions.description: A sprawling hillside garden showcasing thousands of rose varieties alongside tropical flowers, with valley views stretching toward Yalong Bay's coastline. The terraced gardens and colorful blooms create picture-perfect backdrops, especially appealing to photography enthusiasts and flower lovers.

attractions.name: Tianyahaijiao Planet Sculpture
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when tour groups focus on the main beach rocks first, giving you clear shots of the sculpture without crowds blocking your frame.
attractions.description: This striking spherical sculpture stands as a modern landmark within the famous Tianya Haijiao scenic area, contrasting ancient romance with contemporary art. It offers a unique photo opportunity beyond the traditional stone inscriptions.

food.name: Fresh Seafood (Market Cook-Up)
food.chinese: 海鲜市场 自选加工
food.tip: Buy your own seafood from Sanya's wet markets (Jiefang Road market) then pay a nearby restaurant to cook it (加工费 ¥30–50). Best value seafood in Sanya. ALWAYS confirm price per jin (500g) before agreeing to anything. Popular: crab, prawns, abalone, grouper.

food.name: Coconut Chicken Hot Pot
food.tip: Hainan's signature dish — fresh whole coconut cracked at your table, juice becomes the broth, young chicken cooked inside. Light, sweet and fragrant. Completely different from Sichuan hot pot. Every restaurant in Sanya does this — price varies wildly. Ask locals to recommend a non-tourist one.

food.name: Wenchang Chicken Rice
food.tip: Hainan's answer to Singapore chicken rice — free-range chicken steamed then served with rice cooked in chicken broth, with ginger-scallion sauce. Light and clean flavour. SEA visitors immediately recognise it. Wenchang is the origin city (2hrs north) but Sanya has many excellent versions.

food.name: Atlantis Lost Chambers Cafe
food.must_order: Shark-blue latte, tropical fruit cocktail
food.tip: Coffee surrounded by massive aquarium tanks containing sharks and rays. Atlantis's underwater aquarium dining is one of China's most unusual food experiences. The blue ambient light and marine life create extraordinary photos. Accessible with Atlantis resort day pass or aquarium ticket.

food.name: Coconut Chicken Hot Pot Restaurants
food.near: Throughout Sanya — ask hotel for best non-tourist option
food.must_order: Fresh coconut cracked at table, chicken, dipping sauce
food.tip: Hainan's signature dish — a fresh whole coconut cracked at your table, the juice becomes the cooking broth, the young chicken cooks inside. Light, sweet and utterly unique. The freshly cracked coconut fragrance is amazing. Price varies enormously — avoid tourist restaurants near the main beach strip. Ask hotel staff for their local favourite.

food.name: Houhai Village Surf Cafes
food.must_order: Post-surf cold brew, fresh açaí bowl, tropical smoothie
food.tip: Houhai is China's surf capital and has developed a genuine surf cafe culture — colorful shacks and boutique cafes overlooking the beach. The vibe is closest to Bali or Hawaii of anywhere in China. Fresh tropical fruit, cold brew coffee, surf boards everywhere. Best late morning after the first surf session.

food.name: Wenchang Chicken Rice Restaurants
food.near: Any local area in Sanya — ask locals
food.must_order: Wenchang chicken rice — steamed chicken, ginger-scallion sauce, broth-cooked rice
food.tip: Hainan chicken rice is the ancestor of Singapore chicken rice — lighter, more fragrant, with better dipping sauces. The best is found in local coffee shops (not tourist restaurants) around Jiefang Road market area. SEA visitors immediately recognise the format and usually find the Hainanese original superior. ¥25–40 per person.

food.name: 68 Global Food Street
food.near: Adjacent to Duty-Free Mall, Haitang Bay
food.must_order: Browse the stalls — everything from Thai to Cantonese to Japanese
food.tip: Massive international food street next to the duty-free mall — dozens of stalls and restaurants covering global cuisines. Good for families with picky eaters. Evening atmosphere is lively with outdoor seating, live music and food stalls lighting up. Best visited after duty-free shopping.

food.name: Hai Yuan Shu Xiang Restaurant
food.tip: Specializes in Sichuan cuisine with a seafood twist. Try their spicy fish dishes combining fresh Hainan seafood with bold Sichuan flavors. Book ahead during peak dinner hours.

food.name: The Sanya EDITION
food.tip: Book sunset dining at their beachfront restaurants for stunning Haitang Bay views. The resort features multiple dining venues, so check menus in advance to choose your preferred cuisine.

food.name: Sanya Hong Kong, Macau and Cantonese Food Restaurant
food.tip: Order the dim sum during lunch service for the freshest selection. Try the roast goose and char siu, classic Cantonese staples done well here.

food.name: The Ritz Carlton Sanya
food.tip: Book sunset dining at their beachfront restaurant for prime ocean views. The breakfast buffet offers excellent variety, but reservations are essential during peak holiday periods.

food.name: Fly Coffee
food.tip: Perfect post-beach stop in Haitang Bay. Try their signature iced lattes to cool down after exploring nearby Atlantis resort and duty-free shopping complex.

food.name: Coast Coffee Shop
food.tip: Perfect spot for a morning coffee before hitting Haitang Bay's beaches. Grab a seat by the window for ocean views while you sip.

food.name: Ka Coffee Bar
food.tip: Perfect seaside coffee stop in Haitang Bay. Visit during late afternoon for stunning sunset views over the water while enjoying their signature espresso drinks.

food.name: Xiangcao Coffee House
food.tip: Visit during late afternoon to avoid the midday heat and enjoy sunset views over Haitang Bay while sipping their signature vanilla latte.

food.name: Coffee Time
food.tip: A convenient coffee stop in Haitang Bay's shopping district. Best for a quick caffeine fix between beach visits or mall shopping sessions.

food.name: Yiyi Beer Restaurant
food.chinese: 三亚亚龙湾依依啤酒屋
food.tip: Popular spot for cold draft beer and grilled seafood after a beach day. Visit during sunset hours for the best atmosphere and fresher catches.

food.name: Marriott Cafe
food.tip: Visit during breakfast buffet hours for the best value and variety. Ocean-view window seats fill up fast, so arrive early or reserve ahead.

food.name: Starbucks
food.tip: Visit during morning hours to avoid tourist crowds from nearby resorts. Try the regional Hainan coconut latte, exclusive to island locations. Outdoor seating offers bay views.

food.name: Blue Moon Coffee House
food.tip: Perfect spot for a post-beach coffee break. Try their iced Americano to cool down after exploring Yalong Bay's beaches. Quietest during late afternoons before sunset.

food.name: Dujin Coffee
food.tip: Visit in the morning to escape Yalong Bay's midday heat. Try their signature cold brew—perfect refreshment after a beach walk. Seating fills up quickly on weekends.

food.name: Casamia Restaurant Pizzeria
food.tip: Order the wood-fired Margherita pizza—it's authentic Italian style. Visit during weekday lunch for a quieter atmosphere and potential discounts on set menus.

food.name: Diadiade Coconut Chicken Sanya Bay Store
food.chinese: 爹爹嗲嗲椰子鸡三亚湾店
food.tip: Order the signature coconut chicken hotpot with free-range chicken. The coconut water base is naturally sweet. Arrive early during dinner as this popular spot fills quickly with locals.

food.name: Qiongxiangge Hainan Cai Restaurant (Sanya Bay Store)
food.chinese: 琼湘阁海南菜餐厅(三亚湾店)
food.tip: Order the Wenchang chicken, a Hainan specialty with tender poached meat and ginger-scallion dip. Arrive early during peak season as this local favorite fills up quickly at dinner.

food.name: Sanya Xianggechuan Hunan Flavor Restaurant
food.tip: Order the signature spicy fish head with pickled peppers and dry-pot dishes. Request less oil if you're not used to authentic Hunan-style cooking.

food.name: Zizaixuan Coffee Book Bar
food.tip: Perfect quiet retreat from Sanya's tourist crowds. Come in the afternoon for coffee and reading time. The book selection focuses on travel and local culture.

hotels.name: Atlantis Sanya
hotels.tip: The destination resort — waterpark access included, multiple beach clubs, world-class dining. Book the Aquaventure view rooms. Book 3+ months ahead for peak season (Dec–Feb).

hotels.name: The Ritz-Carlton Sanya Yalong Bay
hotels.tip: Best location on Yalong Bay — direct beach access, excellent pool complex. More low-key than Atlantis. Better for couples and families who want beach over waterpark.

hotels.name: Club Med Sanya
hotels.tip: All-inclusive — food, drinks, sports and kids' club all included. Best for families with young children — dedicated kids' programmes. Saves worrying about meals. Particularly popular with SEA visitors.

hotels.name: Dadonghai Budget Hotels
hotels.tip: Multiple budget options near Dadonghai beach — ¥300–500/night. Walk to beach in 5 minutes. More local atmosphere, cheaper food nearby. Good base for budget travellers.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Sanya Haitang Bay
hotels.chinese: 三亚海棠湾皇冠假日酒店
hotels.tip: Excellent value 5-star in Haitang Bay — walking distance from duty-free, Atlantis day pass available. Better value than Atlantis itself for families who don't need the full resort.

hotels.name: Rosewood Sanya
hotels.tip: One of the finest resort hotels in China — private beach villas, spectacular sea views, extraordinary butler service. Rosewood's most acclaimed China property. Multiple dining options. The best pure-luxury beach experience in Sanya. Book direct for best rates.

hotels.name: Ritz-Carlton Sanya Yalong Bay
hotels.chinese: 三亚亚龙湾丽思卡尔顿酒店
hotels.tip: The finest hotel directly on Yalong Bay beach — Forbes Five-Star, private beach section, excellent kids' facilities. Better for families than Rosewood. Great breakfast spread. The most awarded beach resort in Yalong Bay.

hotels.name: MGM Grand Sanya
hotels.tip: Good value 5-star on Yalong Bay — praised for spacious rooms, large pools and beach access. More affordable than Ritz-Carlton next door. Excellent for families. Good for booking late when Ritz and Atlantis are full or over budget.

hotels.name: Sanya Phoenix Airport Hotel
hotels.tip: Clean, functional budget hotel 5 min from Sanya airport — excellent if arriving late or departing early. Not a tourist hotel but does the job perfectly for short stays or transit nights. Free airport shuttle. Book for transit nights only.

hotels.name: Dadonghai Budget Hotels
hotels.tip: Multiple budget hotels cluster around Dadonghai beach — ¥300-500/night, walk to the beach in 5 minutes. More local atmosphere, cheaper restaurants nearby. Pick any with recent Trip.com 8.0+ reviews. Good for budget travellers who want beach access.

xiamen.name: Xiamen
xiamen.recommended_base_reason: Siming district puts you between Gulangyu ferry, Nanputuo temple, Xiamen University and Huandao Road cycling — the core of everything
xiamen.seasonal_note: Spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov) are ideal. Summer is hot and humid with occasional typhoons. January is fine but coolest.

clusters.gulangyu-island: Historic colonial island — mansions, piano museum, seafood snacks, no cars. 20 min ferry from Xiamen
clusters.siming-south: Nanputuo Temple, Xiamen University, Shapowei old harbour, Zengcuoan village, Huandao cycling road
clusters.zhongshan-road: Old shopping street, street food, 8th Market (Ba Shi), colonial architecture
clusters.jimei: Jimei University Village (Jiaogeng Memorial), Jimei Bridge, day trip easy from city
clusters.tulou-day-trip: Fujian earthen castles — 2.5hr from Xiamen. UNESCO. Hakka culture. Standalone day trip

attractions.name: Gulangyu Island
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage site — car-free island of colonial mansions, piano music (highest piano density in China), boutique cafes and extraordinary seafood snacks. Take the tourist ferry (¥35 includes return) from International Cruise Terminal — NOT the local ferry. Key spots: Piano Museum (¥50), Dragon Head Road snack street, 'Most Beautiful Corner' photo spot, Lin Qiaozhi Memorial Garden (Gynecologist hero), Port Jiahou beach. Walk everywhere — no bikes or vehicles. Morning is essential — gets very crowded by midday. Budget 4–5hrs.
attractions.description: A car-free UNESCO World Heritage island where colonial mansions from 13 countries still stand amid banana trees and bougainvillea — the highest concentration of grand pianos per capita of anywhere in the world. Walk the quiet lanes at dawn before the 50,000 daily visitors arrive.

attractions.name: Nanputuo Temple
attractions.tip: Famous Tang dynasty Buddhist temple at the foot of Wulao Peak — free entry. Beautiful multi-layered architecture, lotus ponds, large incense urns. The Buddhist vegetarian restaurant inside is excellent (try the creative tofu dishes — ¥30–80). Climb the rocky hillside behind the temple for panoramic views of the bay and Xiamen University. Must book via WeChat ahead — limited daily capacity. Walk 5 mins to Xiamen University gate.
attractions.description: A Tang dynasty Buddhist temple at the foot of Wulao Peak — the free temple grounds contain beautiful classical Chinese architecture, lotus ponds and a rocky hillside with panoramic views of Xiamen Bay. The famous vegetarian restaurant inside is one of China's most celebrated.

attractions.name: Xiamen University Campus
attractions.tip: Called 'China's most beautiful university' — stunning mix of classical Minnan architecture and modern buildings, surrounded by the sea. The famous芙蓉隧道 (Furong Tunnel) has 500m of student murals — best photo spot. Book entry via official WeChat mini-program — visitor numbers strictly controlled (weekdays easier). Enter through South Gate near Nanputuo. The campus lake and Lotus Road are especially scenic in spring.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zengcuoan Village
attractions.tip: Original fishing village turned artsy neighbourhood — colourful buildings, indie cafes, bookshops, bars and hostels. Much more authentic than Gulangyu's tourist strip. Called 'China's most bohemian village'. Peaceful in the day, transforms into a lively night market with local snacks and live music. The beachfront boardwalk at sunset is beautiful. Walk here along Huandao cycling road from Xiamen University.
attractions.description: Xiamen's most beloved village turned creative neighbourhood — a beachfront village whose fishermen's houses now contain indie cafes, boutiques and bars, while the sandy beach boardwalk at sunset is the most romantic spot in Xiamen. Much more authentic than the polished Gulangyu tourist experience.

attractions.name: Huandao Road Cycling
attractions.tip: China's most beautiful coastal cycling road — 43km total but the 12km stretch from Xiamen University to Zengcuoan is the classic route. Rent bikes at Bai Cheng Beach (¥30/hr) — electric bikes available. Passes Yanwu Bridge (world's longest across-sea railway-road bridge), the famous 'One Country Two Systems' sign, and multiple beach access points. Morning is best — avoid midday heat. Kids 6+ comfortable on adult bikes. Stunning views in both directions.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Fujian Tulou Earthen Castles
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage — massive circular Hakka communal earth buildings, some housing hundreds of families for 700+ years. The 'King of Tulou' Chengqilou holds a Guinness record. The best cluster is Gaobei (高北土楼群). Drone footage location for the animated film 'Big Fish & Begonia'. Takes a full day from Xiamen — book a tour or hire a driver (¥400-500 round trip for 4 people, much better than bus). Kids are fascinated by the 'castle' structure. An aerial view from the hillside makes the circular design click — don't miss it.
attractions.description: The most extraordinary vernacular architecture in China — circular Hakka communal earth buildings up to 5 storeys tall, some housing hundreds of families for 700 years. The aerial view that reveals the perfect circles surrounded by rice paddies inspired the animated film Big Fish and Begonia.
attractions.photo_query: fujian tulou earthen castle hakka circular

attractions.name: Jimei University Village
attractions.tip: Founded by overseas Chinese philanthropist Tan Kah Kee — beautifully preserved campus blending Minnan traditional and Western architectural styles. Bright red tilework, classical Chinese rooflines. The memorial to Tan Kah Kee is touching — a man who donated his entire fortune to build schools for poor Chinese children. Sunset here over Jimei Bridge is excellent. Combine with Jimei seafood market for dinner.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shapowei Old Harbour District
attractions.tip: Xiamen's last surviving working harbour — old fishing boats, weathered warehouses, street art and creative cafes. Less known than Zengcuoan but more genuinely authentic. Artists and architects have moved in alongside old fishermen. The container-style art galleries and coffee shops are excellent. Walk along the seawall as the sun goes down. 15-min walk from Nanputuo Temple.
attractions.description: A must-visit local in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Gulangyu Wharf
attractions.tip: Take ferries from Xiamen International Cruise Center Terminal instead of Xiamen Ferry Terminal during peak seasons to avoid massive crowds and enjoy better views during the crossing.
attractions.description: This historic ferry terminal serves as the gateway to car-free Gulangyu Island, where colonial architecture meets ocean breezes. The wharf itself offers sweeping views of Xiamen's evolving skyline across the shimmering harbor waters.

attractions.name: One Hundred Years Of Gulangyu Island
attractions.tip: Visit early morning on weekdays to avoid tour groups and have the exhibits to yourself. The upstairs section contains rare historical photographs of old Xiamen that most visitors miss.
attractions.description: This exhibition chronicles Gulangyu Island's fascinating transformation from a colonial trading port to a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Through vintage photographs, artifacts, and architectural displays, visitors gain deep insight into the island's unique blend of Eastern and Western cultural heritage.

attractions.name: Gulang Dongtian
attractions.tip: Visit early morning before 9am to avoid tour groups and catch the best natural lighting filtering through the cave openings for photography.
attractions.description: This historic cave formation on Gulangyu Island features dramatic rock formations and winding pathways carved through natural stone. The site offers a unique geological perspective of the island with cool, shaded passages perfect for escaping Xiamen's summer heat.

attractions.name: Haoyueyuan Garden
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when tour groups thin out and the golden light creates perfect conditions for photographing the statue against the sea. The rocks by the water make excellent foreground elements.
attractions.description: This seaside garden on Gulangyu Island showcases a towering 15-meter statue of national hero Zheng Chenggong gazing across the strait toward Taiwan. The waterfront location offers sweeping views of Xiamen's skyline framed by rocky shores and crashing waves.

attractions.name: Longtou Mountain Cave
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide when you can safely explore deeper into the cave and stand on the exposed rocks at the entrance for dramatic photos without getting splashed.
attractions.description: This natural sea cave carved into the rocky coastline of Gulangyu Island offers dramatic views of crashing waves and weathered stone formations. The cave's unique geological features and cooling sea breeze make it a refreshing escape from the island's busier attractions.

attractions.name: Sunlight Rock Scenic Area
attractions.tip: The combined ticket includes Shuzhuang Garden next door — always buy the combo. Go before 9am on weekends or the queue for the summit rocks is 40+ minutes.
attractions.description: Xiamen's most iconic landmark — a massive granite boulder rising 92 metres above Gulangyu Island, reached by a short but steep climb through gardens where Koxinga once trained his troops. The 360-degree summit view takes in the entire island, the Xiamen skyline and the strait beyond.

attractions.name: Hulishan Cannon Fort
attractions.tip: Cannon-firing reenactments happen at 10am and 3pm daily — worth timing your visit around one. The small museum inside the gun emplacement has surprisingly good English labelling.
attractions.description: A 19th-century coastal fortress housing the world's largest preserved ancient cannon — a 50-tonne German Krupp gun that never fired a shot in anger, built to defend Xiamen harbour from naval attack. The cliffside battlements offer panoramic views across the strait.

attractions.name: Tianluokeng Tulou Cluster
attractions.tip: The iconic overhead view is from the hilltop lookout — get there by 7am before tour buses arrive. The square building (Buyun Lou) is less visited and more atmospheric inside than the round ones.
attractions.description: The most photogenic of Fujian's UNESCO-listed earth buildings — four round tulou surrounding one square one, arranged on a hillside like a flower when seen from the famous viewpoint above. Hakka families have lived continuously in these 800-year-old communal structures, and some residents still rent rooms.

attractions.name: Ao Yuan Garden
attractions.tip: The stone carvings along the walls tell Chinese history chronologically left to right — allow time to walk the full perimeter. The garden is in Jimei, the education village Tan Kah Kee built from scratch with his own fortune.
attractions.description: A garden mausoleum built by Tan Kah Kee — the Fujian-born rubber magnate who became Southeast Asia's most famous Chinese philanthropist — combining his tomb with an outdoor gallery of stone carvings depicting Chinese history. A quiet pilgrimage site for overseas Chinese from Singapore and Malaysia.

attractions.name: Hi Heaven Creative Park
attractions.tip: Best on weekend evenings when the outdoor market sets up and local bands play in the courtyard. Weekday afternoons are near-empty — go on weekends for the atmosphere.
attractions.description: A creative lifestyle complex in Jimei's emerging arts district — converted industrial buildings housing independent boutiques, concept restaurants and live music venues that draw the Xiamen University student crowd on weekends. Street art installations change seasonally.

food.name: 8th Market (Ba Shi Seafood)
food.tip: Kaiyuan Road No.8 Market — Xiamen's most authentic wet market where locals buy seafood. Get there by 8am. Buy fresh sea snails, oysters, razor clams, and eat at the stalls inside. A chaotic, wonderful experience. Much cheaper than tourist restaurants. The 土笋冻 (earthworm jelly) — a Xiamen delicacy — is best found here.

food.name: Gulangyu Snack Street
food.tip: Dragon Head Road on Gulangyu is lined with legendary snack shops. Must-try: 鱼丸 (fish balls), 沙茶面 (satay noodle soup), 花生汤 (peanut soup), 土笋冻 (savoury jelly). The Zhang Sanfeng milk tea (张三疯) is the island's most famous — always a queue. Buy before noon before popular items sell out.

food.name: Satay Noodles (Sha Cha Mian)
food.tip: Xiamen's signature noodle dish — thin egg noodles in a rich, fragrant satay-based broth with pork offal, tofu and prawns. The satay in Fujian came via Southeast Asia. SEA visitors often recognise the influence. Best at old-school hole-in-wall shops near Nanputuo or Zhongshan Road, not tourist spots.

food.name: Nanputuo Vegetarian Restaurant
food.tip: Inside Nanputuo Temple — creative Buddhist vegetarian cuisine presented beautifully. Dishes named poetically after Buddhist concepts. The mock-meat dishes are extraordinary. Book ahead — always full. One of China's most famous vegetarian restaurants. Excellent for non-meat eaters.

food.name: Gulangyu Dragon Head Road Snacks
food.near: Dragon Head Road (Longtou Lu), Gulangyu Island
food.must_order: Salty fish balls (鱼丸), satay noodle soup (沙茶面), peanut soup, oyster omelette
food.tip: Gulangyu's main food strip — buy snacks and eat while walking. The island specialties: fish balls in clear broth (bouncy, fresh), satay-based noodle soup (Fujian's Southeast Asian influence is clear), peanut soup (hot, sweet, rich). Zhang Sanfeng (张三疯) milk tea always has a queue — join it. Buy before noon before the best items sell out.

food.name: Only One Coffee Xiamen
food.near: Near Gulangyu ferry terminal or Zengcuoan village
food.must_order: Couple set — drinks, snack platter and dessert
food.tip: The most photogenic cafe in Xiamen — viral on Xiaohongshu (RED) for its beautiful decor and photo spots. Multiple Instagram-worthy corners throughout. The couple set is popular. Check their current location as they may have moved or opened new branches since 2025.

food.name: Nanputuo Buddhist Vegetarian Restaurant
food.must_order: Creative tofu dishes, seasonal vegetarian menu
food.tip: One of China's most famous vegetarian restaurants inside a 1,000-year-old Buddhist temple — dishes are named after Buddhist concepts and presented beautifully. The mock-meat dishes are extraordinary. Book ahead — always full for lunch. ¥30–80 per dish. The setting (temple garden) is as remarkable as the food. Walk to Xiamen University gate afterwards.

food.name: Zengcuoan Night Market
food.must_order: Oyster omelette (蚵仔煎), seafood, local beer
food.tip: The best evening experience in Xiamen — the village transforms into a vibrant night market after 7pm. Local seafood stalls, indie bars and live music. Much more authentic than Gulangyu's tourist strip. The beachfront boardwalk at the edge of the village is beautiful at night. Walk here from Xiamen University along the coastal path.

food.name: Shapowei Old Harbour Cafes
food.must_order: Iced coffee, local oyster toast, passionfruit tea
food.tip: Xiamen's last working harbour turned artsy neighbourhood. The container-style art gallery cafes have harbour views and are much less crowded than Gulangyu. Visit late afternoon when the low light hits the old fishing boats. The combination of weathered boats, colourful murals and artisan coffee is very Xiamen.

food.name: Yijia
food.tip: Popular spot on Gulangyu Island, so arrive early during peak tourist hours to avoid long waits. Ask for local seafood specialties if available that day.

food.name: Bollo
food.tip: This highly-rated spot on Gulangyu Island offers a peaceful escape from tourist crowds. Visit during off-peak hours for the best atmosphere and service.

food.name: Rendeavous Cafe
food.tip: The back terrace has sea views on clear days — ask for a window seat when you arrive. French press coffee is the best value on the menu.

food.name: CBD Cafe
food.tip: Quiet on weekday mornings — good for working with decent wifi. The cold brew is strong and properly made. Gets loud in the evenings.

food.name: Tianhe Hakka Restaurant
food.tip: Order the Hakka salt-baked chicken (客家盐焗鸡) and braised pork belly — these are the two dishes the region is known for. Simple setting, exceptional food.

food.name: Tulou Hakka Food
food.tip: Communal dining style mirrors how tulou residents eat — dishes arrive as ready rather than all at once. The braised mushroom with tofu is the best vegetarian option on the menu.

food.name: Youyun Coffee
food.tip: A rare proper espresso stop near the tulou villages — most food in the scenic area is tourist-grade. Worth the detour for a decent flat white before or after the tulou visit.

food.name: Coffee Worm
food.tip: The owner lived in Shanghai before opening here — the coffee standard is notably higher than the surrounding area. Try the single-origin pour-over.

food.name: Old Xiamen Teahouse
food.tip: More teahouse than coffee shop — the locally grown Tieguanyin oolong is the real draw. The peanut candy served alongside it is made by the owner's family.

food.name: Musilin Halal Noodle Restaurant
food.tip: One of the few reliable halal options in Xiamen — the hand-pulled beef noodle soup is the signature. Busy at lunch; go before 11:30am or after 1:30pm.

food.name: Houhai Cafe
food.tip: Rooftop terrace with views across Jimei Bay — best at sunset. The matcha latte is the most ordered drink and consistently well made.

food.name: Green Island Coffee
food.tip: A neighbourhood cafe popular with Xiamen University students — prices reflect it. Good wifi, patient staff, not tourist-facing at all.

hotels.name: Sofitel Xiamen On the Waterfront
hotels.tip: Best central location — harbour views, walking distance to Gulangyu ferry and old town. Excellent breakfast.

hotels.name: Gulangyu Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Staying ON Gulangyu is magical — total peace, no cars, waking up to birdsong and distant piano. Book well ahead. Guesthouses are small and fill quickly. Night-time island with no day-trippers is a completely different experience.

hotels.name: Marriott Hotel Xiamen
hotels.tip: Near Nanputuo and Xiamen University. Good value 5-star with sea views from upper floors.

hotels.name: Zengcuoan Boutique Hostels
hotels.tip: The village itself has dozens of charming guesthouses at ¥150–300/night. Sea views from some rooftop rooms. Best for solo travellers and couples who want local atmosphere over hotel amenities.

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Xiamen
hotels.tip: Modern 5-star on Haitang Bay — good for families wanting beach access in Xiamen. Slightly out of the main tourist zone but has excellent facilities.

hotels.name: Angsana Gulangyu Xiamen
hotels.chinese: 厦门鼓浪屿昂撒纳酒店
hotels.tip: The only 5-star hotel actually on Gulangyu Island — wake up to the island after all day tourists have left. Colonial mansion conversion, spa, rooftop sea views. The most memorable accommodation in Xiamen. Book months ahead — limited rooms.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Xiamen
hotels.tip: IHG luxury with excellent Gulangyu ferry access — 15 min to ferry terminal. Very good breakfast, large pool. Praised for English-speaking staff and smooth operations for international guests. A strong choice if Gulangyu guesthouses are full.

hotels.name: Wyndham Grand Xiamen Haicang
hotels.chinese: 厦门海沧温德姆至尊豪廷酒店
hotels.tip: Good value 5-star with Xiamen Bay views — slightly further from old town but Metro Line 6 connection. Praised for spacious rooms, breakfast quality and helpful staff. Good option for families who want space and views at mid-range prices.

hotels.name: Marco Polo Xiamen
hotels.tip: Reliable Marco Polo 4-star with good transport connections to Gulangyu ferry and main attractions. Good breakfast, efficient service. Walk to Huandao cycling road. A solid mid-range choice for first-time Xiamen visitors.

hotels.name: Orange Hotel Xiamen Zhongshan Road
hotels.tip: Best budget option in central Xiamen — right on Zhongshan Road pedestrian street, walk to Gulangyu ferry in 15 minutes. Clean, modern rooms. Praised for helpful staff. Orange Hotel brand known for consistent cleanliness. Very popular with budget Chinese and SEA travellers.

huangshan.name: Huangshan (Yellow Mountain)
huangshan.recommended_base_reason: Tangkou town at the mountain base is most convenient — shuttle buses to all entrances, many hotels and guesthouses
huangshan.seasonal_note: Winter (Nov–Feb) has best snow scenery and rime ice, fewer crowds. Spring (Mar–May) has blooming rhododendrons. Autumn (Sep–Nov) has stunning foliage. Summer is peak crowds.

clusters.huangshan-front: Front mountain — Welcoming Pine, Lotus Peak, West Sea Grand Canyon, Bright Summit (Guangming Ding)
clusters.huangshan-back: Back mountain — Shixin Peak, Dreaming Brush (Mengbi Shenghua), Monkey Gazing at Sea
clusters.tangkou-base: Mountain base town — accommodation, shuttle buses, restaurants
clusters.hongcun-xidi: Two UNESCO Huizhou ancient villages, 30 min from base
clusters.tunxi-oldstreet: Huangshan city old street — tea, antiques, Hui architecture, city base

attractions.name: Huangshan Sunrise (Bright Summit)
attractions.seasonal_note: Best cloud sea conditions: after light rain, with high humidity (>80%) and light wind. Winter mornings most dramatic. Summer 7–8am fine but less cloud.
attractions.tip: Huangshan's most coveted experience — watching dawn light break over a sea of clouds between mountain peaks. Guangming Ding (1,860m) is the best panoramic sunrise point. Stay overnight at Beihai Hotel (book months ahead for peak season) to walk to the viewpoint at 5am. Clear sky is good but a sea of clouds is transcendent — check mountain weather app night before. Arrive at viewpoint 1–1.5hrs before sunrise to get a spot. Dress warmly — summit is 10–15°C colder than base.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Welcoming Guest Pine (Yingke Song)
attractions.tip: China's most famous individual tree — a 1,300-year-old Huangshan pine growing from a granite cliff at a 45° angle, 'welcoming' visitors. The image that defines Huangshan. Near Yuping Pavilion on the front mountain cable car upper station — very easy to reach. Get there early to photograph without crowds. The surrounding area has dozens of equally remarkable named pines: Black Tiger Pine, Dragon Claw Pine, Twin Pines.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: West Sea Grand Canyon
attractions.tip: The most dramatic hiking route on Huangshan — a network of cliff-hugging walkways descending into a cloud-filled canyon between sheer granite walls. The 'Valley Floor Cable Car' (¥80 single) saves 2hrs of steep climbing back up. The Paiyun Pavilion viewpoint over the west sea of clouds is the best wide-angle view in Huangshan. Allow 4–5hrs to do justice. Not suitable for young children or elderly — steep and physically demanding. Morning fog in the canyon is magical.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hongcun Ancient Village
attractions.seasonal_note: Dawn mist at Moon Pond is best Mar–May and Sep–Oct. Autumn maple colours at nearby Tachuang are spectacular.
attractions.tip: Called 'China's most beautiful painting' — 500-year-old Hui village designed in the shape of a buffalo. The crescent-shaped Moon Pond (Yuezhaotang) reflecting whitewashed walls in morning mist is one of China's most iconic images. Arrive BEFORE 7am for empty lanes (ticket office opens 7am). The承志堂 (Chengzhi Hall) private mansion has extraordinary wood carvings — don't skip. Film location for Crouching Tiger Hidden Dragon. 3-day ticket validity. Combine with Xidi on same day (20 min drive).
attractions.description: A 500-year-old Hui minority village designed in the shape of a buffalo — the crescent-shaped Moon Pond reflecting whitewashed Huizhou walls in morning mist is one of China's most iconic images. Arrive before 7am for empty lanes and the extraordinary light that prompted UNESCO designation.

attractions.name: Xidi Ancient Village
attractions.tip: UNESCO twin village to Hongcun — nicknamed 'Huizhou Architecture Museum'. Better preserved Ming-Qing architecture, 300+ ancient buildings. The Hu Wenguan Memorial Archway at the entrance is stunning. Less commercialised than Hongcun — more genuine village life. Maze-like lanes are a joy to wander. The local tofu (Huizhou-style braised tofu) sold from street stalls is excellent. Combine with Hongcun — 20 min apart.
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Huizhou village — 300 well-preserved Ming and Qing dynasty residences in a village that has changed little in 500 years. The carved wood screens and ink wash painting-worthy streetscapes make this the most photographically rewarding village in eastern China.

attractions.name: Tunxi Old Street (Huangshan City)
attractions.tip: 1.5km of authentic Song-dynasty style street lined with antique shops, tea merchants, brush-and-ink sellers and Hui snack stalls. The real stuff — Maojian tea, Huizhou inksticks, old woodblock prints. The Liyue Old Street section has live music in the evenings. Huangshan airport/train station is in Tunxi — make this your first or last stop.
attractions.description: The best-preserved Huizhou commercial street in China — 1.5km of Song dynasty shophouses selling Huangshan specialties including Mao Feng tea, She ink sticks and Huizhou inkstone. The morning light on the whitewashed walls is extraordinary and the street empties completely by 8am on weekdays.

attractions.name: Shixin Peak & Back Mountain Loop
attractions.tip: The back mountain approach via Yungu Cable Car gives access to Shixin Peak — where the famous 'Black Tiger Pine' and 'Connected Love Pines' grow. Less crowded than the front mountain route. The circular loop from cable car upper station through Shixin Peak to North Sea viewpoints takes 4hrs of hiking. Photographers often prefer back mountain for less-visited viewpoints. Take Yungu cable car up, Yuping cable car down for the classic front-back traverse.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Huangshan (Yellow Mountain)
attractions.tip: Stay overnight on the mountain to catch sunrise — the light at 5:30am turns the sea of clouds gold. Book mountaintop guesthouses 3–4 months ahead for peak season.
attractions.description: China's most mythologised mountain — twisted pine trees clinging to granite peaks wreathed in cloud, the landscape that inspired a thousand ink paintings and the floating mountains of Avatar. The summit experience changes completely hour by hour as cloud formations roll through the valleys below.

attractions.name: Tiandu Peak
attractions.tip: The climb takes 90 minutes up, 60 down. Go early — the trail is single-file in places and becomes a human traffic jam by 10am. Bring gloves; the chains are cold even in summer.
attractions.description: One of Huangshan's three main peaks and the most challenging to climb — a near-vertical granite staircase with chain handrails cut directly into the rock face, rewarding summit-seekers with views that make the burning thighs worthwhile. Closed in winter due to ice.

attractions.name: Monkey Watching the Sea Viewpoint
attractions.tip: The best photography angle is from the path below, not from standing beside it. Cloud conditions are everything — check the Huangshan weather forecast the night before your hike.
attractions.description: One of Huangshan's most celebrated rock formations — a naturally sculpted granite outcrop on the northern slope bearing an uncanny resemblance to a monkey gazing out over the cloud sea below. It is the single most photographed viewpoint on the mountain and the image on every Huangshan postcard.

food.name: Stinky Mandarin Fish (Chou Guiyu)
food.tip: Huangshan's most famous dish — fish preserved for 7 days until slightly fermented, then braised in spicy Hui sauce. The smell is pungent (like strong cheese) but the taste is extraordinary — rich, umami, melt-in-mouth. Must try at least once. Best at Hongcun's 得月楼 (Deyue Restaurant) or Tangkou base restaurants.

food.name: Huangshan Barbecue Sesame Cakes
food.tip: Flaky, sesame-coated pastry filled with pork and preserved vegetable. Baked in a tandoor-style oven on the street. Hot from the oven is magical — ¥3 each. Perfect hiking snack. Buy extras — they keep well. Available everywhere in the region.

food.name: Mao Tofu (Huizhou Fermented Tofu)
food.tip: Tofu covered in white mould (like brie), pan-fried with chilli sauce. A Huizhou speciality sold from village street stalls — ¥2–3 per piece. Looks alarming, tastes amazing. The fermentation gives it a creamy, rich flavour. Most interesting food experience in the region.

food.name: Mountain Top Provisions
food.tip: Food on the mountain summit costs 3-5x what it costs at the base. Buy energy bars, instant noodles, water and snacks at Tangkou before going up. The mountain hotels serve basic meals (¥60–120/dish) — fine for dinner if staying overnight but overpriced for day trippers.

food.name: Tangkou Base Town Restaurants
food.must_order: Stinky Mandarin Fish (臭鳜鱼), Huangshan sesame cakes
food.tip: Tangkou has many good Huizhou restaurants at reasonable prices. The stinky mandarin fish is the must-try signature dish — fermented for 7 days then braised in spicy Hui sauce. Intimidating smell, extraordinary taste. The sesame sesame cakes (¥3–5 each) from street stalls are the best hiking snack. Stock up before going up the mountain.

food.name: Hongcun Moon Pond Cafes
food.near: Inside Hongcun village, next to Moon Pond
food.must_order: Coffee reflected in Moon Pond, Huizhou osmanthus cake
food.tip: Several cafes around the Moon Pond have second-floor terraces looking down at the reflected Hui village architecture. The early morning (6:30–8am) Moon Pond reflection with mist is one of China's iconic landscape photos. Sit upstairs with coffee and wait for the light to be perfect. Photography-focused visitors come specifically for this view.

food.name: Xidi Village Courtyard Restaurants
food.must_order: Mao tofu (毛豆腐), Huizhou braised tofu, wild mountain vegetables
food.tip: Family-run restaurants inside Xidi village serve genuine Huizhou home cooking. Mao tofu (fermented tofu covered in white mould, pan-fried with chilli) is sold from street stalls for ¥2–3 each. The braised tofu with dried mushrooms is a Huizhou staple. Cheaper and more authentic than Hongcun's tourist restaurants.

food.name: Tunxi Old Street Tea Merchants
food.must_order: Maojian green tea, Keemun black tea
food.tip: The tea merchants on Tunxi Old Street sell Huangshan's legendary teas direct: Huangshan Maojian (yellow mountain fine tip) and Keemun black tea (the world's most famous black tea, used in Earl Grey). Buy 50–100g for tasting at the source — a fraction of city prices. Shop owners brew samples — take your time and taste multiple grades.

food.name: Summit Mountain Instant Noodles
food.near: Any mountain hut on Huangshan summit
food.must_order: Instant noodles, boiled eggs, summit tea
food.tip: At 1,800m in sub-zero temperatures, a ¥25 bowl of instant noodles from a mountain hut is one of China's most satisfying food experiences. The mountain hut restaurants serve basic food at 3–5x base prices but nothing has ever tasted better than hot noodles after 4 hours of mountain hiking. Bring your own snacks but budget for one summit meal.

food.name: Yuan'an Coffee
food.tip: Opens at 6am for early hikers — the only decent coffee option before catching the first cable car up. The condensed milk coffee is worth trying once.

food.name: Lanren (Lazy Man) Cafe
food.tip: Popular with hikers returning from the mountain — the iced coffee and hot spring egg combo is the unofficial post-hike recovery meal. Relaxed atmosphere, no time pressure.

food.name: Linjiang Yilou Huizhou Restaurant
food.tip: Order the Huizhou signature dishes — hairy tofu (毛豆腐), stinky mandarin fish (臭鳜鱼), and braised pork with dried vegetables (梅干菜扣肉). The stinky fish smells alarming but tastes exceptional.

food.name: Yuezhao Courtyard Cafe
food.tip: A converted Huizhou courtyard house — sit in the inner courtyard under the open roof for the classic sky-well (天井) architectural experience alongside decent pour-over coffee.

food.name: Old Street Cafe Bar
food.tip: The rooftop terrace has the best views over the Hongcun roofscape — arrive at opening before other tourists claim the seats. The osmanthus latte is the seasonal specialty.

food.name: Bantang Coffee
food.tip: Hidden in a side lane — quieter than cafes on the main street. The owner hand-roasts the beans; worth asking to see the roaster in the back room.

food.name: Huangshan Local Cuisine
food.tip: A no-frills local restaurant serving Huizhou home cooking — the stone pot chicken (石锅鸡) and bamboo shoot stir-fry are the must-orders. Cash only, arrives fast.

food.name: Haoshi Cafe
food.tip: A tiny cafe in a restored Ming dynasty building — the low wooden beams and stone floor are the real attraction alongside reasonable coffee. Very local crowd, no tourist markup.

food.name: Mai Coffee
food.tip: Americano made with locally sourced spring water — makes a genuine difference to the taste. One of the few cafes on Old Street with outdoor seating.

hotels.name: Beihai Hotel (Summit)
hotels.tip: THE summit hotel for sunrise. 10-min walk to Guangming Ding viewpoint. Book 3+ months ahead for peak season — impossible to get last-minute. Basic rooms but the location is everything. Worth the premium for the sunrise experience.

hotels.name: Shilin Hotel (Summit)
hotels.tip: Alternative summit option to Beihai — west side of mountain, near Paiyun Pavilion. Good for west sea cloud views. Slightly cheaper than Beihai.

hotels.name: Tangkou Boutique Inns
hotels.tip: Base town guesthouses — ¥150–300/night. Good starting point if doing day hike only. 10-min walk to shuttle buses. Many include free shuttle to the cable car. The town has good restaurants and supermarkets for supplies.

hotels.name: Hongcun Moon Pond Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Stay inside Hongcun village itself — waking up before tourists arrive to photograph the empty Moon Pond is priceless. Old mansions converted to boutique guesthouses. Book 1+ month ahead. Rooms often have courtyard access.

hotels.name: Huangshan Y悦榕庄 (Banyan Tree Huangshan)
hotels.tip: Luxury Banyan Tree property near Hongcun — stunning Huizhou-inspired villas with mountain views, spa and infinity pool. The rooftop sundowner watching the mountains fade at dusk is extraordinary. Best luxury base in the region.

hotels.name: InterContinental Huangshan Resort
hotels.tip: IHG luxury at Huangshan's base — shuttle to cable car station included, excellent spa to recover after mountain hiking, beautiful pool. Praised for breakfast quality and English-speaking staff. The most comfortable base for exploring the mountain and villages.

hotels.name: Huangshan Yupinglou Hotel (Summit)
hotels.tip: 'Luxury' here means paying a premium to sleep ON the mountain summit — right next to Welcoming Guest Pine and perfect sunrise positioning. Rooms are basic 3-star but the location is everything. Book 3 months ahead in peak season. This is where sunrise photography happens.

hotels.name: Manxin Hotel Hongcun
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | best design hotel in Hongcun
hotels.tip: The finest designed hotel inside Hongcun village — Hui-style architecture with modern boutique interiors, courtyard and mountain views. Wake up before tourists and photograph empty Moon Pond. Praised for exceptional service and atmosphere. One of Anhui's best boutique hotels.

hotels.name: Tunxi Old Street Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Multiple boutique guesthouses in restored Hui buildings right on Tunxi Old Street — ¥200-400/night. Wake up on the most authentic historic street in the region. Walk to tea shops and antique markets immediately. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ ratings on Trip.com.

hotels.name: Tangkou Town Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses at the mountain base — ¥150-280/night. Many include free shuttle to cable car. Communal atmosphere, meet other hikers. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ Trip.com reviews. Perfect for budget travellers doing a day hike and not staying on the summit.

nanjing.name: Nanjing
nanjing.recommended_base_reason: Daxinggong/New Street area (大行宫/新街口) is central — 15 min to all major sights, excellent metro connections, restaurants and accommodation density
nanjing.seasonal_note: Spring (Mar–Apr) for cherry blossoms at Jiming Temple and Xuanwu Lake. Autumn (Oct–Nov) for Qixia Mountain maple leaves. Summer is very hot and humid (Nanjing is China's 'furnace city').
highlights: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum stone animal road

clusters.zhongshan-scenic: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, Meilinggong (Meiling Palace), Music Terrace — all in Zhongshan National Park
clusters.qinhuai-district: Confucius Temple, Qinhuai River, Old Menbdong Street, Zhanyuan Garden, Jiangnan Gongyuan
clusters.daxinggong: Presidential Palace, Nanjing Museum, 1912 commercial district, Jiming Temple, Xuanwu Lake
clusters.city-north: Xuanwu Lake Park, Nanjing Massacre Memorial, Yunzhen Museum

attractions.name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum (Zhongshan Ling)
attractions.tip: The mausoleum of Sun Yat-sen (founder of modern China) — 392 steps leading to the blue-roofed memorial hall. Arrive before 8am to walk the 'Jade Tunnel' (Cuigu) boulevard in peaceful morning light without crowds. Free but MUST book via official WeChat mini-program. The blue ceramic tile roof that changes colour with cloud and light is extraordinary. Combine with Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum (15 min walk) on same morning. Not suitable for elderly with knee problems — 392 steps are steep.
attractions.description: The mausoleum of modern China's founding father — 392 steep steps lead through forested hills to a blue-roofed memorial hall where Sun Yat-sen lies in state. The view looking back down from the summit over the pine-covered hills toward Nanjing is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum & Stone Animals Road
attractions.seasonal_note: The 'Best 600 Metres' — Stone Animal Road is lined with ancient ginkgo and maple trees that turn gold in October. Enter before 6:30am for free access.
attractions.tip: The 'Sacred Way' — 615m lined with pairs of lions, camels, elephants, unicorns and humans in perfect symmetry, dating from 1398. Enter before 6:30am for FREE (also avoids crowds). The stone animals are extraordinary — among the most impressive Ming-era sculptures in China. Kids love 'spotting all the animals'. The mausoleum itself (¥70 for full access) is impressive but the stone road is the highlight. Combine with Zhongshan Ling in same morning visit.
attractions.description: The mausoleum of the Hongwu Emperor who founded the Ming dynasty — the Sacred Way lined with stone animals and officials leading to the mausoleum is one of China's most serene historical walks, through ancient forest that has been sacred for 600 years.
attractions.photo_query: ming xiaoling mausoleum nanjing sacred way

attractions.name: Confucius Temple & Qinhuai River Night Cruise
attractions.chinese: 夫子庙 & 秦淮河夜游
attractions.tip: GO AT NIGHT — daytime is just a shopping street. After 7:30pm the entire riverbank lights up — lanterns reflected in the water, decorated wooden boats (画舫) drifting past, illuminated bridges. Take the West Line river cruise (¥40) past Peach Leaf Crossing and Wu Yi Lane while a 评弹 (Suzhou opera) singer performs on the boat. Kids love the lanterns and boats. Avoid the tourist restaurants inside — eat at Old Menbdong (老门东) 1.3km away, then walk back to the river. 39th Lantern Festival (Jan–Feb) here is extraordinary.
attractions.description: The ancient examination complex where China's imperial scholars were tested sits beside a canal whose illuminated banks at night, lined with traditional wooden boats and red lanterns, create the most atmospheric evening scene in the Yangtze Delta.
attractions.photo_query: qinhuai river nanjing confucius night lantern
attractions.photo_attribution: Nanjing Confucius Temple-The Qinhuai River Scenic Area

attractions.name: Presidential Palace
attractions.tip: China's largest surviving Republican-era (民国) architectural complex — where Chiang Kai-shek governed and where the Communist takeover of Nanjing happened in 1949. Beautiful Chinese garden alongside Western-influenced official buildings.
